title: 3D Shape Induction from 2D Views of Multiple Objects
searchable_text:
3D Shape Induction from 2D Views of Multiple Objects Inducing distributions over 3D structures from 2D views of multiple objects captured from unknown viewpoints. Train a deep 3D-shape generator with a projection module so generated 3D structures, when projected from latent viewpoints, match distributions of unlabeled 2D views. Adds a projection module to adversarial 3D shape generation, removing the need for 3D data, viewpoint information, or annotations during learning. TITLE: 3D Shape Induction from 2D Views of Multiple Objects

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper we investigate the problem of inducing a distribution over three-dimensional structures given two-dimensional views of multiple objects taken from unknown viewpoints. Our approach called "projective generative adversarial networks" (PrGANs) trains a deep generative model of 3D shapes whose projections match the distributions of the input 2D views. The addition of a projection module allows us to infer the underlying 3D shape distribution without using any 3D, viewpoint

title: (CAD)$^2$RL: Real Single-Image Flight without a Single Real Image
searchable_text:
(CAD)$^2$RL: Real Single-Image Flight without a Single Real Image Vision-based collision-free indoor quadrotor flight using only a single monocular RGB image, trained without any real-world training images. Deep convolutional policy trained entirely on randomized simulated CAD-rendered RGB images via Monte Carlo policy evaluation to map monocular images directly to velocity commands for collision avoidance. Replaced real-image training or explicit geometry/planning with CAD-only simulated training and randomized rendering for direct sim-to-real monocular flight control. TITLE: (CAD)$^2$RL: Real Single-Image Flight without a Single Real Image

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep reinforcement learning has emerged as a promising and powerful technique for automatically acquiring control policies that can process raw sensory inputs, such as images, and perform complex behaviors. However, extending deep RL to real-world robotic tasks has proven challenging, particularly in safety-critical domains such as autonomous flight, where a trial-and-error learning process is often impractical. In this paper, we explore the following question: can we t

title: {Euclidean, metric, and Wasserstein} gradient flows: an overview
searchable_text:
{Euclidean, metric, and Wasserstein} gradient flows: an overview Theory of gradient flows, especially PDEs interpretable as Wasserstein gradient flows on probability measures. Builds from Euclidean gradient flows to metric-space theory, then Wasserstein optimal transport, geodesic convexity, and the Jordan–Kinderlehrer–Otto scheme for convergence. Extends Euclidean gradient-flow exposition through metric-space generalization into Wasserstein theory, adding a recent metric-measure heat-flow development. TITLE: {Euclidean, metric, and Wasserstein} gradient flows: an overview

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This is an expository paper on the theory of gradient flows, and in particular of those PDEs which can be interpreted as gradient flows for the Wasserstein metric on the space of probability measures (a distance induced by optimal transport). The starting point is the Euclidean theory, and then its generalization to metric spaces, according to the work of Ambrosio, Gigli and Savaré. Then comes an independent exposition of the Wasserstein theory, with a short introduction

title: A benchmark environment motivated by industrial control problems
searchable_text:
A benchmark environment motivated by industrial control problems Reinforcement-learning benchmarks for industrial real-world control problems where researchers lack access to industrial data and applications. Use industry experience to design a publicly available RL industrial benchmark, with Java/Python code and an OpenAI Gym wrapper, whose dynamics and experimental settings mimic real industrial control properties. Compared with artificial software benchmarks, IB adds industry-motivated dynamics, prototypic settings, public Java/Python implementation, and an OpenAI Gym wrapper. TITLE: A benchmark environment motivated by industrial control problems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In the research area of reinforcement learning (RL), frequently novel and promising methods are developed and introduced to the RL community. However, although many researchers are keen to apply their methods on real-world problems, implementing such methods in real industry environments often is a frustrating and tedious process. Generally, academic research groups have only limited access to real industrial data and applications. For this reason, new methods are usuall

title: A bit of progress in language modeling
searchable_text:
A bit of progress in language modeling Statistical language modeling, targeting perplexity and word error reduction relative to trigram baselines. Combine caching, higher-order n-grams, skipping, interpolated Kneser?Ney smoothing, clustering, and sentence mixture model explorations for stronger language modeling. Moves beyond a Katz-smoothed trigram baseline by evaluating variations, limits, and a full combination of multiple language modeling improvements. TITLE: A bit of progress in language modeling

YEAR: 2001

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In the past several years, a number of different language modeling improvements over simple trigram models have been found, including caching, higher-order n -grams, skipping, interpolated Kneser?Ney smoothing, and clustering. We present explorations of variations on, or of the limits of, each of these techniques, including showing that sentence mixture models may have more potential. While all of these techniques have been studied separately, they have rarely been studied in combination. We comp

title: A Brief Survey of Deep Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
A Brief Survey of Deep Reinforcement Learning Deep reinforcement learning for autonomous visual understanding, video-game play from pixels, and robot control from camera inputs. Survey and synthesis of value-based and policy-based deep reinforcement learning methods, including DQN, TRPO, and asynchronous advantage actor critic. Compared with prior reinforcement learning, deep learning enables scaling to high-dimensional visual inputs such as raw pixels and camera images. TITLE: A Brief Survey of Deep Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is poised to revolutionize the field of artificial intelligence (AI) and represents a step toward building autonomous systems with a higherlevel understanding of the visual world. Currently, deep learning is enabling reinforcement learning (RL) to scale to problems that were previously intractable, such as learning to play video games directly from pixels. DRL algorithms are also applied to robotics, allowing control policies for robots to be learned direc

title: A Bright Millisecond Radio Burst of Extragalactic Origin
searchable_text:
A Bright Millisecond Radio Burst of Extragalactic Origin Detection and characterization of rare millisecond radio transients in the radio sky. Analyze archival pulsar survey data for dispersed, millisecond-duration impulsive radio bursts. Shifted millisecond radio bursts from pulsar-survey artifacts or local associations toward extragalactic singular events usable as cosmological probes. TITLE: A Bright Millisecond Radio Burst of Extragalactic Origin

YEAR: 2007

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Pulsar surveys offer a rare opportunity to monitor the radio sky for impulsive burst-like events with millisecond durations. We analyzed archival survey data and found a 30-jansky dispersed burst, less than 5 milliseconds in duration, located 3° from the Small Magellanic Cloud. The burst properties argue against a physical association with our Galaxy or the Small Magellanic Cloud. Current models for the free electron content in the universe imply that the burst is less than 1 gi

title: A Broad-Coverage Challenge Corpus for Sentence Understanding through Inference
searchable_text:
A Broad-Coverage Challenge Corpus for Sentence Understanding through Inference Development and evaluation of machine learning models for sentence understanding via natural language inference / recognizing textual entailment. Constructs a 433k-example natural language inference corpus spanning ten distinct genres of written and spoken English, with an explicit cross-genre domain adaptation setting. Compared with Stanford NLI and other available resources, MultiNLI increases genre coverage, task difficulty, and supports cross-genre domain adaptation evaluation. TITLE: A Broad-Coverage Challenge Corpus for Sentence Understanding through Inference

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper introduces the Multi-Genre Natural Language Inference (MultiNLI) corpus, a dataset designed for use in the development and evaluation of machine learning models for sentence understanding. At 433k examples, this resource is one of the largest corpora available for natural language inference (a.k.a. recognizing textual entailment), improving upon available resources in both its coverage and difficulty. MultiNLI accomplishes this by offering data f

title: A Compositional Object-Based Approach to Learning Physical Dynamics
searchable_text:
A Compositional Object-Based Approach to Learning Physical Dynamics Learning simulators of intuitive physics for variable object counts, different scene configurations, and simple two-dimensional rigid body worlds. Factorize scenes into composable object representations and use a neural architecture that decomposes dynamics into pairwise interactions. Compared with less structured architectures, it adds object-based compositional representations and pairwise interaction factorization, analogous to a symbolic physics engine but trainable by gradient descent. TITLE: A Compositional Object-Based Approach to Learning Physical Dynamics

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present the Neural Physics Engine (NPE), a framework for learning simulators of intuitive physics that naturally generalize across variable object count and different scene configurations. We propose a factorization of a physical scene into composable object-based representations and a neural network architecture whose compositional structure factorizes object dynamics into pairwise interactions. Like a symbolic physics engine, the NPE is endowed with generic notio

title: A Computational Study of Stimulus Driven Epileptic Seizure Abatement
searchable_text:
A Computational Study of Stimulus Driven Epileptic Seizure Abatement Abatement of spike-wave epileptic seizures using active single-pulse brain stimulation. Neural population model reconstructing the background-activity basin of attraction as a four-dimensional geometric object to analyze single-pulse stimulus amplitude, phase, and state-space direction in deterministic and noise-induced spike-wave seizures. Moves beyond prior suggestions that single pulses can terminate SW seizures by providing a mechanistic basin-of-attraction analysis and proposing adaptive, patient-specific stimulation. TITLE: A Computational Study of Stimulus Driven Epileptic Seizure Abatement

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Active brain stimulation to abate epileptic seizures has shown mixed success. In spike-wave (SW) seizures, where the seizure and background state were proposed to coexist, single-pulse stimulations have been suggested to be able to terminate the seizure prematurely. However, several factors can impact success in such a bistable setting. The factors contributing to this have not been fully investigated on a theoretical and mechanistic basis. Our aim is to elucidate mechanisms t

title: A conformal prediction approach to explore functional data
searchable_text:
A conformal prediction approach to explore functional data Exploration of functional data through prediction bands and clustering trees to detect outliers and clusters. Inductive conformal prediction with novel conformity scores computes simultaneous prediction bands and clustering trees for functional data. Compared with ordinary conformal prediction, the method uses inductive conformal prediction plus novel conformity scores to simplify computation. TITLE: A conformal prediction approach to explore functional data

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper applies conformal prediction techniques to compute simultaneous prediction bands and clustering trees for functional data. These tools can be used to detect outliers and clusters. Both our prediction bands and clustering trees provide prediction sets for the underlying stochastic process with a guaranteed finite sample behavior, under no distributional assumptions. The prediction sets are also informative in that they correspond to the high density region of the und

title: A contextual-bandit approach to personalized news article recommendation
searchable_text:
A contextual-bandit approach to personalized news article recommendation Personalized recommendation of news articles in web services with changing content pools and large-scale traffic. Contextual bandit learning selects news articles using user and article context, then adapts the selection policy from click feedback to maximize clicks. Compared with standard context-free bandits, the method incorporates contextual information about users and articles into article selection. TITLE: A contextual-bandit approach to personalized news article recommendation

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Personalized web services strive to adapt their services (advertisements, news articles, etc.) to individual users by making use of both content and user information. Despite a few recent advances, this problem remains challenging for at least two reasons. First, web service is featured with dynamically changing pools of content, rendering traditional collaborative filtering methods inapplicable. Second, the scale of most web services of practical interest calls

title: A Corpus and Cloze Evaluation for Deeper Understanding of Commonsense Stories
searchable_text:
A Corpus and Cloze Evaluation for Deeper Understanding of Commonsense Stories Evaluating story understanding and script learning for causal, temporal, commonsense relationships between everyday events. Story Cloze Test: choose the correct ending to a four-sentence story, enabled by ROCStories, a ~50k corpus of five-sentence commonsense stories. Introduces a new Story Cloze evaluation framework plus ROCStories, replacing the prior lack of a proper evaluation framework. TITLE: A Corpus and Cloze Evaluation for Deeper Understanding of Commonsense Stories

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Representation and learning of commonsense knowledge is one of the foundational problems in the quest to enable deep language understanding. This issue is particularly challenging for understanding casual and correlational relationships between events. While this topic has received a lot of interest in the NLP community, research has been hindered by the lack of a proper evaluation framework. This paper attempts to address this problem with a new framework f

title: A Diagram is Worth a Dozen Images
searchable_text:
A Diagram is Worth a Dozen Images Diagram interpretation: identifying diagram structure, constituent semantics, relationships, and answering questions about diagrams. Diagram Parse Graphs model diagram structure, with an LSTM-based syntactic parser and a DPG-based attention model for diagram question answering. Moves beyond natural-image understanding by introducing DPGs, syntactic parsing of diagrams, DPG-based attention QA, and a new annotated diagram QA dataset. TITLE: A Diagram is Worth a Dozen Images

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Diagrams are common tools for representing complex concepts, relationships and events, often when it would be difficult to portray the same information with natural images. Understanding natural images has been extensively studied in computer vision, while diagram understanding has received little attention. In this paper, we study the problem of diagram interpretation, the challenging task of identifying the structure of a diagram and the semantics of its constituents and their relationships. We intr

title: A Diversity-Promoting Objective Function for Neural Conversation Models
searchable_text:
A Diversity-Promoting Objective Function for Neural Conversation Models Generating conversational responses with sequence-to-sequence neural network models. Use Maximum Mutual Information (MMI) as the objective function in neural conversation response generation models. Replaced likelihood of response given message with Maximum Mutual Information as the training/selection objective. TITLE: A Diversity-Promoting Objective Function for Neural Conversation Models

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Sequence-to-sequence neural network models for generation of conversational responses tend to generate safe, commonplace responses (e.g., "I don't know") regardless of the input. We suggest that the traditional objective function, i.e., the likelihood of output (response) given input (message) is unsuited to response generation tasks. Instead we propose using Maximum Mutual Information (MMI) as the objective function in neural models. Experimental results demonstr

title: A DYNAMICAL ANALYSIS OF THE KEPLER-80 SYSTEM OF FIVE TRANSITING PLANETS
searchable_text:
A DYNAMICAL ANALYSIS OF THE KEPLER-80 SYSTEM OF FIVE TRANSITING PLANETS Dynamical characterization of Kepler-80 (KOI-500), a tightly spaced inner system of five transiting planets with 1.0–9.5 day periods. Transit-time measurements and TTV dynamical analysis, with constrained eccentricities and dissipative formation modeling, to infer masses, compositions, and resonant architecture. Moves beyond identifying Kepler-80 as a five-planet STIP by adding transit-time measurements, TTV dynamical analysis, resonance confirmation, and a dissipative formation model. TITLE: A DYNAMICAL ANALYSIS OF THE KEPLER-80 SYSTEM OF FIVE TRANSITING PLANETS

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Kepler has discovered hundreds of systems with multiple transiting exoplanets which hold tremendous potential both individually and collectively for understanding the formation and evolution of planetary systems. Many of these systems consist of multiple small planets with periods less than ∼50 days known as Systems with Tightly spaced Inner Planets, or STIPs. One especially intriguing STIP, Kepler-80 (KOI-500), contains five transiting planets: f, d, e, b, and c

title: A Fast Integrated Planning and Control Framework for Autonomous Driving via Imitation Learning
searchable_text:
A Fast Integrated Planning and Control Framework for Autonomous Driving via Imitation Learning Real-time planning and control for autonomous highway driving under safety and efficiency requirements. Hierarchical safe imitation: a neural policy imitates long-term expert driving, while a short-term model-based controller tracks and refines trajectories with collision avoidance. Changed from direct long-horizon model-based optimization to an imitation-learned long-term policy plus short-term optimal execution layer. TITLE: A Fast Integrated Planning and Control Framework for Autonomous Driving via Imitation Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Safety and efficiency are two key elements for planning and control in autonomous driving. Theoretically, model-based optimization methods, such as Model Predictive Control (MPC), can provide such optimal driving policies. Their computational complexity, however, grows exponentially with horizon length and number of surrounding vehicles. This makes them impractical for real-time implementation, particularly when nonlinear models are consider

title: A fast quantum mechanical algorithm for database search
searchable_text:
A fast quantum mechanical algorithm for database search Database search. A quantum mechanical algorithmic approach for faster search. Extends prior quantum-computing advantages beyond earlier specialized problems toward database search. TITLE: A fast quantum mechanical algorithm for database search

YEAR: 1996

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
were proposed in the early 1980’s [Benioff80] and shown to be at least as powerful as classical computers an important but not surprising result, since classical computers, at the deepest level, ultimately follow the laws of quantum mechanics. The description of quantum mechanical computers was formalized in the late 80’s and early 90’s [Deutsch85][BB92] [BV93] [Yao93] and they were shown to be more powerful than classical computers on various specialized problems. In early 1994,

title: A FIRST COMPARISON OF KEPLER PLANET CANDIDATES IN SINGLE AND MULTIPLE SYSTEMS
searchable_text:
A FIRST COMPARISON OF KEPLER PLANET CANDIDATES IN SINGLE AND MULTIPLE SYSTEMS Early Kepler census of systems hosting one versus multiple candidate transiting planets. Compare characteristics of Kepler transiting-planet candidates in single-candidate systems versus multiple-candidate systems using the first four months of data. Shifted to the first explicit comparison of Kepler planet candidates in single and multiple systems, quantifying 170 multiple systems with 408 candidates. TITLE: A FIRST COMPARISON OF KEPLER PLANET CANDIDATES IN SINGLE AND MULTIPLE SYSTEMS

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this Letter, we present an overview of the rich population of systems with multiple candidate transiting planets found in the first four months of Kepler data. The census of multiples includes 115 targets that show two candidate planets, 45 with three, eight with four, and one each with five and six, for a total of 170 systems with 408 candidates. When compared to the 827 systems with only one candidate, the multiples account for 17% of the total number o

title: A fixed-point approach to barycenters in Wasserstein space
searchable_text:
A fixed-point approach to barycenters in Wasserstein space Computing L2-Wasserstein barycenters or Fréchet means of finite absolutely continuous probability measures, especially location-scatter families including Gaussian distributions. Fixed-point operator on absolutely continuous probability measures, built from optimal transport maps to target distributions, with an iterative procedure approximating its fixed-point barycenter. Recasts Wasserstein barycenter computation as finding a fixed point of a newly introduced operator, enabling a consistent iterative approximation. TITLE: A fixed-point approach to barycenters in Wasserstein space

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Let $\mathcal{P}_{2,ac}$ be the set of Borel probabilities on $\mathbb{R}^d$ with finite second moment and absolutely continuous with respect to Lebesgue measure. We consider the problem of finding the barycenter (or Fr\'echet mean) of a finite set of probabilities $\nu_1,\ldots,\nu_k \in \mathcal{P}_{2,ac}$ with respect to the $L_2-$Wasserstein metric. For this task we introduce an operator on $\mathcal{P}_{2,ac}$ related to the optimal transport maps pushing forward any $\mu

title: A Framework for Wasserstein-1-Type Metrics
searchable_text:
A Framework for Wasserstein-1-Type Metrics Measuring discrepancies between nonnegative measures of different mass. A unifying framework generalizes Wasserstein-1 into a discrepancy measure for nonnegative measures with unequal mass while retaining convexity and computational efficiency. It extends Wasserstein-1 from equal-mass optimal transport to unequal-mass nonnegative measures within a unifying framework. TITLE: A Framework for Wasserstein-1-Type Metrics

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a unifying framework for generalising the Wasserstein-1 metric to a discrepancy measure between nonnegative measures of different mass. This generalization inherits the convexity and computational efficiency from the Wasserstein-1 metric, and it includes several previous approaches from the literature as special cases. For various specific instances of the generalized Wasserstein-1 metric we furthermore demonstrate their usefulness in applications by numerical experiments.

title: A General Safety Framework for Learning-Based Control in Uncertain Robotic Systems
searchable_text:
A General Safety Framework for Learning-Based Control in Uncertain Robotic Systems Safe learning-based control for uncertain robotic systems operating in the physical world, especially safety-critical robots during online learning. Hamilton–Jacobi reachability safety filter using approximate dynamics, Bayesian confidence refinement, and least-restrictive intervention around arbitrary learning-based controllers. Adds a Bayesian mechanism that updates reachability-based safety guarantees from new evidence, reducing conservativeness or strengthening intervention confidence during learning. TITLE: A General Safety Framework for Learning-Based Control in Uncertain Robotic Systems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The proven efficacy of learning-based control schemes strongly motivates their application to robotic systems operating in the physical world. However, guaranteeing correct operation during the learning process is currently an unresolved issue, which is of vital importance in safety-critical systems. We propose a general safety framework based on Hamilton–Jacobi reachability methods that can work in conjunction with an arbitrary learning algorithm. The

title: A genetic programming approach to designing convolutional neural network architectures
searchable_text:
A genetic programming approach to designing convolutional neural network architectures Automatic construction of convolutional neural network architectures for CIFAR-10 image classification. Cartesian genetic programming encodes CNN structure and connectivity, using convolutional blocks and tensor concatenation as node functions, optimized for validation accuracy. Uses highly functional CGP node modules such as convolutional blocks and tensor concatenation to evolve CNN architectures. TITLE: A genetic programming approach to designing convolutional neural network architectures

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The convolutional neural network (CNN), which is one of the deep learning models, has seen much success in a variety of computer vision tasks. However, designing CNN architectures still requires expert knowledge and a lot of trial and error. In this paper, we attempt to automatically construct CNN architectures for an image classification task based on Cartesian genetic programming (CGP). In our method, we adopt highly functional modules, such as co

title: A global reference for human genetic variation
searchable_text:
A global reference for human genetic variation Comprehensive reference description of common human genetic variation across multiple global populations. Whole-genome, exome, and microarray data were combined to reconstruct and phase genomes across diverse populations. The project moved from an effort to a completed global reference: 2,504 individuals from 26 populations with variants phased onto high-quality haplotypes. TITLE: A global reference for human genetic variation

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The 1000 Genomes Project set out to provide a comprehensive description of common human genetic variation by applying whole-genome sequencing to a diverse set of individuals from multiple populations. Here we report completion of the project, having reconstructed the genomes of 2,504 individuals from 26 populations using a combination of low-coverage whole-genome sequencing, deep exome sequencing, and dense microarray genotyping. We characterized a broad spectrum of genetic variation, in

title: A JKO Splitting Scheme for Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao Gradient Flows
searchable_text:
A JKO Splitting Scheme for Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao Gradient Flows Gradient flows for positive Radon measures with varying masses under the Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao metric, especially reaction-advection-diffusion and internal energy cases. A splitting JKO scheme alternating quadratic Wasserstein/Monge-Kantorovich and Hellinger/Fisher-Rao time steps, using the metric’s inf-convolution structure to prove convergence. Instead of a single JKO step for Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao flows, it successively applies Wasserstein/Monge-Kantorovich and Hellinger/Fisher-Rao steps. TITLE: A JKO Splitting Scheme for Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao Gradient Flows

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this article we set up a splitting variant of the JKO scheme in order to handle gradient flows with respect to the Kantorovich-Fisher-Rao metric, recently introduced and defined on the space of positive Radon measure with varying masses. We perform successively a time step for the quadratic Wasserstein/Monge-Kantorovich distance, and then for the Hellinger/Fisher-Rao distance. Exploiting some inf-convolution structure of the metric we show convergence of the whole pro

title: A Kernel Method for the Two-Sample-Problem
searchable_text:
A Kernel Method for the Two-Sample-Problem Determining whether two samples are drawn from different distributions, including database attribute matching and comparing distributions over graphs. Use the distance between sample means after mapping observations into a reproducing kernel Hilbert space, with tests based on a large deviation bound or asymptotic distribution. Introduces RKHS mean-distance two-sample tests, including applicability to graph distribution comparison where no alternative tests currently exist. TITLE: A Kernel Method for the Two-Sample-Problem

YEAR: 2006

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose two statistical tests to determine if two samples are from different distributions. Our test statistic is in both cases the distance between the means of the two samples mapped into a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS). The first test is based on a large deviation bound for the test statistic, while the second is based on the asymptotic distribution of this statistic. The test statistic can be computed in O(m2) time. We apply our approach to a variety of problems, including at

title: A Knowledge-Grounded Neural Conversation Model
searchable_text:
A Knowledge-Grounded Neural Conversation Model Open-domain conversational response generation aimed at producing more contentful and useful responses than casual chatbot-style dialogue. Generalizes Seq2Seq by conditioning response generation on conversation history and external facts for knowledge-grounded neural conversation. Adds external fact grounding to the widely used Seq2Seq conversation model, rather than conditioning only on conversation history. TITLE: A Knowledge-Grounded Neural Conversation Model

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 Neural network models are capable of generating extremely natural sounding conversational interactions.  However, these models have been mostly applied to casual scenarios (e.g., as “chatbots”) and have yet to demonstrate they can serve in more useful conversational applications. This paper presents a novel, fully data-driven, and knowledge-grounded neural conversation model aimed at producing more contentful responses.  We generalize the widely-used Sequence-to-Sequence (Seq2Seq) app

title: A large annotated corpus for learning natural language inference
searchable_text:
A large annotated corpus for learning natural language inference Natural language inference over sentence pairs, focused on recognizing entailment and contradiction. Create SNLI: a freely available, human-written corpus of labeled sentence pairs using a grounded image-captioning task. SNLI increased labeled NLI data to 570K pairs, two orders of magnitude larger than previous resources of its type. TITLE: A large annotated corpus for learning natural language inference

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Understanding entailment and contradiction is fundamental to understanding natural language, and inference about entailment and contradiction is a valuable testing ground for the development of semantic representations. However, machine learning research in this area has been dramatically limited by the lack of large-scale resources. To address this, we introduce the Stanford Natural Language Inference corpus, a new, freely available collection of labeled sentence pairs,

title: A MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUE TO IDENTIFY TRANSIT SHAPED SIGNALS
searchable_text:
A MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUE TO IDENTIFY TRANSIT SHAPED SIGNALS Identifying which periodic signals in Kepler photometric time series are transit-shaped planetary candidates rather than variable stars or instrumental noise. A machine-learning metric using dimensionality reduction and k-nearest neighbors to compare periodic photometric signals against known transits in the same data set. Introduces a new machine-learning transit-shape metric for Kepler Robovetter catalog decisions, replacing reliance on raw pipeline-detected potential signals. TITLE: A MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUE TO IDENTIFY TRANSIT SHAPED SIGNALS

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We describe a new metric that uses machine learning to determine if a periodic signal found in a photometric time series appears to be shaped like the signature of a transiting exoplanet. This metric uses dimensionality reduction and k-nearest neighbors to determine whether a given signal is sufficiently similar to known transits in the same data set. This metric is being used by the Kepler Robovetter to determine which signals should be part of the Q1–Q17 DR24 catalog of

title: A MACROSCOPIC CROWD MOTION MODEL OF GRADIENT FLOW TYPE
searchable_text:
A MACROSCOPIC CROWD MOTION MODEL OF GRADIENT FLOW TYPE Macroscopic modeling of pedestrian flow in emergency evacuation situations under density/incompressibility constraints. Formulate constrained crowd-density evolution as a Wasserstein gradient flow, with actual velocity obtained by projecting desired velocity onto admissible incompressible velocities. Moves from microscopic rigid-disc individual models to a macroscopic density-evolution formulation. TITLE: A MACROSCOPIC CROWD MOTION MODEL OF GRADIENT FLOW TYPE

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A simple model to handle the flow of people in emergency evacuation situations is considered: at every point x, the velocity U(x) that individuals at x would like to realize is given. Yet, the incompressibility constraint prevents this velocity field to be realized and the actual velocity is the projection of the desired one onto the set of admissible velocities. Instead of looking at a microscopic setting (where individuals are represented by rigid discs), here the macroscopic ap

title: A method for the estimation of the significance of cross-correlations in unevenly sampled red-noise time series
searchable_text:
A method for the estimation of the significance of cross-correlations in unevenly sampled red-noise time series Estimating significance of cross-correlations in unevenly sampled red-noise time series data. Monte Carlo significance estimation for cross-correlations, using power-law PSD light-curve models, improved cross-correlation normalization, bootstrap significance estimation, interpolation/Hanning windows, and Neyman error construction. Adds improved cross-correlation normalization, bootstrap significance estimation, Hanning-window/interpolation handling of red-noise leakage, and Neyman construction for PSD power-law index errors. TITLE: A method for the estimation of the significance of cross-correlations in unevenly sampled red-noise time series

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a practical implementation of a Monte Carlo method to estimate the significance of cross-correlations in unevenly sampled time series of data, whose statistical properties are modelled with a simple power-law power spectral density. This implementation builds on published methods; we introduce a number of improvements in the normalization of the cross-correlation function estimate and a bootstrap method for estima

title: A Model of Inductive Bias Learning
searchable_text:
A Model of Inductive Bias Learning Choosing a learner's hypothesis space for reliable generalization in environments of related machine-learning tasks. Automatically learn inductive bias by sampling multiple related tasks and selecting a hypothesis space with good solutions across the task environment. Moves inductive bias from expert hand design to automatic bias learning from multiple related tasks. TITLE: A Model of Inductive Bias Learning

ABSTRACT:
A major problem in machine learning is that of inductive bias: how to choose a learner's hypothesis space so that it is large enough to contain a solution to the problem being learnt, yet small enough to ensure reliable generalization from reasonably-sized training sets. Typically such bias is supplied by hand through the skill and insights of experts. In this paper a model for automatically learning bias is investigated. The central assumption of the model is that the learner is embedded within an e

title: A Neural Algorithm of Artistic Style
searchable_text:
A Neural Algorithm of Artistic Style Creation of artistic images that combine content and style in fine-art-like visual experiences. Use a Deep Neural Network’s neural representations to separate and recombine the content and style of arbitrary images. Moved Deep Neural Networks beyond object and face recognition into arbitrary-image artistic synthesis via explicit content/style recombination. TITLE: A Neural Algorithm of Artistic Style

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In fine art, especially painting, humans have mastered the skill to create unique visual experiences through composing a complex interplay between the content and style of an image. Thus far the algorithmic basis of this process is unknown and there exists no artificial system with similar capabilities. However, in other key areas of visual perception such as object and face recognition near-human performance was recently demonstrated by a class of biologically inspired vision models called Deep Ne

title: A Neural Bayesian Estimator for Conditional Probability Densities
searchable_text:
A Neural Bayesian Estimator for Conditional Probability Densities Event-by-event estimation of smooth conditional probability distributions for unknown inputs in forecasting, likelihood fitting, unfolding, high-energy physics, and econometry. A neural network estimates conditional density f(t|x), interpreting outputs Bayesianly as posterior probabilities and using online Bayesian regularization via extended weight decay. Changed conditional density estimation into a neural Bayesian non-parametric regression method producing event-specific posterior distributions with automatically determined regularisation parameters. TITLE: A Neural Bayesian Estimator for Conditional Probability Densities

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This article describes a robust algorithm to estimate a conditional probability density f(t|~ x ) as a non-parametric smooth regression function. It is based on a neural network and the Bayesian interpretation of the network output as a posteriori probabability. The network is trained using example events from history or simulation, which define the underlying probability density f(t, ~ x ). Once trained, the network is applied on new, unknown examples ~ x, for which it

title: A new class of accurate, mesh-free hydrodynamic simulation methods
searchable_text:
A new class of accurate, mesh-free hydrodynamic simulation methods Astrophysical hydrodynamic simulations requiring adaptive, conservative treatment of self-gravity, cosmology, shocks, mixing instabilities, discs, and subsonic flows. Kernel-discretized Lagrangian mesh-free hydrodynamics using high-order matrix gradients and Riemann solvers over volume overlap, implemented as conservative, second-order moving fluid elements. Replaces SPH particle sums or fixed/moving mesh cells with mesh-free volume kernel discretization plus matrix gradients and Riemann-solved overlaps, retaining Lagrangian adaptivity and conservation. TITLE: A new class of accurate, mesh-free hydrodynamic simulation methods

ABSTRACT:
We present two new Lagrangian methods for hydrodynamics, in a systematic comparison with moving-mesh, smoothed particle hydrodynamics (SPH), and stationary (non-moving) grid methods. The new methods are designed to simultaneously capture advantages of both SPH and grid-based/adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) schemes. title: A new class of transport distances between measures
searchable_text:
A new class of transport distances between measures Defining and analyzing distances between nonnegative Radon measures, including their topology, geometry, and relation to Wasserstein and Sobolev distances. Dynamical transport distances minimizing movement costs that depend on particle velocity and intermediate densities relative to a reference measure γ. Compared with Benamou-Brenier Wasserstein distances, the dynamical cost also depends on intermediate configuration densities with respect to γ, not only particle velocities. TITLE: A new class of transport distances between measures

title: A new method to quantify the effects of baryons on the matter power spectrum
searchable_text:
A new method to quantify the effects of baryons on the matter power spectrum Parametrizing baryonic impacts on the total matter power spectrum for future large-scale galaxy-survey cosmology. Modify dark-matter-only N-body density fields by replacing halo profiles with four baryon-aware components, then deriving particle displacement fields that reproduce adopted feedback effects. Rather than hydrodynamical simulations tied to specific feedback implementations, it offers a baryonic correction model connecting matter fields to observable gas and stellar halo distributions. TITLE: A new method to quantify the effects of baryons on the matter power spectrum

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Future large-scale galaxy surveys have the potential to become leading probes for cosmology provided the influence of baryons on the total mass distribution is understood well enough. As hydrodynamical simulations strongly depend on details in the feedback implementations, no unique and robust predictions for baryonic effects currently exist. In this paper we propose a baryonic correction model that modifies the density field of dark-matter-only N-body simula

title: A PAC-Bayesian Approach to Spectrally-Normalized Margin Bounds for Neural Networks
searchable_text:
A PAC-Bayesian Approach to Spectrally-Normalized Margin Bounds for Neural Networks Generalization bounds for feedforward neural networks. PAC-Bayes analysis deriving spectrally-normalized margin generalization bounds using products of layer spectral norms and Frobenius norms.  TITLE: A PAC-Bayesian Approach to Spectrally-Normalized Margin Bounds for Neural Networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a generalization bound for feedforward neural networks in terms of the product of the spectral norm of the layers and the Frobenius norm of the weights. The generalization bound is derived using a PAC-Bayes analysis.

title: A Parallel Corpus of Python Functions and Documentation Strings for Automated Code Documentation and Code Generation
searchable_text:
A Parallel Corpus of Python Functions and Documentation Strings for Automated Code Documentation and Code Generation Automated documentation of Python source code and automated generation of Python code from natural language documentation strings. Build and release a large Python function–docstring parallel corpus scraped from GitHub, with neural machine translation baselines and data augmentation for code documentation and generation. Compared with small, domain-constrained predecessors, this introduces a large, diverse corpus of hundreds of thousands of GitHub Python functions paired with docstrings. TITLE: A Parallel Corpus of Python Functions and Documentation Strings for Automated Code Documentation and Code Generation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Automated documentation of programming source code and automated code generation from natural language are challenging tasks of both practical and scientific interest. Progress in these areas has been limited by the low availability of parallel corpora of code and natural language descriptions, which tend to be small and constrained to specific domains. In this work we introduce a large and diverse parallel corpus of a

title: A phenomenological model of seizure initiation suggests network structure may explain seizure frequency in idiopathic generalised epilepsy
searchable_text:
A phenomenological model of seizure initiation suggests network structure may explain seizure frequency in idiopathic generalised epilepsy Explaining seizure initiation and seizure frequency in idiopathic generalised epilepsy using patient/control EEG-derived network structure. Phenomenological seizure-initiation model using a bistable switch between fixed-point and limit-cycle attractors, with quasi-analytic noisy escape-time formula extended to networks. Moved from a noise-driven bistable seizure model and numerical validation to studying combined noise and network-structure effects, including EEG-inferred patient/control networks. TITLE: A phenomenological model of seizure initiation suggests network structure may explain seizure frequency in idiopathic generalised epilepsy

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
We describe a phenomenological model of seizure initiation, consisting of a bistable switch between stable fixed point and stable limit-cycle attractors. We determine a quasi-analytic formula for the exit time problem for our model in the presence of noise. This formula--which we equate to seizure frequency--is then validated numerically, before we extend our study to explore the combined effects of noise an

title: A PLATEAU IN THE PLANET POPULATION BELOW TWICE THE SIZE OF EARTH
searchable_text:
A PLATEAU IN THE PLANET POPULATION BELOW TWICE THE SIZE OF EARTH Measuring occurrence of 0.5–8 Earth-radius transiting planets with 5–50 day periods around low-noise Kepler solar-type stars. Use TERRA to search quiet-star Kepler transits, refine stellar/planet radii with Keck spectra, and inject synthetic transits into real photometry to calibrate detection completeness and occurrence. Relative to Batalha et al., this work uses quarters 1–12, an independent TERRA search, added candidates, Keck spectra, and injected-transit completeness corrections. TITLE: A PLATEAU IN THE PLANET POPULATION BELOW TWICE THE SIZE OF EARTH

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We carry out an independent search of Kepler photometry for small transiting planets with sizes 0.5–8.0 times that of Earth and orbital periods between 5 and 50 days, with the goal of measuring the fraction of stars harboring such planets. We use a new transit search algorithm, TERRA, optimized to detect small planets around photometrically quiet stars. We restrict our stellar sample to include the 12,000 stars having the lowest photometric noise in the Kepler survey, th

title: A Population of Fast Radio Bursts at Cosmological Distances
searchable_text:
A Population of Fast Radio Bursts at Cosmological Distances Determining whether short, bright, nonrepeating radio bursts are terrestrial or celestial, and assessing their value as probes of extragalactic and intergalactic matter. Search Parkes radio telescope survey data for millisecond radio transients, then use burst properties plus host-galaxy and intergalactic-medium models to infer celestial origin and cosmological distance. Moved from uncertainty about whether single bright radio bursts are celestial or terrestrial to evidence for a cosmological population of fast, extragalactic, nonrepeating bursts. TITLE: A Population of Fast Radio Bursts at Cosmological Distances

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Mysterious Radio Bursts It has been uncertain whether single, short, and bright bursts of radio emission that have been observed are celestial or terrestrial in origin. Thornton et al. (p. 53; see the Perspective by Cordes) report the detection of four nonrepeating radio transient events with millisecond duration in data from the 64-meter Parkes radio telescope in Australia. title: A Primer on Neural Network Models for Natural Language Processing
searchable_text:
A Primer on Neural Network Models for Natural Language Processing Textual natural language processing research and tasks using neural network models. Surveys neural NLP techniques: input encoding, feed-forward, convolutional, recurrent and recursive networks, plus computation graphs for automatic gradient computation. Extends neural network attention from image recognition and speech processing toward textual natural language signals and NLP-focused model abstractions. TITLE: A Primer on Neural Network Models for Natural Language Processing

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Over the past few years, neural networks have re-emerged as powerful machine-learning models, yielding state-of-the-art results in fields such as image recognition and speech processing. More recently, neural network models started to be applied also to textual natural language signals, again with very promising results. This tutorial surveys neural network models from the perspective of natural language processing research, in an attempt to bring natural-language resea

title: A Proximal Stochastic Gradient Method with Progressive Variance Reduction
searchable_text:
A Proximal Stochastic Gradient Method with Progressive Variance Reduction Strongly convex regularized empirical risk minimization: minimizing an average of many smooth convex components plus a proximable convex function. Proximal stochastic gradient optimization using a multistage scheme that progressively reduces stochastic-gradient variance. Adds progressive variance reduction to proximal stochastic gradient, lowering overall complexity versus proximal full gradient and standard proximal stochastic gradient methods. TITLE: A Proximal Stochastic Gradient Method with Progressive Variance Reduction

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider the problem of minimizing the sum of two convex functions: one is the average of a large number of smooth component functions, and the other is a general convex function that admits a simple proximal mapping. We assume the whole objective function is strongly convex. Such problems often arise in machine learning, known as regularized empirical risk minimization. title: A Quantum Adiabatic Evolution Algorithm Applied to Random Instances of an NP-Complete Problem
searchable_text:
A Quantum Adiabatic Evolution Algorithm Applied to Random Instances of an NP-Complete Problem Randomly generated hard instances of an NP-complete problem. Slowly vary a quantum system’s Hamiltonian so it stays near its instantaneous ground state, using quantum adiabatic evolution as the computational algorithm. Introduces and tests a quantum adiabatic evolution algorithm as a new quantum-computing approach for hard NP-complete problem instances. TITLE: A Quantum Adiabatic Evolution Algorithm Applied to Random Instances of an NP-Complete Problem

YEAR: 2001

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A quantum system will stay near its instantaneous ground state if the Hamiltonian that governs its evolution varies slowly enough. This quantum adiabatic behavior is the basis of a new class of algorithms for quantum computing. We tested one such algorithm by applying it to randomly generated hard instances of an NP-complete problem. For the small examples that we could simulate, the quantum adiabatic algorithm worked well, providing evidence

title: A Randomized Algorithm for Principal Component Analysis
searchable_text:
A Randomized Algorithm for Principal Component Analysis Principal component analysis requiring low-rank approximation of data matrices, especially when rank-deficient approximations can only achieve a few digits of relative spectral-norm accuracy. An efficient low-rank matrix approximation algorithm for PCA that achieves spectral-norm accuracy close to the best possible on arbitrary-size matrices. Extends efficient low-rank approximation guarantees from small-dimension cases to matrices of arbitrary sizes with near-best possible accuracy. TITLE: A Randomized Algorithm for Principal Component Analysis

YEAR: 2008

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Principal component analysis (PCA) requires the computation of a low-rank approximation to a matrix containing the data being analyzed. In many applications of PCA, the best possible accuracy of any rank-deficient approximation is at most a few digits (measured in the spectral norm, relative to the spectral norm of the matrix being approximated). In such circumstances, efficient algorithms have not come with guarantees of good accuracy, unless one or both dimensions of the matrix

title: A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning
searchable_text:
A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning Sequential prediction problems where future observations depend on previous predictions, including imitation learning and structured sequence labeling. An iterative reduction that trains a stationary deterministic policy by treating sequential prediction as no-regret online learning. Unlike prior methods training non-stationary or stochastic policies over many iterations, it trains a stationary deterministic policy via no-regret online learning. TITLE: A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Sequential prediction problems such as imitation learning, where future observations depend on previous predictions (actions), violate the common i.i.d. assumptions made in statistical learning. This leads to poor performance in theory and often in practice. Some recent approaches provide stronger guarantees in this setting, but remain somewhat unsatisfactory as they train either non-stationary or stochastic policies and require a large number of

title: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Weaning of Mechanical Ventilation in Intensive Care Units
searchable_text:
A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Weaning of Mechanical Ventilation in Intensive Care Units Personalized decision support for weaning ICU patients from invasive mechanical ventilation, including extubation readiness, sedation dosage, and ventilator support. Off-policy reinforcement learning learns best sedation and ventilator-support actions for each ICU patient state from historical data. Replaces variable clinical weaning protocols with learned off-policy reinforcement-learning policies that recommend patient-state-specific sedation dosage and ventilator support. TITLE: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Weaning of Mechanical Ventilation in Intensive Care Units

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The management of invasive mechanical ventilation, and the regulation of sedation and analgesia during ventilation, constitutes a major part of the care of patients admitted to intensive care units. Both prolonged dependence on mechanical ventilation and premature extubation are associated with increased risk of complications and higher hospital costs, but clinical opinion on the best protocol for weaning patients off of a ventilator varies.

title: A resonant chain of four transiting, sub-Neptune planets
searchable_text:
A resonant chain of four transiting, sub-Neptune planets Formation and orbital architecture of tightly packed, resonant chains of transiting sub-Neptune planets in compact multi-planet systems. Moved from population-level excesses of near-resonant planet pairs to an individual four-planet system whose architecture indicates migration formation. YEAR: 2016

ABSTRACT:
Surveys have revealed many multi-planet systems containing super-Earths and Neptunes in orbits of a few days to a few months. There is debate whether in situ assembly or inward migration is the dominant mechanism of the formation of such planetary systems. Simulations suggest that migration creates tightly packed systems with planets whose orbital periods may be expressed as ratios of small integers (resonances), often in a many-planet series (chain). title: A seven-planet resonant chain in TRAPPIST-1
searchable_text:
A seven-planet resonant chain in TRAPPIST-1 Orbital characterization of the seventh Earth-sized transiting planet in the ultracool-dwarf TRAPPIST-1 system. Use K2 mission observations and Laplace-relation expectations to constrain TRAPPIST-1 h and place it within a three-body resonant chain. DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

The TRAPPIST-1 system is the first transiting planet system found orbiting an ultracool dwarf star1. Subsequently, TRAPPIST-1 was observed as part of the K2 mission and, with these new data, we report the measurement of an 18.77 day orbital period for the outermost transiting planet, TRAPPIST-1 h, which was previously unconstrained. title: A simple formula for the average gate fidelity of a quantum dynamical operation [rapid communication]
searchable_text:
A simple formula for the average gate fidelity of a quantum dynamical operation [rapid communication] Estimating average gate fidelity for quantum dynamical operations on qudits. Derives a simple formula for average fidelity between a unitary quantum gate and a general quantum operation on a qudit, with a simplified proof linking it to entanglement fidelity. ABSTRACT:
This Letter presents a simple formula for the average fidelity between a unitary quantum gate and a general quantum operation on a qudit, generalizing the formula for qubits found by Bowdrey et al. [Phys. Lett. A 294 (2002) 258]. This formula may be useful for experimental determination of average gate fidelity. We also give a simplified proof of a formula due to Horodecki et al. [Phys. Rev. title: A Smoothed Dual Approach for Variational Wasserstein Problems
searchable_text:
A Smoothed Dual Approach for Variational Wasserstein Problems Variational problems over probability measures involving Wasserstein distances, including barycenters and gradient flows of spatial regularization functionals. Entropically smooth the dual formulation of Wasserstein variational problems to obtain differentiable convex optimization objectives. Adds entropic smoothing to the Carlier, Oberman, and Oudet dual formulation, changing it into smooth, differentiable convex optimization problems. title: A structural approach to relaxation in glassy liquids
searchable_text:
A structural approach to relaxation in glassy liquids Understanding whether and how local structure controls dynamics and relaxation in three-dimensional glassy liquids. Machine learning identifies ‘softness’, a local structural field, and uses it to model relaxation in glassy liquids. Replaces uniform-structure mean-field assumptions and weak structural descriptors with a machine-learned local field, softness, that links structure to dynamics. TITLE: A structural approach to relaxation in glassy liquids

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The relation between structure and dynamics in glasses is not fully understood. A new approach based on machine learning now reveals a correlation between softness—a structural property—and glassy dynamics. In contrast with crystallization, there is no noticeable structural change at the glass transition. title: A STUDY OF THE SHORTEST-PERIOD PLANETS FOUND WITH KEPLER
searchable_text:
A STUDY OF THE SHORTEST-PERIOD PLANETS FOUND WITH KEPLER Discovering and characterizing Kepler transiting ultra-short-period planets with orbital periods shorter than one day. TITLE: A STUDY OF THE SHORTEST-PERIOD PLANETS FOUND WITH KEPLER

We computed Fourier transforms of the photometric time series for all 200,000 target stars, and detected transit signals based on the presence of regularly spaced sharp peaks in the Fourier spectrum. title: A SURVEY FOR VERY SHORT-PERIOD PLANETS IN THE KEPLER DATA
searchable_text:
A SURVEY FOR VERY SHORT-PERIOD PLANETS IN THE KEPLER DATA Very short-period transiting planetary candidates in Kepler data, especially objects with orbital periods under 12 hours. Search public Kepler data for very short-period transits, then model transit light curves, ellipsoidal variations, and secondary eclipses to constrain radii, masses, and temperatures. Focused Kepler mining specifically on ultra-short orbital periods below 12 hours and combined photometric effects to characterize such candidates. Our preliminary survey has revealed four planetary candidates, all with orbital periods less than 12 hr. We have analyzed the data for these candidates using photometric models that include transit light curves, ellipsoidal variations, and secondary eclipses to constrain the candidates' radii, masses, and effective temperatures. title: A Survey of Gaussian Convolution Algorithms
searchable_text:
A Survey of Gaussian Convolution Algorithms Efficient Gaussian convolution for signal and image processing algorithms. Survey and comparison of approximate Gaussian convolution algorithms using finite impulse response filters, DFT/DCT convolution, box filters, and recursive filters, with explicit boundary handling. Adds focused development of boundary handling and experimental speed-quality comparison across major approximate Gaussian convolution methods. TITLE: A Survey of Gaussian Convolution Algorithms

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Gaussian convolution is a common operation and building block for algorithms in signal and image processing. Consequently, its ecient computation is important, and many fast approximations have been proposed. In this survey, we discuss approximate Gaussian convolution based on nite impulse response lters, DFT and DCT based convolution, box lters, and several recursive lters. title: A Survey of Model Compression and Acceleration for Deep Neural Networks
searchable_text:
A Survey of Model Compression and Acceleration for Deep Neural Networks Compacting and accelerating deep neural networks for deployment under low memory resources or strict latency requirements. Survey and taxonomy of DNN compression/acceleration methods: parameter pruning and quantization, low-rank factorization, compact convolutional filters, and knowledge distillation. Deep neural networks (DNNs) have recently achieved great success in many visual recognition tasks. However, existing deep neural network models are computationally expensive and memory intensive, hindering their deployment in devices with low memory resources or in applications with strict latency requirements. title: A survey of robot learning from demonstrations for Human-Robot Collaboration
searchable_text:
A survey of robot learning from demonstrations for Human-Robot Collaboration Human-robot collaborative tasks where non-robotics experts teach robots new knowledge without mechanical engineering or programming expertise. Survey robot learning from demonstration for human-robot collaboration, emphasizing communication frameworks, Interactive/Active learning, and complex-task learning approaches. TITLE: A survey of robot learning from demonstrations for Human-Robot Collaboration

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Robot learning from demonstration (LfD) is a research paradigm that can play an important role in addressing the issue of scaling up robot learning. Since this type of approach enables non-robotics experts can teach robots new knowledge without any professional background of mechanical engineering or computer programming skills, robots can appear in the real world even if it does not have any prior knowledge for any tasks like a new born baby. title: A survey of the Schr\"odinger problem and some of its connections with optimal transport
searchable_text:
A survey of the Schr\"odinger problem and some of its connections with optimal transport The Schrödinger problem and its connections with optimal transport, intended as a basic user’s guide and literature survey. A survey-style presentation of the Schrödinger problem, its optimal transport connections, related literature, and additional new results. Unlike a basic survey alone, the article also proves some new results. TITLE: A survey of the Schr\"odinger problem and some of its connections with optimal transport

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This article is aimed at presenting the Schr\"odinger problem and some of its connections with optimal transport. We hope that it can be used as a basic user's guide to Schr\"odinger problem. We also give a survey of the related literature. In addition, some new results are proved.

title: A Survey on Multi-Task Learning
searchable_text:
A Survey on Multi-Task Learning Machine-learning settings with multiple related tasks where the goal is improved generalization performance across all tasks. Survey and taxonomy of MTL: define the paradigm, classify algorithms into five categories, and review combinations with other learning paradigms plus scalable implementations. YEAR: 2017

ABSTRACT:
Multi-Task Learning (MTL) is a learning paradigm in machine learning and its aim is to leverage useful information contained in multiple related tasks to help improve the generalization performance of all the tasks. In this paper, we give a survey for MTL from the perspective of algorithmic modeling, applications and theoretical analyses. title: A Survey on Software Fault Localization
searchable_text:
A Survey on Software Fault Localization Software fault localization: identifying the locations of faults in programs during debugging. Catalogs and comprehensively overviews a broad spectrum of software fault localization techniques, discussing cross-cutting issues and concerns.  TITLE: A Survey on Software Fault Localization

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Software fault localization, the act of identifying the locations of faults in a program, is widely recognized to be one of the most tedious, time consuming, and expensive - yet equally critical - activities in program debugging. Due to the increasing scale and complexity of software today, manually locating faults when failures occur is rapidly becoming infeasible, and consequently, there is a strong demand for techniques that can guide software developers to the locations of faults in a progra

title: A systematic study of the class imbalance problem in convolutional neural networks
searchable_text:
A systematic study of the class imbalance problem in convolutional neural networks Class imbalance effects on convolutional neural network classification across benchmark image datasets of increasing complexity. Systematically compare CNN class-imbalance remedies: oversampling, undersampling, two-phase training, and thresholding using multi-class ROC AUC on MNIST, CIFAR-10, and ImageNet. Moves class-imbalance study from classical machine learning into a systematic deep-learning/CNN evaluation across multiple benchmark datasets and mitigation methods. TITLE: A systematic study of the class imbalance problem in convolutional neural networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this study, we systematically investigate the impact of class imbalance on classification performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and compare frequently used methods to address the issue. Class imbalance is a common problem that has been comprehensively studied in classical machine learning, yet very limited systematic research is available in the context of deep learning. title: A Technique for Extracting Highly Precise Photometry for the Two-Wheeled Kepler Mission
searchable_text:
A Technique for Extracting Highly Precise Photometry for the Two-Wheeled Kepler Mission Highly precise photometric light-curve extraction from pixel-level data for the two-wheeled extended Kepler mission, K2. Correlate pixel-level flux measurements with spacecraft pointing to remove detector pixel-response-dependent photometric variations in K2 light curves. Unlike raw two-wheeled K2 photometry after degraded pointing, it explicitly removes flux dependence on spacecraft pointing caused by nonuniform detector pixel response. TITLE: A Technique for Extracting Highly Precise Photometry for the Two-Wheeled Kepler Mission

YEAR: 2014

ABSTRACT:
The original Kepler mission achieved high photometric precision thanks to ultrastable pointing enabled by use of four reaction wheels. We present a technique for generating photometric light curves from pixel-level data obtained with the two-wheeled extended Kepler mission

title: A temperate rocky super-Earth transiting a nearby cool star

A temperate rocky super-Earth transiting a nearby cool star Nearby transiting temperate Earth-sized or super-Earth planets around M dwarf stars whose atmospheres could be probed by transmission spectroscopy. Observe a transiting planet around nearby cool M dwarf LHS 1140, measuring radius, mass, insolation, and eccentricity to infer rocky composition, habitable-zone placement, and atmospheric follow-up potential. Compared with Proxima Centauri’s likely non-transiting planet and TRAPPIST-1’s poorly constrained densities, LHS 1140b is a nearby transiting temperate planet with measured mass and rocky-density evidence. Statistical results suggest that the nearest transiting Earth-sized planet in the liquid-water, habitable zone of an M dwarf star is probably around 10.5 parsecs away. A temperate planet

title: A Theoretically Grounded Application of Dropout in Recurrent Neural Networks

A Theoretically Grounded Application of Dropout in Recurrent Neural Networks Reducing overfitting in recurrent neural networks for language modelling and sentiment analysis tasks. Variational inference based dropout applied to recurrent neural networks, specifically LSTM and GRU models. Yet a major difficulty with these models is their tendency to overfit, with dropout shown to fail when applied to recurrent layers. Recent results at the intersection of Bayesian modelling and deep learning offer a Bayesian interpretation of common deep learning techniques such as dropout. title: A Theory of Generative ConvNet

A Theory of Generative ConvNet Generative modeling and synthesis of realistic natural image patterns using ConvNet-derived energy-based models. Derive a generative random field ConvNet from a discriminative multicategory ConvNet with a Gaussian white-noise base category, yielding a ReLU piecewise-Gaussian energy model with auto-encoder-defined means. Transforms the commonly used discriminative ConvNet into a generative ConvNet by adding a reference-distribution base category and interpreting bottom-up filters as top-down basis functions. title: A theory of learning from different domains

A theory of learning from different domains Domain adaptation for classification when labeled source data are plentiful but target data have a different distribution and little or no labels. Bound target error using source error plus classifier-induced domain divergence, then optimize a convex combination of source and target empirical errors based on divergence, sample sizes, and hypothesis-class complexity. Instead of source-only, target-only, or equal weighting, it derives an optimally chosen source-target error mixture governed by divergence, sample sizes, and hypothesis complexity. TITLE: A theory of learning from different domains

ABSTRACT:
Discriminative learning methods for classification perform well when training and test data are drawn from the same distribution. Often, however, we have plentiful labeled training data from a source domain but wish to learn a classifier which performs well on a target domain with a different distribution and little or no labeled training data. title: A TRANSITING JUPITER ANALOG
searchable_text:
A TRANSITING JUPITER ANALOG Detecting and characterizing long-period Jupiter analogs in transit survey data, especially cold post-snow-line planets where transit methods are intrinsically biased. Validate transiting Jupiter analogs from Kepler archival photometry by using two observed transits under continuous photometric coverage to precisely measure long orbital periods. Shifted Jupiter-analog discovery from mainly radial-velocity surveys to the first validated transiting Jupiter analog with a uniquely precise period from Kepler photometry. TITLE: A TRANSITING JUPITER ANALOG

ABSTRACT:
Decadal-long radial velocity surveys have recently started to discover analogs to the most influential planet of our solar system, Jupiter. Despite the great successes of recent transit surveys, Jupiter analogs represent a terra incognita, owing to the strong intrinsic bias of this method against long orbital periods. title: A tutorial on conformal prediction
searchable_text:
A tutorial on conformal prediction Online successive prediction of labels with precise confidence levels, where each true label is revealed before the next prediction. Uses past labeled experience and a chosen error probability to wrap any predictor, producing a label set with calibrated confidence for the true label. Recasts conformal prediction as a self-contained tutorial with numerical examples, compared with the more comprehensive 2005 book treatment. TITLE: A tutorial on conformal prediction

ABSTRACT:
Conformal prediction uses past experience to determine precise levels of confidence in new predictions. Given an error probability e, together with a method that makes a prediction ŷ of a label y, it produces a set of labels, typically containing ŷ, that also contains y with probability 1  e. Conformal prediction can be applied to any method for producing ŷ: a nearest-neighbor method, a support-vector machine, ridge regression, etc. 
 
Conformal prediction is designed for an on-line setting in which

title: A Universal Catalyst for First-Order Optimization

A Universal Catalyst for First-Order Optimization First-order convex optimization methods, including gradient, coordinate, variance-reduced, dual coordinate, Finito/MISO, and proximal variants. Accelerate first-order methods by approximately solving a sequence of well-chosen auxiliary convex problems via a catalyst scheme based on accelerated proximal point analysis. Turns accelerated proximal point analysis into a generic acceleration wrapper with explicit support for non-strongly convex objectives across many first-order algorithms. TITLE: A Universal Catalyst for First-Order Optimization

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a generic scheme for accelerating first-order optimization methods in the sense of Nesterov, which builds upon a new analysis of the accelerated proximal point algorithm. Our approach consists of minimizing a convex objective by approximately solving a sequence of well-chosen auxiliary problems, leading to faster convergence. title: Accelerated Gradient Descent Escapes Saddle Points Faster than Gradient Descent

title: Accelerated gradient methods for nonconvex nonlinear and stochastic programming

Accelerated gradient methods for nonconvex nonlinear and stochastic programming Nonconvex smooth nonlinear programming, composite optimization, and nonconvex stochastic optimization using first-order information. Generalize Nesterov’s accelerated gradient to nonconvex deterministic, composite, and stochastic programs via properly specified or aggressive stepsize policies and stochastic approximation variants. Extends accelerated gradient beyond convex smooth optimization to nonconvex nonlinear and stochastic programming, establishing convergence there for the first time. title: Accelerated proximal stochastic dual coordinate ascent for regularized loss minimization
searchable_text:
Accelerated proximal stochastic dual coordinate ascent for regularized loss minimization Regularized loss minimization for machine learning optimization problems including SVM, logistic regression, ridge regression, Lasso, and multiclass SVM. A proximal stochastic dual coordinate ascent method accelerated through an inner-outer iteration procedure. Adds a proximal version of stochastic dual coordinate ascent and accelerates it with an inner-outer iteration procedure. TITLE: Accelerated proximal stochastic dual coordinate ascent for regularized loss minimization

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a proximal version of the stochastic dual coordinate ascent method and show how to accelerate the method using an inner-outer iteration procedure. We analyze the runtime of the framework and obtain rates that improve state-of-the-art results for various key machine learning optimization problems including SVM, logistic regression, ridge regression, Lasso, and multiclass SVM. title: Accelerating incoherent dedispersion

Implement direct, tree, and sub-band incoherent dedispersion algorithms in C for CUDA on modern GPUs, guided by performance analysis. ABSTRACT:

For current and future transient pipelines, dedispersion can dominate the total execution time, meaning its computational speed acts as a constraint on the quality and quantity of science results. It is thus critical that the algorithm be able to take advantage of trends in commodity computing hardware. With this goal in mind, we present an analysis of the ‘direct’, ‘tree’ and ‘

title: Accurate and efficient waveforms for compact binaries on eccentric orbits
searchable_text:
Accurate and efficient waveforms for compact binaries on eccentric orbits Gravitational-wave modeling for compact binaries on small-eccentricity orbits in the sensitivity band of ground-based detectors just prior to merger. Purely analytic frequency-domain waveform model using a spectral approximation to the post-Newtonian Kepler problem, including eccentricity effects up to O(e^8) at each post-Newtonian order. Extends TaylorF2 and the Yunes et al. post-circular approximation into an analytic frequency-domain model with higher-order eccentricity corrections through O(e^8). title: Action-Conditional Video Prediction using Deep Networks in Atari Games
searchable_text:
Action-Conditional Video Prediction using Deep Networks in Atari Games Action-conditioned future frame prediction for vision-based reinforcement learning in Atari games from the Arcade Learning Environment. Deep neural video predictors with encoding, action-conditional transformation, and decoding layers using convolutional and recurrent networks.
