title: Actively Learning what makes a Discrete Sequence Valid
searchable_text:
Actively Learning what makes a Discrete Sequence Valid Validity assessment for discrete objects represented as sequences, especially when discrete generative deep learning models produce invalid sequences. Learn a deep recurrent validator that estimates whether a partial sequence can begin a full valid sequence, using a Bayesian active learning-inspired approach. Instead of only generating discrete sequences, it adds an actively learned recurrent validator estimating prefix validity probabilities and element-level influence on validity. title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization

Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization Large-scale stochastic objective optimization with non-stationary objectives and noisy or sparse gradients. We introduce Adam, an algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions, based on adaptive estimates of lower-order moments. The method is straightforward to implement, is computationally efficient, has little memory requirements, is invariant to diagonal rescaling of the gradients, and is well suited for problems that are large in terms of data and/or parameters. title: Adversarial Examples Are Not Easily Detected: Bypassing Ten Detection Methods

Adversarial Examples Are Not Easily Detected: Bypassing Ten Detection Methods Detecting adversarial examples in neural networks: inputs close to natural inputs but classified incorrectly. Survey ten adversarial-example detection proposals and evaluate them by constructing new loss functions designed to bypass each detector. Shifted evaluation from testing proposed detectors as-is to adaptive attacks that construct new loss functions to bypass ten detection methods. title: Adversarial Examples for Evaluating Reading Comprehension Systems

title: Aggregated Residual Transformations for Deep Neural Networks

title: Agile Autonomous Driving using End-to-End Deep Imitation Learning
searchable_text:
Agile Autonomous Driving using End-to-End Deep Imitation Learning Agile, high-speed off-road autonomous driving using only low-cost sensors. title: AI Safety Gridworlds

title: Alchemical and structural distribution based representation for universal quantum machine learning.

Universal quantum machine learning of electronic properties across chemical compound space, including molecules, protein side-chains, water clusters, and crystalline solids. title: Almost-linear time decoding algorithm for topological codes

title: An Actor-Critic Algorithm for Sequence Prediction

An Actor-Critic Algorithm for Sequence Prediction Neural sequence generation, especially German-English machine translation and broader natural language generation tasks. title: An Analog of the 2-Wasserstein Metric in Non-Commutative Probability Under Which the Fermionic Fokker–Planck Equation is Gradient Flow for the Entropy

title: AN EFFICIENT AUTOMATED VALIDATION PROCEDURE FOR EXOPLANET TRANSIT CANDIDATES

AN EFFICIENT AUTOMATED VALIDATION PROCEDURE FOR EXOPLANET TRANSIT CANDIDATES Automated validation of transiting exoplanet candidates, especially Kepler candidates where many signals remain unconfirmed. title: An efficient optimal planning and control framework for quadrupedal locomotion

title: An Emphatic Approach to the Problem of Off-policy Temporal-Difference Learning

title: An Integrated Encyclopedia of DNA Elements in the Human Genome
searchable_text:
An Integrated Encyclopedia of DNA Elements in the Human Genome Determining functional elements across the human genome, especially outside well-studied protein-coding regions. Systematic genome-wide mapping of transcription, transcription factor association, chromatin structure, and histone modification to annotate biochemical function. Shifted functional annotation from protein-coding emphasis to an expansive genome-wide encyclopedia of regulatory and biochemical elements. TITLE: An Integrated Encyclopedia of DNA Elements in the Human Genome

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The human genome encodes the blueprint of life, but the function of the vast majority of its nearly three billion bases is unknown. The Encyclopedia of DNA Elements (ENCODE) project has systematically mapped regions of transcription, transcription factor association, chromatin structure and histone modification. These data enabled us to assign biochemical functions for 80% of the genome, in particular outside of the well-studied protein-coding regions. Many discovered cand

title: An introduction to PYTHIA 8.2
searchable_text:
An introduction to PYTHIA 8.2 Generation of high-energy collision events and LHC physics studies from few-body hard processes to complex multiparticle final states. C++ event-generation framework combining hard-process libraries, parton showers, matching/merging, multiparton interactions, beam remnants, string fragmentation, particle decays, utilities, and external interfaces. Second main release after the complete Fortran-to-C++ rewrite, adding many new features and reaching broader replacement maturity. TITLE: An introduction to PYTHIA 8.2

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Pythia program is a standard tool for the generation of events in high-energy collisions, comprising a coherent set of physics models for the evolution from a few-body hard process to a complex multiparticle final state. It contains a library of hard processes, models for initial- and final-state parton showers, matching and merging methods between hard processes and parton showers, multiparton interactions, beam remnants, string fragmentation and particle decays. It also has a set of utilities and se

title: An iterative thresholding algorithm for linear inverse problems with a sparsity constraint
searchable_text:
An iterative thresholding algorithm for linear inverse problems with a sparsity constraint Linear inverse problems whose solutions are assumed to have sparse expansions in an arbitrary preassigned orthonormal basis. Weighted 𝓁p-penalization of expansion coefficients, optimized by a Landweber iteration with thresholding/nonlinear shrinkage at each step. Replaces usual quadratic regularizing penalties with weighted 𝓁p penalties, especially p < 2, to better suit sparse coefficient expansions. TITLE: An iterative thresholding algorithm for linear inverse problems with a sparsity constraint

YEAR: 2003

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider linear inverse problems where the solution is assumed to have a sparse expansion on an arbitrary preassigned orthonormal basis. We prove that replacing the usual quadratic regularizing penalties by weighted 𝓁p‐penalties on the coefficients of such expansions, with 1 ≤ p ≤ 2, still regularizes the problem. Use of such 𝓁p‐penalized problems with p < 2 is often advocated when one expects the underlying ideal noiseless solution to have a

title: An optimal strategy for epilepsy surgery: Disruption of the rich-club?
searchable_text:
An optimal strategy for epilepsy surgery: Disruption of the rich-club? Pre-surgical planning for drug-resistant epilepsy: identifying brain regions whose resection will prevent seizure generation and improve post-operative seizure freedom. Use mathematical seizure models, reduced from neural mass to canonical dynamics, to simulate network topologies and predict which node removals/resections render epileptic networks seizure free. Shifted surgical planning from mapping seizure regions with multimodal/iEEG data toward mechanistic network modeling that prospectively compares virtual resections across network topologies. TITLE: An optimal strategy for epilepsy surgery: Disruption of the rich-club?

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Surgery is a therapeutic option for people with epilepsy whose seizures are not controlled by anti-epilepsy drugs. In pre-surgical planning, an array of data modalities, often including intra-cranial EEG, is used in an attempt to map regions of the brain thought to be crucial for the generation of seizures. These regions are then resected with the hope that the individual is rendered seizure free as a consequence. However, post-operative seizure freedom is currently sub-opti

title: An overview of gradient descent optimization algorithms
searchable_text:
An overview of gradient descent optimization algorithms Statistical comparison of machine learning model metric values on test sets, especially additive metrics such as binary cross-entropy for CTR prediction models. Divide the test sample into N parts, compute additive metrics per part, verify normality/QQ behavior, then compare mean metric values with a modified Student's t-test or base-model confidence intervals. Replaces traditional single test-set metric comparison and cross-validation t-tests with a partitioned test-sample procedure intended to yield normally distributed metric realizations for valid modified t-testing. TITLE: An overview of gradient descent optimization algorithms

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The statistical testing technique is considered to compare the metrics values of machine learning models on a test set. Since the values of metrics depend not only on the models, but also on the data, it may turn out that different models are the best on different test sets. For this reason, the traditional approach to comparing the values of metrics on a test set is often not enough. Sometimes a statistical comparison of the results obtained on the basis of cross-validation is u

title: Analytic Light Curves for Planetary Transit Searches
searchable_text:
Analytic Light Curves for Planetary Transit Searches Detection, simulation, and parameter fitting of planetary transit light curves for limb-darkened stars. Exact analytic formulae compute stellar eclipses under quadratic or nonlinear limb darkening, with a small-planet constant-surface-brightness approximation for faster transit light curves. Introduces exact analytic light-curve formulae for quadratic or nonlinear limb darkening rather than relying on slower numerical eclipse computations or cruder approximations. TITLE: Analytic Light Curves for Planetary Transit Searches

YEAR: 2002

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present exact analytic formulae for the eclipse of a star described by quadratic or nonlinear limb darkening. In the limit that the planet radius is less than a tenth of the stellar radius, we show that the exact light curve can be well approximated by assuming the region of the star blocked by the planet has constant surface brightness. We apply these results to the Hubble Space Telescope observations of HD 209458, showing that the ratio of the planetary to stellar radii is 0.12

title: Analyzing the Performance of Multilayer Neural Networks for Object Recognition
searchable_text:
Analyzing the Performance of Multilayer Neural Networks for Object Recognition Object recognition and standard computer vision recognition datasets/tasks. Experimentally probe several aspects of CNN feature learning to build evidence-backed intuitions for applying CNNs to computer vision problems. Moves from relying on engineered SIFT/HOG representations to empirically analyzing learned CNN features for practical object-recognition guidance. TITLE: Analyzing the Performance of Multilayer Neural Networks for Object Recognition

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In the last two years, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved an impressive suite of results on standard recognition datasets and tasks. CNN-based features seem poised to quickly replace engineered representations, such as SIFT and HOG. However, compared to SIFT and HOG, we understand much less about the nature of the features learned by large CNNs. In this paper, we experimentally probe several aspects of CNN feature learning in an attempt to h

title: APFELgrid : A high performance tool for parton density determinations
searchable_text:
APFELgrid : A high performance tool for parton density determinations Fast computation of hadronic observables under variation of parton distribution functions in hadron collider PDF fits. Combines APPLgrid interpolated weight grids with APFEL PDF and αs evolution factors into efficient FastKernel tables, reducing observable evaluation to an optimized scalar product. Transforms APPLgrid interpolated weight tables into a more efficient PDF-fitting format by incorporating APFEL evolution factors. TITLE: APFELgrid : A high performance tool for parton density determinations

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Abstract We present a new software package designed to reduce the computational burden of hadron collider measurements in Parton Distribution Function (PDF) fits. The APFELgrid package converts interpolated weight tables provided by APPLgrid files into a more efficient format for PDF fitting by the combination with PDF and α s evolution factors provided by APFEL . This combination significantly reduces the number of operations required to perform the calculation of

title: Approximate Nash Equilibria in Partially Observed Stochastic Games with Mean-Field Interactions
searchable_text:
Approximate Nash Equilibria in Partially Observed Stochastic Games with Mean-Field Interactions Discrete-time partially observed mean-field games with infinite-horizon discounted costs and mean-field interactions among many decentralized agents. Convert partial observations into a fully observed belief-space control problem, then apply dynamic programming to prove mean-field Nash equilibria and approximate finite-agent equilibria. Rather than solving the large finite game directly, it studies the infinite-population mean-field limit and transfers its equilibrium policy back to many-agent games. TITLE: Approximate Nash Equilibria in Partially Observed Stochastic Games with Mean-Field Interactions

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Establishing the existence of Nash equilibria for partially observed stochastic dynamic games is known to be quite challenging, with the difficulties stemming from the noisy nature of the measurements available to individual players (agents) and the decentralized nature of this information. When the number of players is sufficiently large and the interactions among agents is of the mean-field type, one way to overcome this challenge is to i

title: Approximating Likelihood Ratios with Calibrated Discriminative Classifiers
searchable_text:
Approximating Likelihood Ratios with Calibrated Discriminative Classifiers Likelihood-free statistical inference for high-dimensional observations when simulators describe data-generation but cannot evaluate likelihoods. Use calibrated discriminative classifiers as one-dimensional reductions to approximate generalized likelihood ratio statistics from simulator-generated data. Replaces prior-dependent Approximate Bayesian Computation with a complementary discriminative-classifier route to likelihood-free inference that does not require a parameter prior. TITLE: Approximating Likelihood Ratios with Calibrated Discriminative Classifiers

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In many fields of science, generalized likelihood ratio tests are established tools for statistical inference. At the same time, it has become increasingly common that a simulator (or generative model) is used to describe complex processes that tie parameters $\theta$ of an underlying theory and measurement apparatus to high-dimensional observations $\mathbf{x}\in \mathbb{R}^p$. However, simulator often do not provide a way to evaluate the likelihood function f

title: Arbitrary Style Transfer in Real-Time with Adaptive Instance Normalization
searchable_text:
Arbitrary Style Transfer in Real-Time with Adaptive Instance Normalization Arbitrary neural style transfer in real time, rendering a content image in the style of another image without restricting styles to a predefined set. Use an adaptive instance normalization layer to align content-feature mean and variance with style-feature mean and variance in a single feed-forward network. Replaces slow iterative optimization and fixed-style feed-forward networks with real-time arbitrary style transfer using adaptive instance normalization. TITLE: Arbitrary Style Transfer in Real-Time with Adaptive Instance Normalization

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Gatys et al. recently introduced a neural algorithm that renders a content image in the style of another image, achieving so-called style transfer. However, their framework requires a slow iterative optimization process, which limits its practical application. Fast approximations with feed-forward neural networks have been proposed to speed up neural style transfer. Unfortunately, the speed improvement comes at a cost: the network is usually tied to a fixed set

title: Artificial neural networks for solving ordinary and partial differential equations
searchable_text:
Artificial neural networks for solving ordinary and partial differential equations Initial and boundary value problems for ordinary differential equations, coupled ODE systems, and partial differential equations. Construct trial solutions with a fixed condition-satisfying term plus a feedforward neural-network term trained to satisfy the differential equation without altering initial/boundary conditions. Replaces conventional discretization-centered solvers with neural-network trial functions that satisfy initial/boundary conditions by construction and optimize equation residuals. TITLE: Artificial neural networks for solving ordinary and partial differential equations

YEAR: 1997

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a method to solve initial and boundary value problems using artificial neural networks. A trial solution of the differential equation is written as a sum of two parts. The first part satisfies the initial/boundary conditions and contains no adjustable parameters. The second part is constructed so as not to affect the initial/boundary conditions. This part involves a feedforward neural network containing adjustable parameters (the weights). He

title: Associations of Unilateral Whisker and Olfactory Signals Induce Synapse Formation and Memory Cell Recruitment in Bilateral Barrel Cortices: Cellular Mechanism for Unilateral Training Toward Bilateral Memory
searchable_text:
Associations of Unilateral Whisker and Olfactory Signals Induce Synapse Formation and Memory Cell Recruitment in Bilateral Barrel Cortices: Cellular Mechanism for Unilateral Training Toward Bilateral Memory Cellular basis by which unilateral sensory training or limb skills can be retrieved bilaterally. Pair unilateral whisker and odor stimulation to co-activate sensory cortices, inducing contralateral barrel axon projection, glutamatergic synapse formation, GABAergic downregulation, and associative memory-cell recruitment. Shifts from bilateral retrieval after unilateral learning to a cellular substrate: bilateral associative memory-cell recruitment plus synapse innervations between barrel cortices. TITLE: Associations of Unilateral Whisker and Olfactory Signals Induce Synapse Formation and Memory Cell Recruitment in Bilateral Barrel Cortices: Cellular Mechanism for Unilateral Training Toward Bilateral Memory

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Somatosensory signals and operative skills learned by unilateral limbs can be retrieved bilaterally. In terms of cellular mechanism underlying this unilateral learning toward bilateral memory, we hypothesized that associative memory cells in bilateral cortices and synapse innervations between them were produced. In the examination of this hy

title: Asynchronous Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent
searchable_text:
Asynchronous Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent Distributed machine learning in heterogeneous environments requiring communication-efficient training without centralized bottlenecks. An asynchronous decentralized stochastic gradient descent algorithm that avoids centralized parameter servers while enabling parallel worker updates. Compared with AllReduce-SGD, parameter-server A-PSGD, and D-PSGD, it combines asynchrony with decentralization while preserving optimal convergence and linear speedup. TITLE: Asynchronous Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most commonly used distributed machine learning systems are either synchronous or centralized asynchronous. Synchronous algorithms like AllReduce-SGD perform poorly in a heterogeneous environment, while asynchronous algorithms using a parameter server suffer from 1) communication bottleneck at parameter servers when workers are many, and 2) significantly worse convergence when the traffic to parameter server is congested. Can we design an algorithm that is robust in a het

title: Autoencoder-augmented neuroevolution for visual doom playing
searchable_text:
Autoencoder-augmented neuroevolution for visual doom playing Visual reinforcement learning from raw pixel observations in VizDoom, specifically Doom health-pack gathering with incomplete information and delayed rewards. Train an autoencoder for low-dimensional environment observations, then use CMA-ES to evolve neural network controllers on that representation, updating autoencoder training throughout evolution. Compared with direct neuroevolution on high-dimensional raw observations, it inserts a progressively trained autoencoder representation before CMA-ES controller evolution. TITLE: Autoencoder-augmented neuroevolution for visual doom playing

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Neuroevolution has proven effective at many re-inforcement learning tasks, including tasks with incomplete information and delayed rewards, but does not seem to scale well to high-dimensional controller representations, which are needed for tasks where the input is raw pixel data. We propose a novel method where we train an autoencoder to create a comparatively low-dimensional representation of the environment observation, and then use CMA-ES to train neural network controll

title: Automation of the matrix element reweighting method
searchable_text:
Automation of the matrix element reweighting method Evaluating matrix-element reweighting weights for collider processes in the standard model and beyond. Automatically builds a phase-space mapping from specified particles and transfer functions to integrate squared matrix elements and transfer functions, implemented on MadGraph. Matrix-element reweighting weights become automatically evaluable for arbitrary processes of interest, rather than requiring manual, process-specific construction. TITLE: Automation of the matrix element reweighting method

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Matrix element reweighting is a powerful experimental technique widely employed to maximize the amount of information that can be extracted from a collider data set. We present a procedure that allows to automatically evaluate the weights for any process of interest in the standard model and beyond. Given the initial, intermediate and final state particles, and the transfer functions for the final physics objects, such as leptons, jets, missing transverse energy, our algorithm create

title: Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks
searchable_text:
Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks Attributing deep-network predictions to input features across image, text, and chemistry models. Integrated Gradients attributes predictions using axioms, requiring no network modification and only a few calls to the standard gradient operator. Compared with prior attribution methods, it introduces an axiom-guided method, Integrated Gradients, instead of methods that fail Sensitivity and Implementation Invariance. TITLE: Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We study the problem of attributing the prediction of a deep network to its input features, a problem previously studied by several other works. We identify two fundamental axioms— Sensitivity and Implementation Invariance that attribution methods ought to satisfy. We show that they are not satisfied by most known attribution methods, which we consider to be a fundamental weakness of those methods. We use the axioms to guide the design of a new attribution method called Integrated Gradients. Our

title: Batch Policy Gradient Methods for Improving Neural Conversation Models
searchable_text:
Batch Policy Gradient Methods for Improving Neural Conversation Models Improving neural conversation models for chatbot settings where human quality rewards are noisy, expensive, and time consuming to obtain. An off-policy batch policy gradient method for reinforcement learning of recurrent neural-network chatbots under costly, noisy reward feedback. Replaces prior on-policy and/or online NLP reinforcement learning updates with an off-policy batch policy gradient method. TITLE: Batch Policy Gradient Methods for Improving Neural Conversation Models

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We study reinforcement learning of chatbots with recurrent neural network architectures when the rewards are noisy and expensive to obtain. For instance, a chatbot used in automated customer service support can be scored by quality assurance agents, but this process can be expensive, time consuming and noisy. Previous reinforcement learning work for natural language processing uses on-policy updates and/or is designed for on-line learning settings. We demonstrate e

title: Batch Renormalization: Towards Reducing Minibatch Dependence in Batch-Normalized Models
searchable_text:
Batch Renormalization: Towards Reducing Minibatch Dependence in Batch-Normalized Models Training deep models using Batch Normalization when minibatches are small or contain non-independent samples. Extend Batch Normalization so training and inference outputs match and depend on individual examples rather than the whole minibatch. Compared with Batch Normalization, Batch Renormalization reduces minibatch dependence by aligning training and inference behavior around individual-example outputs. TITLE: Batch Renormalization: Towards Reducing Minibatch Dependence in Batch-Normalized Models

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Batch Normalization is quite effective at accelerating and improving the training of deep models. However, its effectiveness diminishes when the training minibatches are small, or do not consist of independent samples. We hypothesize that this is due to the dependence of model layer inputs on all the examples in the minibatch, and different activations being produced between training and inference. We propose Batch Renormalization, a simple and eff

title: Bayes' Bluff: Opponent Modelling in Poker
searchable_text:
Bayes' Bluff: Opponent Modelling in Poker Opponent modelling and response selection in poker games with non-deterministic dynamics, partial observability, and unknown adversaries. A Bayesian probabilistic poker model separates game-dynamics uncertainty from opponent-strategy uncertainty, infers posteriors over strategies, and selects responses to that distribution. It explicitly separates uncertainty in game dynamics from uncertainty in the opponent's strategy within a Bayesian poker model. TITLE: Bayes' Bluff: Opponent Modelling in Poker

YEAR: 2005

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Poker is a challenging problem for artificial intelligence, with non-deterministic dynamics, partial observability, and the added difficulty of unknown adversaries. Modelling all of the uncertainties in this domain is not an easy task. In this paper we present a Bayesian probabilistic model for a broad class of poker games, separating the uncertainty in the game dynamics from the uncertainty of the opponent's strategy. We then describe approaches to two key sub-problems: (i) inferring a poster

title: Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization for Ensemble Learning
searchable_text:
Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization for Ensemble Learning Hyperparameter optimization for ensemble learning, targeting ensembles of classifiers across multiple hyperparameter spaces. Build a fixed-size ensemble and use Bayesian optimization to tune one classifier configuration per iteration while evaluating interactions with the other ensemble members. Instead of optimizing models independently then ensembling, Bayesian optimization directly evaluates candidate classifier configurations in the context of their ensemble interactions. TITLE: Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization for Ensemble Learning

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we bridge the gap between hyperparameter optimization and ensemble learning by performing Bayesian optimization of an ensemble with regards to its hyperparameters. Our method consists in building a fixed-size ensemble, optimizing the configuration of one classifier of the ensemble at each iteration of the hyperparameter optimization algorithm, taking into consideration the interaction with the other models when evaluating potential performances. We also consider

title: Bayesian optimization with safety constraints: safe and automatic parameter tuning in robotics
searchable_text:
Bayesian optimization with safety constraints: safe and automatic parameter tuning in robotics Automatic tuning of algorithm parameters on real-world robotic systems where unsafe evaluations can cause safety-critical failures or damage. Safe Bayesian optimization using Gaussian process regularity assumptions to explore only parameters satisfying multiple independent safety constraints with high probability, starting from an initial safe set. Generalizes SafeOpt by separating safety constraints from the performance objective and allowing multiple safety constraints plus contextual transfer. TITLE: Bayesian optimization with safety constraints: safe and automatic parameter tuning in robotics

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Selecting the right tuning parameters for algorithms is a pravelent problem in machine learning that can significantly affect the performance of algorithms. Data-efficient optimization algorithms, such as Bayesian optimization, have been used to automate this process. During experiments on real-world systems such as robotic platforms these methods can evaluate unsafe parameters that lead to safety-critical system failures and can destroy the

title: Bayesian Reinforcement Learning: A Survey
searchable_text:
Bayesian Reinforcement Learning: A Survey Reinforcement learning settings including single-step Bandit models, model-based RL, and model-free RL. Use Bayesian inference and priors over bandit, Markov-model parameters, value functions, or policy classes to guide RL learning and action selection. Consolidates Bayesian methods across bandits, model-based RL, and model-free RL into a comprehensive survey of algorithms and properties. TITLE: Bayesian Reinforcement Learning: A Survey

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Bayesian methods for machine learning have been widely investigated, yielding principled methods for incorporating prior information into inference algorithms. In this survey, we provide an in-depth review of the role of Bayesian methods for the reinforcement learning (RL) paradigm. The major incentives for incorporating Bayesian reasoning in RL are: 1) it provides an elegant approach to action-selection (exploration/exploitation) as a function of the uncertainty in learning; and 2) it provide

title: Benchmarking Deep Reinforcement Learning for Continuous Control
searchable_text:
Benchmarking Deep Reinforcement Learning for Continuous Control Benchmarking deep reinforcement learning progress on continuous-control tasks spanning cart-pole swing-up, high-dimensional 3D humanoid locomotion, partial observations, and hierarchical structure. A released benchmark suite of diverse continuous-control tasks plus reference implementations for systematic evaluation of implemented reinforcement-learning algorithms. Introduced and released a common continuous-control benchmark suite and reference implementations where prior work lacked an adopted benchmark. TITLE: Benchmarking Deep Reinforcement Learning for Continuous Control

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Recently, researchers have made significant progress combining the advances in deep learning for learning feature representations with reinforcement learning. Some notable examples include training agents to play Atari games based on raw pixel data and to acquire advanced manipulation skills using raw sensory inputs. However, it has been difficult to quantify progress in the domain of continuous control due to the lack of a commonly adopted benchmark. In this work, we pre

title: Better Mini-Batch Algorithms via Accelerated Gradient Methods
searchable_text:
Better Mini-Batch Algorithms via Accelerated Gradient Methods Speeding up mini-batch algorithms for stochastic convex optimization problems. Apply accelerated gradient methods to mini-batch stochastic convex optimization, with a novel analysis and accelerated algorithm. Replaces standard gradient mini-batch analysis with a novel accelerated gradient algorithm designed to overcome insufficient speed-up. TITLE: Better Mini-Batch Algorithms via Accelerated Gradient Methods

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Mini-batch algorithms have been proposed as a way to speed-up stochastic convex optimization problems. We study how such algorithms can be improved using accelerated gradient methods. We provide a novel analysis, which shows how standard gradient methods may sometimes be insufficient to obtain a significant speed-up and propose a novel accelerated gradient algorithm, which deals with this deficiency, enjoys a uniformly superior guarantee and works well in practice.

title: Beyond Convexity: Stochastic Quasi-Convex Optimization
searchable_text:
Beyond Convexity: Stochastic Quasi-Convex Optimization Stochastic optimization for quasi-convex, locally-Lipschitz functions beyond standard convex Lipschitz objectives. Stochastic Normalized Gradient Descent updates using gradient directions rather than raw gradient magnitudes. Replaces vanilla SGD on convex Lipschitz functions with stochastic NGD that converges for quasi-convex, locally-Lipschitz functions. TITLE: Beyond Convexity: Stochastic Quasi-Convex Optimization

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Stochastic convex optimization is a basic and well studied primitive in machine learning. It is well known that convex and Lipschitz functions can be minimized efficiently using Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD). 
 
The Normalized Gradient Descent (NGD) algorithm, is an adaptation of Gradient Descent, which updates according to the direction of the gradients, rather than the gradients themselves. In this paper we analyze a stochastic version of NGD and prove its convergence to a g

title: Beyond linear galaxy alignments
searchable_text:
Beyond linear galaxy alignments Modeling galaxy intrinsic alignments as a systematic uncertainty in current and future weak-lensing and combined-probes cosmology analyses. Perturbative IA expansion analogous to galaxy biasing, including second-order density/tidal terms and one-loop two-point statistics for tidal alignment, tidal torquing, and their cross-correlation. Extends beyond the standard NLA linear-alignment treatment by adding second-order density/tidal IA operators, tidal-torquing contributions, cross-correlations, and one-loop two-point predictions. TITLE: Beyond linear galaxy alignments

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Galaxy intrinsic alignments (IA) are a critical uncertainty for current and future weak lensing measurements. We describe a perturbative expansion of IA, analogous to the treatment of galaxy biasing. From an astrophysical perspective, this model includes the expected large-scale alignment mechanisms for galaxies that are pressure-supported (tidal alignment) and rotation-supported (tidal torquing) as well as the cross-correlation between the two. Alternatively, this expansion can be viewed as an effectiv

title: Bifurcation study of a neural field competition model with an application to perceptual switching in motion integration
searchable_text:
Bifurcation study of a neural field competition model with an application to perceptual switching in motion integration Perceptual switching during ambiguous multistable motion perception, specifically the barberpole illusion, with attention to contrast-dependent switching behavior. A neural field competition model represents competing perceptual states in continuous feature space and uses bifurcation analysis to characterize spatio-temporal dynamics across parameters and inputs.  TITLE: Bifurcation study of a neural field competition model with an application to perceptual switching in motion integration

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Perceptual multistability is a phenomenon in which alternate interpretations of a fixed stimulus are perceived intermittently. Although correlates between activity in specific cortical areas and perception have been found, the complex patterns of activity and the underlying mechanisms that gate multistable perception are little understood. Here, we present a neural field competition model in which competing states are represen

title: Billion-Scale Similarity Search with GPUs
searchable_text:
Billion-Scale Similarity Search with GPUs Billion-scale nearest-neighbor similarity search over high-dimensional image/video feature vectors on GPUs. A novel GPU k-selection design applied to brute-force, approximate, and product-quantized compressed-domain similarity search. Replaced prior GPU bottlenecks in k-min selection and memory hierarchy usage with a new k-selection design optimized across multiple search settings. TITLE: Billion-Scale Similarity Search with GPUs

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Similarity search finds application in database systems handling complex data such as images or videos, which are typically represented by high-dimensional features and require specific indexing structures. This paper tackles the problem of better utilizing GPUs for this task. While GPUs excel at data parallel tasks such as distance computation, prior approaches in this domain are bottlenecked by algorithms that expose less parallelism, such as <inline-formula><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</t

title: Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Implications for Advanced LIGO.
searchable_text:
Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Implications for Advanced LIGO. Predicting Advanced LIGO detection rates and source characteristics for binary black hole mergers originating in globular clusters. Model black hole binary formation through well-understood gravitational interactions in dense globular clusters, using an extensive collection of realistic cluster models compared with observed clusters. Shifted the merger-rate focus from uncertain galactic-field stellar-evolution channels to dynamically formed binaries in realistic globular cluster models. TITLE: Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Implications for Advanced LIGO.

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The predicted rate of binary black hole mergers from galactic fields can vary over several orders of magnitude and is extremely sensitive to the assumptions of stellar evolution. But in dense stellar environments such as globular clusters, binary black holes form by well-understood gravitational interactions. In this Letter, we study the formation of black hole binaries in an extensive collection of realistic globular cluster models. By comparing these m

title: Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Masses, Merger Rates, and the Impact of Stellar Evolution
searchable_text:
Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Masses, Merger Rates, and the Impact of Stellar Evolution Predicting masses, mass ratios, redshift distribution, and local merger rates of binary black holes formed in globular clusters. Monte Carlo globular-cluster simulations incorporating stellar dynamics, binary formation, encounters, and updated metallicity-dependent stellar wind/supernova prescriptions to model binary black hole formation and mergers. Expands Rodriguez et al. 2015 with 52 cluster models spanning mass, metallicity, and radius plus updated metallicity-dependent stellar wind and supernova prescriptions. TITLE: Binary Black Hole Mergers from Globular Clusters: Masses, Merger Rates, and the Impact of Stellar Evolution

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The recent discovery of GW150914, the binary black hole merger detected by Advanced LIGO, has the potential to revolutionize observational astrophysics. But to fully utilize this new window into the universe, we must compare these new observations to detailed models of binary black hole formation throughout cosmic time. Expanding upon our previous work (Rodriguez et al., 2015), we study merging binary black holes formed in globu

title: Binary Black Hole Mergers in the First Advanced LIGO Observing Run
searchable_text:
Binary Black Hole Mergers in the First Advanced LIGO Observing Run Detect and characterize gravitational waves from stellar-mass binary black hole mergers in the first Advanced LIGO observing run. Search Advanced LIGO O1 data using general-relativistic gravitational-wave models of binary black hole mergers with total masses up to 100M⊙. Advanced LIGO moved the field from no direct gravitational-wave detections of binary black hole mergers to the first observed events and rate constraints. In this paper we present full results from a search for binary black hole merger signals with total masses up to 100M⊙ and detailed implications from our observations of these systems. title: BinaryConnect: Training Deep Neural Networks with binary weights during propagations
searchable_text:
BinaryConnect: Training Deep Neural Networks with binary weights during propagations Efficient deep neural network training and inference for specialized hardware and low-power devices, evaluated on permutation-invariant MNIST, CIFAR-10, and SVHN. Train DNNs using binarized weights in forward and backward propagations, while keeping high-precision stored weights for gradient accumulation. Instead of using full-precision weights during propagations, BinaryConnect constrains propagated weights to two values while retaining precise accumulated weights. TITLE: BinaryConnect: Training Deep Neural Networks with binary weights during propagations

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep Neural Networks (DNN) have achieved state-of-the-art results in a wide range of tasks, with the best results obtained with large training sets and large models. In the past, GPUs enabled these breakthroughs because of their greater computational speed. In the future, faster computation at both training and test time is likely to be crucial for further progress and for consumer applications on low-power devices. As a result, there is much interest

title: Black-box hamiltonian simulation and unitary implementation
searchable_text:
Black-box hamiltonian simulation and unitary implementation Simulation of sparse black-box Hamiltonians and implementation of arbitrary black-box unitary operations from matrix-element oracle descriptions. Use quantum-walk-based methods to simulate black-box Hamiltonians and implement black-box unitary operations. DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present general methods for simulating black-box Hamiltonians using quantum walks. These techniques have two main applications: simulating sparse Hamiltonians and implementing black-box unitary operations. In particular, we give the best known simulation of sparse Hamiltonians with constant precision. title: BLACK HOLE MERGERS AND BLUE STRAGGLERS FROM HIERARCHICAL TRIPLES FORMED IN GLOBULAR CLUSTERS
searchable_text:
BLACK HOLE MERGERS AND BLUE STRAGGLERS FROM HIERARCHICAL TRIPLES FORMED IN GLOBULAR CLUSTERS Production of Advanced LIGO-detectable black-hole mergers and blue straggler stars from hierarchical triples dynamically formed in globular clusters. Identify dynamically formed triples in Monte Carlo dense-cluster models and integrate their three-body evolution with relativistic/tidal effects to evaluate LK-driven inner-binary collisions/mergers. Moves beyond standard secular perturbation predictions by combining cluster Monte Carlo triple formation with direct three-body integrations including relativistic and tidal effects. Hierarchical triple-star systems are expected to form frequently via close binary–binary encounters in the dense cores of globular clusters (GCs). title: BLENDER: Enabling Local Search with a Hybrid Differential Privacy Model
searchable_text:
BLENDER: Enabling Local Search with a Hybrid Differential Privacy Model Privately computing the head of search logs from large search click data sets. A hybrid differential privacy model combines local-private regular users with trusted-curator opt-in users, using a blended algorithm to improve data utility while preserving each group’s desired privacy guarantees. Instead of relying solely on local privacy or trusted curator privacy, BLENDER blends regular and opt-in users under a hybrid differential privacy model. TITLE: BLENDER: Enabling Local Search with a Hybrid Differential Privacy Model

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a hybrid model of differential privacy that considers a combination of regular and opt-in users who desire the differential privacy guarantees of the local privacy model and the trusted curator model, respectively. We demonstrate that within this model, it is possible to design a new type of blended algorithm that improves the utility of obtained data, while providing users with their desired privacy guarantees.

title: Boosted objects: a probe of beyond the standard model physics
searchable_text:
Boosted objects: a probe of beyond the standard model physics Discovery-oriented analysis of hadronic decays of highly boosted particles at the LHC, especially as probes of beyond-standard-model physics. Use jet substructure methods, including jet grooming and top-tagging algorithms, to reconstruct and isolate hadronic decays of highly boosted particles. Adds new common-benchmark comparisons of jet grooming techniques and top tagging algorithms, plus a study of Monte Carlo uncertainty sensitivity. TITLE: Boosted objects: a probe of beyond the standard model physics

title: Boosted objects and jet substructure at the LHC. Report of BOOST2012, held at IFIC Valencia, 23rd–27th of July 2012

Boosted objects and jet substructure at the LHC. Report of BOOST2012, held at IFIC Valencia, 23rd–27th of July 2012 Jet substructure for boosted objects at the LHC, including QCD description, Monte Carlo accuracy, experimental limitations, pile-up effects, and boosted top-quark new-physics searches. title: Boosting the Actor with Dual Critic
searchable_text:
Boosting the Actor with Dual Critic Actor-critic reinforcement learning algorithms for benchmark control tasks. Derive Dual Actor-Critic from the Lagrangian dual Bellman optimality equation, framing learning as a minimax actor–dual-critic game with cooperative updates on one objective. Unlike prior actor-critic methods, the actor and dual critic are updated cooperatively to optimize the same objective function. TITLE: Boosting the Actor with Dual Critic

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper proposes a new actor-critic-style algorithm called Dual Actor-Critic or Dual-AC. It is derived in a principled way from the Lagrangian dual form of the Bellman optimality equation, which can be viewed as a two-player game between the actor and a critic-like function, which is named as dual critic. title: Bootstrapping with Models: Confidence Intervals for Off-Policy Evaluation
searchable_text:
Bootstrapping with Models: Confidence Intervals for Off-Policy Evaluation Determining confidence interval lower bounds on a given policy’s performance without executing that policy in reinforcement learning applications. Two bootstrapping off-policy evaluation methods use learned MDP transition models to estimate lower confidence bounds on policy performance from limited data. Introduces bootstrapping off-policy evaluation methods that incorporate learned MDP transition models for confidence-bound estimation. TITLE: Bootstrapping with Models: Confidence Intervals for Off-Policy Evaluation

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 In many reinforcement learning applications, it is desirable to determine confidence interval lower bounds on the performance of any given policy without executing said policy. In this context, we propose two bootstrapping off-policy evaluation methods which use learned MDP transition models in order to estimate lower confidence bounds on policy performance with limited data. We empirically evaluate the proposed methods in a standard policy evaluation tasks.

title: Bottom-Up and Top-Down Attention for Image Captioning and Visual Question Answering
searchable_text:
Bottom-Up and Top-Down Attention for Image Captioning and Visual Question Answering Image captioning and visual question answering requiring fine-grained visual attention and deeper image understanding. Combined bottom-up and top-down attention: Faster R-CNN proposes object/salient regions with feature vectors, while top-down attention determines feature weightings. Changed attention from purely top-down visual attention to object/salient-region attention using bottom-up region proposals plus top-down weighting. TITLE: Bottom-Up and Top-Down Attention for Image Captioning and Visual Question Answering

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Top-down visual attention mechanisms have been used extensively in image captioning and visual question answering (VQA) to enable deeper image understanding through fine-grained analysis and even multiple steps of reasoning. In this work, we propose a combined bottom-up and top-down attention mechanism that enables attention to be calculated at the level of objects and other salient image regions. title: Breaking the Softmax Bottleneck: A High-Rank RNN Language Model
searchable_text:
Breaking the Softmax Bottleneck: A High-Rank RNN Language Model Neural language modeling for highly context-dependent natural language, evaluated on Penn Treebank and WikiText-2 perplexity. Treat language modeling as matrix factorization and use a high-rank RNN language model to address the Softmax bottleneck. Moves beyond conventional Softmax-based neural language models by explicitly addressing the Softmax bottleneck with a high-rank RNN language model. TITLE: Breaking the Softmax Bottleneck: A High-Rank RNN Language Model

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We formulate language modeling as a matrix factorization problem, and show that the expressiveness of Softmax-based models (including the majority of neural language models) is limited by a Softmax bottleneck. Given that natural language is highly context-dependent, this further implies that in practice Softmax with distributed word embeddings does not have enough capacity to model natural language. We propose a simple and effective method to address this issue, and impro

title: calclens: weak lensing simulations for large-area sky surveys and second-order effects in cosmic shear power spectra
searchable_text:
calclens: weak lensing simulations for large-area sky surveys and second-order effects in cosmic shear power spectra Weak gravitational lensing shear simulation for large-area curved-sky N-body light-cone surveys and synthetic shear catalogs. CALCLENS uses a sphere Poisson solver combining spherical harmonic transforms with multigrid methods, embedded in multiple-plane curved-sky ray tracing to compute redshift-dependent weak-lensing shear. Compared with simpler large-sky lensing calculations, it adds curvature and boundary handling, multiple-plane Born corrections, lensed source images, and an efficient hybrid spherical Poisson solver. TITLE: calclens: weak lensing simulations for large-area sky surveys and second-order effects in cosmic shear power spectra

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
I present a new algorithm, CALCLENS, for efficiently computing weak gravitational lensing shear signals from large N-body light cone simulations over a curved sky. This new algorithm properly accounts for the sky curvature and boundary conditions, is able to produce redshift-dependent shear signals including corrections to the Born approximation by using multiple-plane ray tracing, and properly computes the lensed image

title: Can Decentralized Algorithms Outperform Centralized Algorithms? A Case Study for Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent
searchable_text:
Can Decentralized Algorithms Outperform Centralized Algorithms? A Case Study for Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent Distributed stochastic gradient descent for large-scale machine learning across multi-GPU systems and frameworks such as CNTK and Torch. Decentralized parallel stochastic gradient descent distributes communication across peer nodes instead of relying on a central parameter server bottleneck. Unlike prior D-PSGD analyses that assumed only decentralized networks, this work compares directly to centralized PSGD and identifies when decentralization can outperform it. TITLE: Can Decentralized Algorithms Outperform Centralized Algorithms? A Case Study for Decentralized Parallel Stochastic Gradient Descent

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most distributed machine learning systems nowadays, including TensorFlow and CNTK, are built in a centralized fashion. One bottleneck of centralized algorithms lies on high communication cost on the central node. Motivated by this, we ask, can decentralized algorithms be faster than its centralized counterpart?

title: Can Playing the Computer Game “Tetris” Reduce the Build-Up of Flashbacks for Trauma? A Proposal from Cognitive Science
searchable_text:
Can Playing the Computer Game “Tetris” Reduce the Build-Up of Flashbacks for Trauma? A Proposal from Cognitive Science Preventing the build-up of involuntary PTSD-like flashbacks after exposure to traumatic material. Use a visuospatial task (“Tetris”) soon after trauma to compete for limited mental-imagery resources during memory consolidation, disrupting sensory-perceptual flashback formation. Shifted from treating full-blown PTSD to an early post-trauma “cognitive vaccine” exploiting the 6-hour memory-consolidation window with a simple visuospatial game. TITLE: Can Playing the Computer Game “Tetris” Reduce the Build-Up of Flashbacks for Trauma? A Proposal from Cognitive Science

YEAR: 2009

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Background Flashbacks are the hallmark symptom of Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD). Although we have successful treatments for full-blown PTSD, early interventions are lacking. We propose the utility of developing a ‘cognitive vaccine’ to prevent PTSD flashback development following exposure to trauma. Our theory is based on two key findings: 1) Cognitive science suggests that the brain has selective resources wit

title: CARLA: An Open Urban Driving Simulator
searchable_text:
CARLA: An Open Urban Driving Simulator Development, training, and validation of autonomous urban driving systems in simulated urban environments. An open-source urban driving simulator with open digital assets, configurable sensor suites and environmental conditions, plus metrics for controlled autonomous-driving evaluation. Compared with closed or asset-limited simulators, CARLA adds open-source code, protocols, and freely usable urban layouts, buildings, and vehicles built for autonomous-driving research. TITLE: CARLA: An Open Urban Driving Simulator

We introduce CARLA, an open-source simulator for autonomous driving research. CARLA has been developed from the ground up to support development, training, and validation of autonomous urban driving systems. In addition to open-source code and protocols, CARLA provides open digital assets (urban layouts, buildings, vehicles) that were created for this purpose and can be used freely. The simulation platform supports flexible specification of sensor suites and environmental conditions. We use CARLA

title: Cascade R-CNN: Delving Into High Quality Object Detection

Cascade R-CNN: Delving Into High Quality Object Detection High-quality object detection where positives and negatives depend on IoU thresholds, especially reducing noisy detections and degraded performance at stricter localization criteria. A multi-stage detector cascade trains sequential R-CNN stages with increasing IoU thresholds, resampling improved hypotheses so each stage learns from matched, progressively higher-quality proposals. Replaces a single detector trained at one IoU threshold with a cascade of detectors trained and applied stage-by-stage at increasing IoU thresholds. TITLE: Cascade R-CNN: Delving Into High Quality Object Detection

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

In object detection, an intersection over union (IoU) threshold is required to define positives and negatives. An object detector, trained with low IoU threshold, e.g. 0.5, usually produces noisy detections. However, detection performance tends to degrade with increasing the IoU thresholds. title: Causal inference and causal explanation with background knowledge
searchable_text:
Causal inference and causal explanation with background knowledge Causal inference and causal explanation from observed independence facts in a sample, constrained by background knowledge. Correct algorithms decide existence of causal explanations under background knowledge and derive causal relationships shared by all consistent explanations.  TITLE: Causal inference and causal explanation with background knowledge

YEAR: 1995

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents correct algorithms for answering the following two questions; (i) Does there exist a causal explanation consistent with a set of background knowledge which explains all of the observed independence facts in a sample? Causal Inference in the Presence of Latent Variables and Selection Bias Causal discovery among measured variables when latent variables and selection bias may affect the observed independence structure. title: Cautious Model Predictive Control Using Gaussian Process Regression
searchable_text:
Cautious Model Predictive Control Using Gaussian Process Regression Control of nonlinear dynamical systems from data, including autonomous racing of remote-controlled race cars with fast 20 ms sampling times. Chance-constrained MPC combining a nominal system with additive nonlinear GP-modeled dynamics, using GP residual uncertainty for cautious control. title: Certifying Some Distributional Robustness with Principled Adversarial Training
searchable_text:
Certifying Some Distributional Robustness with Principled Adversarial Training Robust training and certification for neural networks against adversarial input perturbations under distributional robustness. Use a Lagrangian penalty for Wasserstein-ball distributional perturbations, augmenting parameter updates with worst-case perturbations of training data. Replaces heuristic adversarial attacks and defenses with a principled distributionally robust optimization formulation that supports population-loss robustness certification. YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Neural networks are vulnerable to adversarial examples and researchers have proposed many heuristic attack and defense mechanisms. We address this problem through the principled lens of distributionally robust optimization, which guarantees performance under adversarial input perturbations. By considering a Lagrangian penalty formulation of perturbing the underlying data distribution in a Wasserstein ball, we provide a training procedure that augments model

title: Character-level Convolutional Networks for Text Classification
searchable_text:
Character-level Convolutional Networks for Text Classification Text classification on large-scale datasets. Character-level convolutional networks applied directly to text for classification. Shifted text classification from bag-of-words, n-grams, TFIDF, word-based ConvNets, and recurrent neural networks toward character-level ConvNets. TITLE: Character-level Convolutional Networks for Text Classification

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This article offers an empirical exploration on the use of character-level convolutional networks (ConvNets) for text classification. Comparisons are offered against traditional models such as bag of words, n-grams and their TFIDF variants, and deep learning models such as word-based ConvNets and recurrent neural networks.

title: CHARACTERIZING THE COOL KOIs. III. KOI 961: A SMALL STAR WITH LARGE PROPER MOTION AND THREE SMALL PLANETS

CHARACTERIZING THE COOL KOIs. III. title: Charge-insensitive qubit design derived from the Cooper pair box
searchable_text:
Charge-insensitive qubit design derived from the Cooper pair box Reducing charge-noise dephasing in superconducting Cooper pair box-derived qubits while preserving selective control and qubit-photon coupling. Design a superconducting transmon at increased Josephson-to-charging energy ratio, exploiting exponential charge-dispersion suppression while retaining enough anharmonicity. title: CHIME FRB: An application of FFT beamforming for a radio telescope
searchable_text:
CHIME FRB: An application of FFT beamforming for a radio telescope Searching for and localizing Fast Radio Bursts across CHIME’s large instantaneous field-of-view while maintaining full angular resolution. YEAR: 2017

ABSTRACT:

title: Citation Analysis with Neural Attention Models
searchable_text:
Citation Analysis with Neural Attention Models Automated citation analysis for citation classification and sentiment analysis, supporting applications such as author ranking, literature retrieval, extraction, summarization, and question answering. A compositional attention network integrates local and global attention representations using a hierarchical attention mechanism. Introduces a new compositional attention network that integrates local and global attention representations with hierarchical attention. TITLE: Citation Analysis with Neural Attention Models

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

Automated citation analysis (ACA) can be important for many applications including author ranking and literature based information retrieval, extraction, summarization and question answering. title: Classification methods for noise transients in advanced gravitational-wave detectors II: performance tests on Advanced LIGO data
searchable_text:
Classification methods for noise transients in advanced gravitational-wave detectors II: performance tests on Advanced LIGO data Fast, accurate characterization of short-duration non-astrophysical transient noise in advanced LIGO/Virgo gravitational-wave detector data. Apply three transient-noise classifiers—PCAT, PC-LIB, and WDF-ML—with improvements for higher performance and lower latency on real Advanced LIGO data. YEAR: 2015

The data taken by the advanced LIGO and Virgo gravitational-wave detectors contains short duration noise transients that limit the significance of astrophysical detections and reduce the duty cycle of the instruments. title: Climate Change 2014 - Synthesis Report
searchable_text:
Climate Change 2014 - Synthesis Report Comprehensive synthesis of climate change physical science, impacts, adaptation, vulnerability, mitigation, renewable energy, and extreme-event risk. Distils and integrates findings from AR5 Working Group reports and incorporates relevant Special Reports into a unified synthesis. Extends the three AR5 Working Group contributions by incorporating two 2011 Special Reports on renewable energy and extreme-event/disaster risk. TITLE: Climate Change 2014 - Synthesis Report

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

The Synthesis Report (SYR) distils and integrates the findings of the three Working Group contributions to the Fifth Assessment Report (AR5) of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), the most comprehensive assessment of climate change undertaken thus far by the IPCC: Climate Change 2013: The Physical Science Basis; Climate Change 2014: Impacts, Adaptation, and Vulnerability; and Climate Change 2014: Mitigation of Climate Change. title: CNN architectures for large-scale audio classification

CNN architectures for large-scale audio classification Large-scale audio classification of video soundtracks with video-level labels, plus Acoustic Event Detection classification on Audio Set. We examine fully connected Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), AlexNet [1], VGG [2], Inception [3], and ResNet [4]. title: CNN Features Off-the-Shelf: An Astounding Baseline for Recognition
searchable_text:
CNN Features Off-the-Shelf: An Astounding Baseline for Recognition Visual recognition across object classification, scene recognition, fine-grained recognition, attribute detection, and image retrieval on datasets increasingly distant from OverFeat’s original ImageNet classification training task. Use 4096-dimensional features extracted from an off-the-shelf OverFeat CNN trained on ILSVRC13 as generic image representations, with simple augmentation and linear SVM classifiers or L2 distance for retrieval. title: COCO-Stuff: Thing and Stuff Classes in Context
searchable_text:
COCO-Stuff: Thing and Stuff Classes in Context Understanding and analyzing thing and stuff semantic classes in image context for semantic segmentation and contextual scene reasoning. Augment COCO images with pixel-wise stuff annotations using a superpixel-based protocol that leverages existing thing annotations. Semantic classes can be either things (objects with a well-defined shape, e.g. car, person) or stuff (amorphous background regions, e.g. grass, sky).
