Nonetheless, stuff classes are important as they allow to explain important aspects of an image, including (1) scene type; (2) which thing classes are likely to be present and their location (through contextual reasoning);

title: Cognition-Based Networks: A New Perspective on Network Optimization Using Learning and Distributed Intelligence
searchable_text:
Cognition-Based Networks: A New Perspective on Network Optimization Using Learning and Distributed Intelligence Design, operation, management, and optimization of complex, scalable communication and telecommunication networks. Apply advanced machine learning—especially unsupervised deep learning and probabilistic generative models—combined with network virtualization for system-wide learning, modeling, optimization, data representation, and reconfiguration. Relative to past work, COBANETS proposes a more comprehensive interdisciplinary cognition-based framework combining deep probabilistic learning with network virtualization for network optimization. TITLE: Cognition-Based Networks: A New Perspective on Network Optimization Using Learning and Distributed Intelligence

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In response to the new challenges in the design and operation of communication networks, and taking inspiration from how living beings deal with complexity and scalability, in this paper we introduce an innovative system concept called COgnition-BAsed NETworkS (COBANETS). The proposed approach develops around the systematic application of advanced machine learning techniques and, in particular, unsupervised deep learning and

title: Collecting Telemetry Data Privately
searchable_text:
Collecting Telemetry Data Privately Repeated telemetry collection of counter data from user devices, such as daily app usage statistics, for population mean and histogram estimation. Locally differentially private mechanisms for repeated counter collection with formal privacy guarantees over arbitrarily long execution, targeting mean and histogram estimation while matching single-round accuracy. Shifted from single-round LDP telemetry mechanisms to repeated-data-collection mechanisms that retain formal privacy indefinitely with comparable or identical accuracy. TITLE: Collecting Telemetry Data Privately

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The collection and analysis of telemetry data from users' devices is routinely performed by many software companies. Telemetry collection leads to improved user experience but poses significant risks to users' privacy. Locally differentially private (LDP) algorithms have recently emerged as the main tool that allows data collectors to estimate various population statistics, while preserving privacy. The guarantees provided by such algorithms are typically very strong for a single round of telemetry

title: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
searchable_text:
Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data Learning shared deep models from decentralized, privacy-sensitive or large mobile-device data for applications such as language, speech, text entry, and image models. Federated Learning trains deep networks by leaving data on mobile devices and aggregating locally computed updates via iterative model averaging. Instead of logging mobile data to a data center for conventional training, training data remains distributed on devices while a shared model is learned through aggregated local updates. TITLE: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['golden_trace']

ABSTRACT:
Modern mobile devices have access to a wealth of data suitable for learning models, which in turn can greatly improve the user experience on the device. For example, language models can improve speech recognition and text entry, and image models can automatically select good photos. However, this rich data is often privacy sensitive, large in quantity, or both, which may preclude logging to the data center and training there using conventional approaches. We advocat

title: Communication lower bounds for statistical estimation problems via a distributed data processing inequality
searchable_text:
Communication lower bounds for statistical estimation problems via a distributed data processing inequality Distributed high-dimensional statistical estimation, especially sparse Gaussian mean estimation and sparse linear regression with multiple machines communicating to estimate unknown parameters. Proves a distributed data processing inequality to derive communication lower bounds for distributed statistical estimation under message passing. Extends prior lower bounds by covering multi-round iterative communication models, improving on Shamir (NIPS'14) and Steinhardt-Duchi (COLT'15). TITLE: Communication lower bounds for statistical estimation problems via a distributed data processing inequality

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We study the tradeoff between the statistical error and communication cost of distributed statistical estimation problems in high dimensions. In the distributed sparse Gaussian mean estimation problem, each of the m machines receives n data points from a d-dimensional Gaussian distribution with unknown mean θ which is promised to be k-sparse. The machines communicate by message passing and aim to estimate the mean θ. We provide

title: Community detection and stochastic block models: recent developments
searchable_text:
Community detection and stochastic block models: recent developments Fundamental limits for community detection in stochastic block models across exact, partial, and weak recovery, plus SBM parameter learning and computational-statistical gaps. Use the stochastic block model as a planted-cluster random graph framework to analyze community-detection thresholds and algorithms such as graph-splitting, SDP, belief propagation, and spectral methods. Compared with earlier SBM work, this consolidates recent threshold characterizations for multiple recovery regimes and algorithms designed to approach those limits. TITLE: Community detection and stochastic block models: recent developments

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The stochastic block model (SBM) is a random graph model with planted clusters. It is widely employed as a canonical model to study clustering and community detection, and provides generally a fertile ground to study the statistical and computational tradeoffs that arise in network and data sciences. 
 
This note surveys the recent developments that establish the fundamental limits for community detection in the SBM, both with respect to information-theoretic and com

title: Complete waveform model for compact binaries on eccentric orbits
searchable_text:
Complete waveform model for compact binaries on eccentric orbits Gravitational-wave waveforms for compact, nonspinning binaries on low-to-moderate eccentric orbits, spanning inspiral, merger, and ringdown. Time-domain inspiral-merger-ringdown model combining 3PN eccentric inspiral dynamics/radiation, higher-order quasicircular flux and self-force energy corrections, with an analytical merger-ringdown prescription informed by numerical-relativity simulations. Extends zero-eccentricity TaylorT4/quasicircular IMR modeling to eccentric binaries by adding 3PN eccentric dynamics/radiation, memory and tail terms, flux/self-force corrections, and an analytical NR-informed merger-ringdown prescription. TITLE: Complete waveform model for compact binaries on eccentric orbits

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a time domain waveform model that describes the inspiral, merger and ringdown of compact binary systems whose components are nonspinning, and which evolve on orbits with low to moderate eccentricity. The inspiral evolution is described using third-order post-Newtonian equations both for the equations of motion of the binary, and its far-zone radiation field. This latter component also includes instantaneous, tails and tails-of-tails contributions, and a contri

title: Complex dynamics in recurrent cortical networks based on spatially realistic connectivities
searchable_text:
Complex dynamics in recurrent cortical networks based on spatially realistic connectivities Idle dynamics of recurrent cortical networks with spatially realistic local and long-range connectivity. 2D conductance-based integrate-and-fire cortical network simulations with enlarged 25 mm2 spatial extent and connectivities ranging from random to distance-dependent local/remote patchy projections. Replaces typical 1 mm2 random or neighborhood-coupled models with a 25 mm2 setup enabling long-range and patchy spatially clustered synapses. TITLE: Complex dynamics in recurrent cortical networks based on spatially realistic connectivities

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Most studies on the dynamics of recurrent cortical networks are either based on purely random wiring or neighborhood couplings. Neuronal cortical connectivity, however, shows a complex spatial pattern composed of local and remote patchy connections. We ask to what extent such geometric traits influence the “idle” dynamics of two-dimensional (2d) cortical network models composed of conductance-based integrate-and-fire (iaf) neurons. In contrast to the typ

title: Computational Complexity and Simulation of Rare Events of Ising Spin Glasses
searchable_text:
Computational Complexity and Simulation of Rare Events of Ising Spin Glasses Random 2D Ising spin glasses as highly multimodal constraint satisfaction and black-box optimization problems, including ±J, Gaussian, and intermediate spin-glass cases. Use a hybrid optimizer combining hierarchical Bayesian optimization with deterministic bit-flip hill climbing, then characterize global and local search effort via Frechet extremal-value distributions. Moves beyond purely local or mutation-based search by using recombination-based hBOA with local search and rare-event distributional analysis. TITLE: Computational Complexity and Simulation of Rare Events of Ising Spin Glasses

YEAR: 2004

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We discuss the computational complexity of random 2D Ising spin glasses, which represent an interesting class of constraint satisfaction problems for black box optimization. Two extremal cases are considered: (1) the ± J spin glass, and (2) the Gaussian spin glass. We also study a smooth transition between these two extremal cases. The computational complexity of all studied spin glass systems is found to be dominated by rare events of extremely hard spin gla

title: Computational methods for martingale optimal transport problems
searchable_text:
Computational methods for martingale optimal transport problems Numerical solution of martingale optimal transport problems, especially one-dimensional cases and settings with partially specified marginal distributions. Approximate MOT via discretised marginal distributions, relaxed martingale constraints, linear programming, entropic regularisation, iterative Bregman projection, and dual unconstrained minimisation using concave envelopes. Extends classical optimal transport computation by adding a martingale constraint on transport dynamics and designing discretisation/relaxation schemes to solve the resulting MOT problem. TITLE: Computational methods for martingale optimal transport problems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We establish numerical methods for solving the martingale optimal transport problem (MOT) - a version of the classical optimal transport with an additional martingale constraint on transport's dynamics. We prove that the MOT value can be approximated using linear programming (LP) problems which result from a discretisation of the marginal distributions combined with a suitable relaxation of the martingale constraint. Specialising to dimension one, we provide bounds on the

title: Computational models of epilepsy.
searchable_text:
Computational models of epilepsy. Medically refractory epilepsy: understanding drug-resistant spontaneous seizures and developing alternative effective treatment strategies. Use computational models grounded in dynamical-systems concepts, including multi-stability and bifurcations, to study seizure emergence and support treatment-protocol development. Reframes computational epilepsy modeling through epilepsy as a dynamical brain disease primarily characterized by unprovoked spontaneous seizures. TITLE: Computational models of epilepsy.

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
PURPOSE
Approximately 30% of epilepsy patients suffer from medically refractory epilepsy, in which seizures can not controlled by the use of anti-epileptic drugs (AEDs). Understanding the mechanisms underlying these forms of drug-resistant epileptic seizures and the development of alternative effective treatment strategies are fundamental challenges for modern epilepsy research. In this context, computational modeling has gained prominence as an important tool for tackling the complexity of the epileptic phenom

title: Computer Vision for Autonomous Vehicles: Problems, Datasets and State-of-the-Art
searchable_text:
Computer Vision for Autonomous Vehicles: Problems, Datasets and State-of-the-Art Computer vision problems, datasets, and methods for autonomous vehicles. Comprehensive survey of autonomous-driving computer vision datasets, techniques, literature, benchmark performance, open problems, and navigable topic/method resources. Moves beyond surveys of particular sub-problems to a comprehensive treatment of problems, datasets, and methods. TITLE: Computer Vision for Autonomous Vehicles: Problems, Datasets and State-of-the-Art

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Recent years have witnessed enormous progress in AI-related fields such as computer vision, machine learning, and autonomous vehicles. As with any rapidly growing field, it becomes increasingly difficult to stay up-to-date or enter the field as a beginner. While several survey papers on particular sub-problems have appeared, no comprehensive survey on problems, datasets, and methods in computer vision for autonomous vehicles has been published. This book

title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
searchable_text:
Concrete Problems in AI Safety Accident risk in real-world machine learning systems: unintended and harmful behavior from poor AI system design. Categorize AI accident risks into five practical research problems by source: wrong objective, costly objective evaluation, or unsafe learning behavior. It reframes prior AI safety concerns as a concrete agenda of five practical research problems relevant to cutting-edge AI systems. TITLE: Concrete Problems in AI Safety

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Rapid progress in machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) has brought increasing attention to the potential impacts of AI technologies on society. In this paper we discuss one such potential impact: the problem of accidents in machine learning systems, defined as unintended and harmful behavior that may emerge from poor design of real-world AI systems. We present a list of five practical research problems related to accident risk, categorized according to whether the problem originates from hav

title: Connectivity Learning in Multi-Branch Networks
searchable_text:
Connectivity Learning in Multi-Branch Networks Multi-class image classification with convolutional multi-branch networks. Learn multi-branch network connectivity jointly with weights by optimizing a single end-task loss, rather than fixing branch inputs and aggregation structure by design. Replaces predefined multi-branch connectivity choices used in prior work with connectivity learned simultaneously with network weights. TITLE: Connectivity Learning in Multi-Branch Networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
While much of the work in the design of convolutional networks over the last five years has revolved around the empirical investigation of the importance of depth, filter sizes, and number of feature channels, recent studies have shown that branching, i.e., splitting the computation along parallel but distinct threads and then aggregating their outputs, represents a new promising dimension for significant improvements in performance. To combat the complexity of design choices in multi-bra

title: Consistent On-Line Off-Policy Evaluation
searchable_text:
Consistent On-Line Off-Policy Evaluation Online off-policy evaluation for temporal-difference reinforcement learning with function approximation. Consistent Off-Policy TD corrects stationary-distribution mismatch between behavior and target policies to reduce function-approximation bias and match the target-policy on-policy TD value. Unlike most TD-based OPE methods, it explicitly accounts for behavior-target stationary distribution discrepancy and its convergence-limit bias. TITLE: Consistent On-Line Off-Policy Evaluation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The problem of on-line off-policy evaluation (OPE) has been actively studied in the last decade due to its importance both as a stand-alone problem and as a module in a policy improvement scheme. However, most Temporal Difference (TD) based solutions ignore the discrepancy between the stationary distribution of the behavior and target policies and its effect on the convergence limit when function approximation is applied. In this paper we propose the Consistent Off-Policy Temporal Difference (C

title: Contextual Dueling Bandits
searchable_text:
Contextual Dueling Bandits Learning policies for choosing actions with contextual information when feedback is limited to relative pairwise comparisons. Introduce a von Neumann winner for contextual dueling bandits, plus efficient online and oracle-based algorithms to learn or approximate randomized policies from pairwise comparison feedback. Extends Yue et al.'s dueling-bandits framework to incorporate context and replaces the usual Condorcet winner target with a game-theoretic von Neumann winner. TITLE: Contextual Dueling Bandits

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider the problem of learning to choose actions using contextual information when provided with limited feedback in the form of relative pairwise comparisons. We study this problem in the dueling-bandits framework of Yue et al. (2009), which we extend to incorporate context. Roughly, the learner's goal is to find the best policy, or way of behaving, in some space of policies, although "best" is not always so clearly defined. Here, we propose a new and natural solution concept, rooted in game theory, ca

title: Continuation of Localized Coherent Structures in Nonlocal Neural Field Equations
searchable_text:
Continuation of Localized Coherent Structures in Nonlocal Neural Field Equations Localized activity patterns in neural field equations on the Euclidean plane modeling coarse-grained cortical neuron ensemble activity. Matrix-free Newton--Krylov solvers with numerical continuation applied directly to the integral neural field equation. Continuation moved from PDE-equivalent formulations to the integral equation, enabling kernels without equivalent PDE formulations. TITLE: Continuation of Localized Coherent Structures in Nonlocal Neural Field Equations

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
We study localized activity patterns in neural field equations posed on the Euclidean plane; such models are commonly used to describe the coarse-grained activity of large ensembles of cortical neurons in a spatially continuous way. We employ matrix-free Newton--Krylov solvers and perform numerical continuation of localized patterns directly on the integral form of the equation. This opens up the possibility of studying systems whose synaptic kernel does not lead t

title: Continuous Deep Q-Learning with Model-based Acceleration
searchable_text:
Continuous Deep Q-Learning with Model-based Acceleration Sample-efficient deep reinforcement learning for continuous control tasks, especially simulated robotic control domains and physical systems constrained by sample complexity. Continuous Q-learning via normalized advantage functions with experience replay, augmented by iteratively refitted local linear learned models for model-based acceleration. Replaces commonly used policy gradient and actor-critic approaches with a continuous Q-learning variant, then adds learned local linear models to accelerate model-free reinforcement learning. TITLE: Continuous Deep Q-Learning with Model-based Acceleration

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Model-free reinforcement learning has been successfully applied to a range of challenging problems, and has recently been extended to handle large neural network policies and value functions. However, the sample complexity of modelfree algorithms, particularly when using high-dimensional function approximators, tends to limit their applicability to physical systems. In this paper, we explore algorithms and representations to reduce the sample complexity of deep reinforcement lea

title: Convergence of Entropic Schemes for Optimal Transport and Gradient Flows
searchable_text:
Convergence of Entropic Schemes for Optimal Transport and Gradient Flows Optimal transport with squared Euclidean cost and transportation-distance gradient flows, especially entropic regularized formulations used in imaging, machine learning, and social sciences. Approximate optimal transport by replacing positivity constraints with an entropy barrier, then prove Γ-convergence and convergence of entropic implicit Euler steps as regularization vanishes. Adds rigorous Γ-convergence of entropic optimal transport to Monge-Kantorovich and convergence of entropic implicit-gradient-flow steps, beyond earlier Schrödinger-style entropic approximations and applications. TITLE: Convergence of Entropic Schemes for Optimal Transport and Gradient Flows

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Replacing positivity constraints by an entropy barrier is popular to approximate solutions of linear programs. In the special case of the optimal transport problem, this technique dates back to the early work of Schr\"odinger. This approach has recently been used successfully to solve optimal transport related problems in several applied fields such as imaging sciences, machine learning and social sciences. title: Convergence of Monte Carlo Tree Search in Simultaneous Move Games
searchable_text:
Convergence of Monte Carlo Tree Search in Simultaneous Move Games Zero-sum extensive-form games with perfect information and simultaneous moves. A general MCTS template for simultaneous-move games instantiated with selection methods that are e-Hannan consistent and satisfy exploration requirements. Extends MCTS convergence analysis to simultaneous-move extensive-form games via matrix-game selection-method consistency conditions. TITLE: Convergence of Monte Carlo Tree Search in Simultaneous Move Games

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We study Monte Carlo tree search (MCTS) in zero-sum extensive-form games with perfect information and simultaneous moves. We present a general template of MCTS algorithms for these games, which can be instantiated by various selection methods. We formally prove that if a selection method is e-Hannan consistent in a matrix game and satisfies additional requirements on exploration, then the MCTS algorithm eventually converges to an approximate Nash equilibrium (NE) of the

title: Convergent Learning: Do different neural networks learn the same representations?
searchable_text:
Convergent Learning: Do different neural networks learn the same representations? Understanding whether independently trained neural networks learn individually similar features or shared low-dimensional representational subspaces. Train multiple neural networks and align learned features via bipartite matching, sparse prediction, and spectral clustering to compare neurons or neuron groups. Introduces convergent learning as a representation-comparison framing using three feature-alignment techniques spanning one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many mappings. TITLE: Convergent Learning: Do different neural networks learn the same representations?

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Recent success in training deep neural networks have prompted active investigation into the features learned on their intermediate layers. Such research is difficult because it requires making sense of non-linear computations performed by millions of parameters, but valuable because it increases our ability to understand current models and create improved versions of them. In this paper we investigate the extent to which neural networks exhibit what we c

title: Convolutional LSTM Network: A Machine Learning Approach for Precipitation Nowcasting
searchable_text:
Convolutional LSTM Network: A Machine Learning Approach for Precipitation Nowcasting Precipitation nowcasting: predicting future rainfall intensity in a local region over a relatively short period of time. Extend FC-LSTM with convolutional structures in both input-to-state and state-to-state transitions, yielding ConvLSTM for end-to-end spatiotemporal sequence forecasting. Compared with fully connected LSTM, the change is adding convolutional structures to input-to-state and state-to-state transitions. TITLE: Convolutional LSTM Network: A Machine Learning Approach for Precipitation Nowcasting

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The goal of precipitation nowcasting is to predict the future rainfall intensity in a local region over a relatively short period of time. Very few previous studies have examined this crucial and challenging weather forecasting problem from the machine learning perspective. In this paper, we formulate precipitation nowcasting as a spatiotemporal sequence forecasting problem in which both the input and the prediction target are spatiotemporal sequences

title: Convolutional Neural Fabrics
searchable_text:
Convolutional Neural Fabrics CNN architecture selection for image classification and semantic segmentation tasks. A sparse homogeneous 3D trellis connects response maps across layers, scales, and channels, embedding exponentially many CNN architectures whose overlapping paths share weights and can be trained by back-propagation. Rather than selecting one optimal CNN architecture, the method replaces architecture search with a single fabric embedding exponentially many architectures and ensembling them through shared weights. TITLE: Convolutional Neural Fabrics

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Despite the success of CNNs, selecting the optimal architecture for a given task remains an open problem. Instead of aiming to select a single optimal architecture, we propose a "fabric" that embeds an exponentially large number of architectures. The fabric consists of a 3D trellis that connects response maps at different layers, scales, and channels with a sparse homogeneous local connectivity pattern. The only hyper-parameters of a fabric are the number of channels and layers. While individual architectu

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Noniterative Reconstruction of Compressively Sensed Images
searchable_text:
Convolutional Neural Networks for Noniterative Reconstruction of Compressively Sensed Images Compressive sensing recovery of images, especially block-wise reconstruction at low measurement rates and under measurement or sensor noise. ReconNet is an end-to-end learned deep neural network mapping block-wise compressive measurements to image blocks via a single forward pass. Replaces computationally expensive iterative compressive sensing recovery with a data-driven noniterative CNN; variants add adversarial loss and joint learning of measurement matrix plus reconstruction. TITLE: Convolutional Neural Networks for Noniterative Reconstruction of Compressively Sensed Images

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Traditional algorithms for compressive sensing recovery are computationally expensive and are ineffective at low measurement rates. In this paper, we propose a data-driven noniterative algorithm to overcome the shortcomings of earlier iterative algorithms. Our solution, ReconNet, is a deep neural network, which is learned end-to-end to map block-wise compressive measurements of the scene to the desired image blocks. Reconstruction of an image

title: Coping with qubit leakage in topological codes
searchable_text:
Coping with qubit leakage in topological codes Qubit leakage causing time-correlated errors in topological quantum error correction codes, studied via the repetition code. Add simple leakage-handling quantum circuitry to the error detection cycle of topological error-correction codes. Extends topological-code error-correction analysis from standard computational-state errors to leakage-induced time-correlated errors, adding dedicated circuitry to restore acceptable performance. TITLE: Coping with qubit leakage in topological codes

Without specific methods of dealing with leakage, long-lived leakage can lead to time-correlated errors. We study the impact of such time-correlated errors on topological quantum error correction codes, which are considered highly practical codes, using the repetition code as a representative case study. title: Correcting coherent errors with surface codes
searchable_text:
Correcting coherent errors with surface codes Estimating thresholds and logical error rates for surface-code quantum error correction when physical errors include coherent processes from imperfect control. Large-scale simulation of surface-code quantum error correction protocols under coherent physical noise, comparing coherent dynamics with standard Pauli-noise approximations. Moved beyond toy random Pauli-error simulations to the first large-scale surface-code simulations explicitly including coherent noise. TITLE: Correcting coherent errors with surface codes

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Surface codes are building blocks of quantum computing platforms based on 2D arrays of qubits responsible for detecting and correcting errors. The error suppression achieved by the surface code is usually estimated by simulating toy noise models describing random Pauli errors. However, Pauli noise models fail to capture coherent processes such as systematic unitary errors caused by imperfect control pulses. title: Cosmology constraints from shear peak statistics in Dark Energy Survey Science Verification data
searchable_text:
Cosmology constraints from shear peak statistics in Dark Energy Survey Science Verification data Cosmological parameter constraints from weak gravitational lensing in the 139 deg² Dark Energy Survey Science Verification field. Measure the abundance of peaks in aperture mass maps as a function of signal-to-noise, using DES Science Verification weak-lensing shear data to constrain cosmological parameters. Compared with the closest two-point SV analysis, this uses shear peak abundance in aperture mass maps as a practical alternative and compares resulting constraints to two-point results. TITLE: Cosmology constraints from shear peak statistics in Dark Energy Survey Science Verification data

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Shear peak statistics has gained a lot of attention recently as a practical alternative to the two-point statistics for constraining cosmological parameters. We perform a shear peak statistics analysis of the Dark Energy Survey (DES) Science Verification (SV) data, using weak gravitational lensing measurements from a 139 deg² field. We measure the abundance of peaks identified in aperture mass maps, as a function of their signal-to-noise r

title: Cosmology with the Large Synoptic Survey Telescope: an overview
searchable_text:
Cosmology with the Large Synoptic Survey Telescope: an overview Precision cosmology and dark-energy tests using LSST survey data products across multiple astrophysical probes. Deep-wide-fast six-passband LSST imaging coupled to interlocking cosmological probes: large-scale structure, weak lensing, type Ia supernovae, galaxy clusters, strong lensing, and CMB combinations. Compared with prior survey capability, LSST adds deep-wide-fast imaging, six passbands, 800 main-survey visits, no proprietary period, and unprecedented statistical power for cosmology. TITLE: Cosmology with the Large Synoptic Survey Telescope: an overview

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Large Synoptic Survey Telescope (LSST) is a high étendue imaging facility that is being constructed atop Cerro Pachón in northern Chile. It is scheduled to begin science operations in 2022. With an ( effective) aperture, a novel three-mirror design achieving a seeing-limited field of view, and a 3.2 gigapixel camera, the LSST has the deep-wide-fast imaging capability necessary to carry out an survey in six passbands (ugrizy) to a coadded depth of over 10 years using o

title: Counterfactual Fairness
searchable_text:
Counterfactual Fairness Fair machine-learning decisions in legally or ethically consequential settings such as insurance, lending, hiring, predictive policing, and law-school success prediction. Model fairness with causal-inference tools by requiring predictions to match between the actual world and counterfactual worlds where the individual belongs to a different demographic group. Recasts fairness as an individual-level counterfactual property within a causal-inference framework, rather than relying only on patterns in observed biased data. TITLE: Counterfactual Fairness

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Machine learning can impact people with legal or ethical consequences when it is used to automate decisions in areas such as insurance, lending, hiring, and predictive policing. In many of these scenarios, previous decisions have been made that are unfairly biased against certain subpopulations, for example those of a particular race, gender, or sexual orientation. Since this past data may be biased, machine learning predictors must account for this to avoid perpetuating or creating discriminatory practices. In

title: Counterfactual Multi-Agent Policy Gradients
searchable_text:
Counterfactual Multi-Agent Policy Gradients Learning decentralised policies for cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning, evaluated on partially observable StarCraft unit micromanagement. A multi-agent actor-critic with a centralised Q-function critic, decentralised policy actors, and an efficient counterfactual baseline marginalising one agent's action while fixing others. Compared with prior multi-agent actor-critic methods, COMA adds a counterfactual credit-assignment baseline computed efficiently from a critic representation in one forward pass. TITLE: Counterfactual Multi-Agent Policy Gradients

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 Many real-world problems, such as network packet routing and the coordination of autonomous vehicles, are naturally modelled as cooperative multi-agent systems. There is a great need for new reinforcement learning methods that can efficiently learn decentralised policies for such systems. To this end, we propose a new multi-agent actor-critic method called counterfactual multi-agent (COMA) policy gradients. COMA uses a centralised critic to estimate the Q-function and decentralised actor

title: Counterfactual reasoning and learning systems: the example of computational advertising
searchable_text:
Counterfactual reasoning and learning systems: the example of computational advertising Understanding and improving computational advertising, specifically the ad placement system associated with the Bing search engine. Leverage causal inference for counterfactual reasoning about complex learning systems interacting with their environment, predicting consequences of system changes. Applies causal inference to predict effects of changes in complex learning systems, illustrated in Bing search advertising. TITLE: Counterfactual reasoning and learning systems: the example of computational advertising

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This work shows how to leverage causal inference to understand the behavior of complex learning systems interacting with their environment and predict the consequences of changes to the system. Such predictions allow both humans and algorithms to select the changes that would have improved the system performance. This work is illustrated by experiments on the ad placement system associated with the Bing search engine.

title: Cross-conformal predictors
searchable_text:
Cross-conformal predictors Efficient conformal prediction for settings where full conformal predictors are computationally inefficient and inductive conformal predictors lose informational efficiency. Hybridize inductive conformal prediction with cross-validation to form cross-conformal predictors. Changes inductive conformal prediction by combining it with cross-validation, aiming to reduce training-set sacrifice while retaining computational efficiency. TITLE: Cross-conformal predictors

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Inductive conformal predictors have been designed to overcome the computational inefficiency exhibited by conformal predictors for many underlying prediction algorithms. Whereas computationally efficient, inductive conformal predictors sacrifice different parts of the training set at different stages of prediction, which affects their informational efficiency. This paper introduces the method of cross-conformal prediction, which is a hybrid of the methods of inductive conformal prediction and cross-validatio

title: Crowdsourcing Multiple Choice Science Questions
searchable_text:
Crowdsourcing Multiple Choice Science Questions Crowdsourced generation of high-quality, domain-targeted multiple-choice science exam questions with original, relevant, diverse answer options. Leverage domain-specific text plus existing questions to suggest documents and answer distractors that guide crowd workers in writing multiple-choice science questions. Moved from unaided question generation toward corpus- and model-assisted crowdsourcing for document selection and distractor choice. TITLE: Crowdsourcing Multiple Choice Science Questions

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a novel method for obtaining high-quality, domain-targeted multiple choice questions from crowd workers. Generating these questions can be difficult without trading away originality, relevance or diversity in the answer options. Our method addresses these problems by leveraging a large corpus of domain-specific text and a small set of existing questions. It produces model suggestions for document selection and answer distractor choice which aid the human question generation pr

title: Crystal Graph Convolutional Neural Networks for an Accurate and Interpretable Prediction of Material Properties.
searchable_text:
Crystal Graph Convolutional Neural Networks for an Accurate and Interpretable Prediction of Material Properties. Accurate and interpretable prediction of crystalline material properties across varied crystal structure types and compositions. Crystal graph convolutional neural networks directly learn material properties from atom connections in crystals, yielding a universal and interpretable representation. Replaced manually constructed feature vectors or complex atom-coordinate transformations with direct learning from crystal atom connections. TITLE: Crystal Graph Convolutional Neural Networks for an Accurate and Interpretable Prediction of Material Properties.

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The use of machine learning methods for accelerating the design of crystalline materials usually requires manually constructed feature vectors or complex transformation of atom coordinates to input the crystal structure, which either constrains the model to certain crystal types or makes it difficult to provide chemical insights. Here, we develop a crystal graph convolutional neural networks framework to directly learn mate

title: cuDNN: Efficient Primitives for Deep Learning
searchable_text:
cuDNN: Efficient Primitives for Deep Learning Computational kernels for deep learning workloads on parallel processors, especially within existing deep learning frameworks. A BLAS-like library of optimized GPU routines implementing deep learning primitives for easy integration into frameworks. Introduces an analogous library for deep learning where none existed, replacing repeated hand-optimized kernels with reusable optimized primitives. TITLE: cuDNN: Efficient Primitives for Deep Learning

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a library that provides optimized implementations for deep learning primitives. Deep learning workloads are computationally intensive, and optimizing the kernels of deep learning workloads is difficult and time-consuming. As parallel architectures evolve, kernels must be reoptimized for new processors, which makes maintaining codebases difficult over time. Similar issues have long been addressed in the HPC community by libraries such as the Basic Linear Algebra Subroutines (BLAS

title: Data-Driven Learning of Total and Local Energies in Elemental Boron.
searchable_text:
Data-Driven Learning of Total and Local Energies in Elemental Boron. Structural elucidation, total-energy modeling, and local-energy analysis of elemental boron allotropes. Machine learning combined with random structure searching, alternating quantum-mechanical energy/force calculations, GAP fitting, and GAP-driven RSS to iteratively learn boron's potential-energy surface. Moves from total-energy-focused boron modeling toward an iteratively generated GAP that also yields atom-resolved local energies for complex allotropes. TITLE: Data-Driven Learning of Total and Local Energies in Elemental Boron.

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The allotropes of boron continue to challenge structural elucidation and solid-state theory. Here we use machine learning combined with random structure searching (RSS) algorithms to systematically construct an interatomic potential for boron. Starting from ensembles of randomized atomic configurations, we use alternating single-point quantum-mechanical energy and force computations, Gaussian approximation potential (GAP) fitting, and GAP-driven RSS to iteratively ge

title: Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning with Probabilistic Model Predictive Control
searchable_text:
Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning with Probabilistic Model Predictive Control Data-efficient reinforcement learning for autonomous real-world systems such as robotics that require few environment interactions and must satisfy state-space or control constraints. Learn a GP probabilistic transition model that propagates model uncertainty into long-term predictions, then use MPC to optimize an expected long-term cost control sequence. Compared with interaction-heavy autonomous RL, it introduces probabilistic MPC with GP transition uncertainty for constrained, data-efficient long-term planning. TITLE: Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning with Probabilistic Model Predictive Control

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Trial-and-error based reinforcement learning (RL) has seen rapid advancements in recent times, especially with the advent of deep neural networks. However, the majority of autonomous RL algorithms require a large number of interactions with the environment. A large number of interactions may be impractical in many real-world applications, such as robotics, and many practical systems have to obey limitations in the form of state space or control constrain

title: Deal or No Deal? End-to-End Learning of Negotiation Dialogues
searchable_text:
Deal or No Deal? End-to-End Learning of Negotiation Dialogues Natural-language human-human negotiations in semi-cooperative multi-issue bargaining, where agents with hidden reward functions must reach an agreement or deal. End-to-end negotiation dialogue models learn linguistic and reasoning skills without annotated dialogue states, using dialogue rollouts to plan via simulated conversation continuations. Introduced the first end-to-end negotiation dialogue models and added dialogue rollouts for planning ahead through simulated complete continuations. TITLE: Deal or No Deal? End-to-End Learning of Negotiation Dialogues

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Much of human dialogue occurs in semi-cooperative settings, where agents with different goals attempt to agree on common decisions. Negotiations require complex communication and reasoning skills, but success is easy to measure, making this an interesting task for AI. We gather a large dataset of human-human negotiations on a multi-issue bargaining task, where agents who cannot observe each other’s reward functions must reach an agreement (or a deal) via natural language di

title: Decentralized Collaborative Learning of Personalized Models over Networks
searchable_text:
Decentralized Collaborative Learning of Personalized Models over Networks Personalized model learning for agents in collaborative peer-to-peer networks with heterogeneous local learning objectives. Two asynchronous decentralized gossip algorithms: confidence-aware smoothing of pretrained local models and ADMM-based joint model learning/propagation using local data and neighbors. Extends label-propagation-inspired smoothing to personalized models by accounting for confidence in initial models and adds decentralized ADMM for joint learning and propagation. TITLE: Decentralized Collaborative Learning of Personalized Models over Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider a set of learning agents in a col-laborative peer-to-peer network, where each agent learns a personalized model according to its own learning objective. The question addressed in this paper is: how can agents improve upon their locally trained model by communicating with other agents that have similar objectives? We introduce and analyze two asynchronous gossip algorithms running in a fully decentralized manner. Our first approach , inspired from lab

title: Decentralized control of Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes using belief space macro-actions
searchable_text:
Decentralized control of Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes using belief space macro-actions Multi-robot planning and coordination in continuous spaces under partial observability and uncertainty. Extends Dec-POMDPs to Dec-POSMDPs and solves them using closed-loop belief space macro-actions that support asynchronous decentralized planning. Moves from intractable Dec-POMDP solving toward Dec-POSMDPs with asynchronous robot decisions and automatically constructed robust belief-space macro-actions. TITLE: Decentralized control of Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes using belief space macro-actions

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The focus of this paper is on solving multi-robot planning problems in continuous spaces with partial observability. Decentralized Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes (Dec-POMDPs) are general models for multi-robot coordination problems, but representing and solving Dec-POMDPs is often intractable for large problems. To allow for a high-level representation that is natural for multi-robot problems and scalable to large d

title: Decoupled Neural Interfaces using Synthetic Gradients
searchable_text:
Decoupled Neural Interfaces using Synthetic Gradients Training directed neural networks whose layers or modules are locked by forward propagation and backpropagation dependencies. Train auxiliary models to predict future error gradients from local information, using synthetic gradients instead of true backpropagated gradients so modules can update independently and asynchronously. Replaced waiting for true backpropagated error gradients with locally predicted synthetic gradients, breaking update locking across subgraphs. TITLE: Decoupled Neural Interfaces using Synthetic Gradients

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Training directed neural networks typically requires forward-propagating data through a computation graph, followed by backpropagating error signal, to produce weight updates. All layers, or more generally, modules, of the network are therefore locked, in the sense that they must wait for the remainder of the network to execute forwards and propagate error backwards before they can be updated. In this work we break this constraint by decoupling modules by introducing a model of the

title: Deep Compression: Compressing Deep Neural Network with Pruning, Trained Quantization and Huffman Coding
searchable_text:
Deep Compression: Compressing Deep Neural Network with Pruning, Trained Quantization and Huffman Coding Deploying computationally and memory intensive neural networks on embedded and mobile systems with limited hardware resources, application size, bandwidth, and energy budgets. A three-stage compression pipeline prunes unimportant connections, applies trained weight quantization with sharing, retrains, then uses Huffman coding. Compared with storing full 32-bit dense networks, it combines pruning, trained quantization, retraining, and Huffman coding to drastically reduce connections and bits per connection. TITLE: Deep Compression: Compressing Deep Neural Network with Pruning, Trained Quantization and Huffman Coding

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Neural networks are both computationally intensive and memory intensive, making them difficult to deploy on embedded systems with limited hardware resources. To address this limitation, we introduce "deep compression", a three stage pipeline: pruning, trained quantization and Huffman coding, that work together to reduce the storage requirement of neural networks by 35x to 49x without affecting their accuracy. Our method first prunes

title: Deep Convolutional Networks on Graph-Structured Data
searchable_text:
Deep Convolutional Networks on Graph-Structured Data Large-scale classification on data such as text documents or bioinformatic data that may lack image-like statistical regularities. Extend Spectral Networks by incorporating a Graph Estimation procedure for deep architectures on general non-Euclidean domains. Adds a Graph Estimation procedure to Spectral Networks. TITLE: Deep Convolutional Networks on Graph-Structured Data

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep Learning's recent successes have mostly relied on Convolutional Networks, which exploit fundamental statistical properties of images, sounds and video data: the local stationarity and multi-scale compositional structure, that allows expressing long range interactions in terms of shorter, localized interactions. However, there exist other important examples, such as text documents or bioinformatic data, that may lack some or all of these strong statistical regularities.

title: Deep convolutional neural networks as strong gravitational lens detectors
searchable_text:
Deep convolutional neural networks as strong gravitational lens detectors Automated detection of galaxy-scale strong gravitational lenses in large ground-based and space-based high-resolution imaging surveys. A strong gravitational lens finder using convolutional neural networks, including invariant, views, residual variants and committees of five independently trained CNNs. Shifted lens discovery from human visual classification toward a fully automated CNN lens finder evaluated on the Bologna Lens Factory Strong Lensing challenge. TITLE: Deep convolutional neural networks as strong gravitational lens detectors

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Future large-scale surveys with high resolution imaging will provide us with a few $10^5$ new strong galaxy-scale lenses. These strong lensing systems however will be contained in large data amounts which are beyond the capacity of human experts to visually classify in a unbiased way. We present a new strong gravitational lens finder based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The method was applied to the Strong Lensing challenge organised by the Bologna Len

title: Deep Decentralized Multi-task Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability
searchable_text:
Deep Decentralized Multi-task Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability Multi-task multi-agent reinforcement learning under partial observability, limited communication, and non-observable task identities. Decentralized single-task learning robust to teammate interactions, followed by distillation of single-task policies into one multi-task policy without explicit task identity. Replaces task-specialized policy learning with a unified distilled policy for related tasks that does not require observable task identity. TITLE: Deep Decentralized Multi-task Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many real-world tasks involve multiple agents with partial observability and limited communication. Learning is challenging in these settings due to local viewpoints of agents, which perceive the world as non-stationary due to concurrently-exploring teammates. Approaches that learn specialized policies for individual tasks face problems when applied to the real world: not only do agents have to learn and store distinct policies for each task,

title: Deep Gradient Compression: Reducing the Communication Bandwidth for Distributed Training
searchable_text:
Deep Gradient Compression: Reducing the Communication Bandwidth for Distributed Training Reducing communication bandwidth for large-scale distributed training and federated learning across image classification, speech recognition, and language modeling. Compress distributed SGD gradient exchange using Deep Gradient Compression with momentum correction, local gradient clipping, momentum factor masking, and warm-up training. Changed distributed SGD gradient exchange from full communication to highly compressed gradients while adding accuracy-preserving methods. TITLE: Deep Gradient Compression: Reducing the Communication Bandwidth for Distributed Training

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Large-scale distributed training requires significant communication bandwidth for gradient exchange that limits the scalability of multi-node training, and requires expensive high-bandwidth network infrastructure. The situation gets even worse with distributed training on mobile devices (federated learning), which suffers from higher latency, lower throughput, and intermittent poor connections. In this paper, we find 99.9% of the gradient exchange

title: Deep-HiTS: Rotation Invariant Convolutional Neural Network for Transient Detection
searchable_text:
Deep-HiTS: Rotation Invariant Convolutional Neural Network for Transient Detection Classifying High cadence Transient Survey images of transient candidates as artifacts or real astronomical sources. A rotation-invariant convolutional neural network that automatically learns image features to classify transient candidates. Replaced feature-engineered random forests with a rotation-invariant CNN for astronomical transient detection. TITLE: Deep-HiTS: Rotation Invariant Convolutional Neural Network for Transient Detection

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce Deep-HiTS, a rotation-invariant convolutional neural network (CNN) model for classifying images of transient candidates into artifacts or real sources for the High cadence Transient Survey (HiTS). CNNs have the advantage of learning the features automatically from the data while achieving high performance. We compare our CNN model against a feature engineering approach using random forests (RFs). We show that our CNN significantly outperfor

title: Deep Imitation Learning for Complex Manipulation Tasks from Virtual Reality Teleoperation
searchable_text:
Deep Imitation Learning for Complex Manipulation Tasks from Virtual Reality Teleoperation Robot skill acquisition for complex manipulation tasks requiring visuomotor control from raw pixel observations. Use consumer-grade VR headsets and hand tracking to teleoperate robots, collect demonstrations, and train deep neural-network visuomotor policies mapping raw pixels to actions. Replaces more cumbersome demonstration collection with natural robot teleoperation using consumer-grade Virtual Reality headsets and hand tracking hardware. TITLE: Deep Imitation Learning for Complex Manipulation Tasks from Virtual Reality Teleoperation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Imitation learning is a powerful paradigm for robot skill acquisition. However, obtaining demonstrations suitable for learning a policy that maps from raw pixels to actions can be challenging. In this paper we describe how consumer-grade Virtual Reality headsets and hand tracking hardware can be used to naturally teleoperate robots to perform complex tasks. We also describe how imitation learning can learn deep neural network policies (mapping fr

title: Deep Interactive Object Selection
searchable_text:
Deep Interactive Object Selection Interactive object selection with minimal user clicks across seen and unseen object classes. Convert positive/negative clicks into Euclidean distance maps, concatenate with RGB, finetune FCNs on simulated click patterns, then refine output probability maps using graph cut optimization. Replaces interaction-heavy foreground/background distribution estimation with deep objectness learned from click-distance-map-conditioned FCNs, reducing user interaction to a few clicks. TITLE: Deep Interactive Object Selection

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Interactive object selection is a very important research problem and has many applications. Previous algorithms require substantial user interactions to estimate the foreground and background distributions. In this paper, we present a novel deep-learning-based algorithm which has much better understanding of objectness and can reduce user interactions to just a few clicks. Our algorithm transforms user-provided positive and negative clicks into two Euclidean distance maps which are then concatenated

title: Deep Learning Based MIMO Communications
searchable_text:
Deep Learning Based MIMO Communications Single-user Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless physical-layer communications using spatial diversity and spatial multiplexing. Unsupervised autoencoder jointly optimizes MIMO transmitter, channel-impaired representation, and receivers end-to-end, incorporating a Rayleigh fading channel model for spatial diversity and multiplexing. Extends prior end-to-end physical-layer autoencoder optimization by expanding transmitter and receiver design to the multi-antenna MIMO case and adding Rayleigh fading impairment. TITLE: Deep Learning Based MIMO Communications

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a novel physical layer scheme for single user Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) communications based on unsupervised deep learning using an autoencoder. This method extends prior work on the joint optimization of physical layer representation and encoding and decoding processes as a single end-to-end task by expanding transmitter and receivers to the multi-antenna case. title: Deep Learning for Classification of Colorectal Polyps on Whole-slide Images
searchable_text:
Deep Learning for Classification of Colorectal Polyps on Whole-slide Images Classification of five common colorectal polyp types on whole-slide histopathology images for cancer-risk assessment and surveillance decisions. Deep-learning image analysis using multiple approaches, with a residual network architecture performing best, trained from expert-annotated crop images to classify whole-slide histopathology. Automated whole-slide classification across five guideline-relevant colorectal polyp types using deep learning and expert pathologist annotations. TITLE: Deep Learning for Classification of Colorectal Polyps on Whole-slide Images

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Context: Histopathological characterization of colorectal polyps is critical for determining the risk of colorectal cancer and future rates of surveillance for patients. However, this characterization is a challenging task and suffers from significant inter- and intra-observer variability. Aims: We built an automatic image analysis method that can accurately classify different types of colorectal polyps on whole-slide images to help pathologists with this char

title: Deep Learning for Precipitation Nowcasting: A Benchmark and A New Model
searchable_text:
Deep Learning for Precipitation Nowcasting: A Benchmark and A New Model High-resolution regional rainfall forecasting for precipitation nowcasting and related public services. Trajectory GRU learns location-variant recurrent connection structures for precipitation nowcasting, moving beyond fixed convolutional recurrence. Replaces ConvLSTM’s location-invariant convolutional recurrence with TrajGRU’s actively learned location-variant recurrent connections, alongside a benchmark dataset, loss, and evaluation protocol. TITLE: Deep Learning for Precipitation Nowcasting: A Benchmark and A New Model

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
With the goal of making high-resolution forecasts of regional rainfall, precipitation nowcasting has become an important and fundamental technology underlying various public services ranging from rainstorm warnings to flight safety. Recently, the Convolutional LSTM (ConvLSTM) model has been shown to outperform traditional optical flow based methods for precipitation nowcasting, suggesting that deep learning models have a huge potential for solving the problem. title: Deep Learning for Real-time Gravitational Wave Detection and Parameter Estimation with Advanced LIGO Data
searchable_text:
Deep Learning for Real-time Gravitational Wave Detection and Parameter Estimation with Advanced LIGO Data Real-time detection and parameter estimation of gravitational waves from binary black hole mergers in continuous Advanced LIGO detector streams. Deep Filtering: convolutional neural networks taking time-series inputs for rapid detection and parameter estimation of gravitational-wave signals in LIGO data. Moved Deep Filtering from simulated LIGO noise to real LIGO data, handling detection and parameter estimation on continuous streams from multiple LIGO detectors. TITLE: Deep Learning for Real-time Gravitational Wave Detection and Parameter Estimation with Advanced LIGO Data

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

To enhance the scope of this emergent field of science, we pioneered the use of deep learning with convolutional neural networks, that take time-series inputs,

title: Deep learning in neural networks: An overview
searchable_text:
Deep learning in neural networks: An overview Historical understanding of deep artificial neural networks for pattern recognition and machine learning. Define deep learning via credit-assignment path depth, then synthesize supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement/evolutionary, and indirect program-search approaches for deep and large neural networks. It reframes earlier work through credit-assignment path depth while compactly surveying relevant deep-learning history, including much work from the previous millennium. TITLE: Deep learning in neural networks: An overview

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In recent years, deep artificial neural networks (including recurrent ones) have won numerous contests in pattern recognition and machine learning. This historical survey compactly summarizes relevant work, much of it from the previous millennium. Shallow and Deep Learners are distinguished by the depth of their credit assignment paths, which are chains of possibly learnable, causal links between actions and effects. title: Deep Learning without Poor Local Minima
searchable_text:
Deep Learning without Poor Local Minima Optimization difficulty and local-minimum structure in training deep neural networks. Theoretical landscape analysis of squared-loss deep linear networks, extended to nonlinear networks by reduction under an independence assumption. Proved a 1989 conjecture and partially addressed a COLT 2015 open problem without unrealistic assumptions. TITLE: Deep Learning without Poor Local Minima

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we prove a conjecture published in 1989 and also partially address an open problem announced at the Conference on Learning Theory (COLT) 2015. With no unrealistic assumption, we first prove the following statements for the squared loss function of deep linear neural networks with any depth and any widths: 1) the function is non-convex and non-concave, 2) every local minimum is a global minimum, 3) every critical point that is not a global minimum is a saddle point, and 4) there ex

title: Deep MIMO detection
searchable_text:
Deep MIMO detection Multiple-Input-Multiple-Output (MIMO) detection under constant or changing known channel conditions. A modern deep neural network architecture learns MIMO detectors, first for fixed channels and then a single detector across varying known channel parameters. Replaces hand-designed MIMO detection methods such as approximate message passing and semidefinite relaxation with a learned deep neural detector. TITLE: Deep MIMO detection

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we consider the use of deep neural networks in the context of Multiple-Input-Multiple-Output (MIMO) detection. We give a brief introduction to deep learning and propose a modern neural network architecture suitable for this detection task. First, we consider the case in which the MIMO channel is constant, and we learn a detector for a specific system. Next, we consider the harder case in which the parameters are known yet changing and a single detector must be learned for all multiple varying channel

title: Deep Models Under the GAN: Information Leakage from Collaborative Deep Learning
searchable_text:
Deep Models Under the GAN: Information Leakage from Collaborative Deep Learning Privacy leakage from collaborative, federated, or decentralized deep learning systems that share model parameters while trying to keep participants’ training data private. Exploit real-time collaborative learning updates to train a GAN that generates prototypical samples from a targeted participant’s private training distribution. Compared with prior collaborative deep learning with subset parameter sharing and optional DP, this work introduces a GAN attack that reconstructs prototypical private training samples. TITLE: Deep Models Under the GAN: Information Leakage from Collaborative Deep Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep Learning has recently become hugely popular in machine learning for its ability to solve end-to-end learning systems, in which the features and the classifiers are learned simultaneously, providing significant improvements in classification accuracy in the presence of highly-structured and large databases. Its success is due to a combination of recent algorithmic breakthroughs, increasingly powerful computers, and access to significant amounts of data

title: Deep multi-view models for glitch classification
searchable_text:
Deep multi-view models for glitch classification Automatic classification of non-cosmic, non-Gaussian glitches in aLIGO gravitational-wave data. A deep multi-view convolutional neural network classifies glitch spectrogram images by exploiting four different views. Replaces traditional single-view glitch classifiers with a four-view deep neural network using spectrogram-based image classification. TITLE: Deep multi-view models for glitch classification

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Non-cosmic, non-Gaussian disturbances known as “glitches”, show up in gravitational-wave data of the Advanced Laser Interferometer Gravitational-wave Observatory, or aLIGO. In this paper, we propose a deep multi-view convolutional neural network to classify glitches automatically. The primary purpose of classifying glitches is to understand their characteristics and origin, which facilitates their removal from the data or from the detector entirely. We visualize glitches as spectrograms

title: Deep Network Guided Proof Search
searchable_text:
Deep Network Guided Proof Search Automated first-order theorem proving for Mizar Mathematical Library statements, especially finding ATP-generated proofs for first-order logic translations. Train deep neural networks on traces of existing ATP proofs of Mizar statements and use them to select processed clauses during E prover proof search. Replaced or augmented conventional clause-selection proof guidance with deep neural network models trained from prior ATP proof traces. TITLE: Deep Network Guided Proof Search

title: Deep Networks with Stochastic Depth
searchable_text:
Deep Networks with Stochastic Depth Training very deep convolutional/residual networks with hundreds to over 1200 layers while avoiding vanishing gradients, diminished forward flow, and slow training. During training, randomly drop subsets of residual-network layers per mini-batch and bypass them with identity functions, while using the full deep network at test time. Instead of training every layer in a very deep residual network, training uses randomly shortened networks by dropping layers, then restores the full depth at test time. TITLE: Deep Networks with Stochastic Depth

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Very deep convolutional networks with hundreds of layers have led to significant reductions in error on competitive benchmarks. Although the unmatched expressiveness of the many layers can be highly desirable at test time, training very deep networks comes with its own set of challenges. The gradients can vanish, the forward flow often diminishes, and the training time can be painfully slow. title: Deep neural networks for improved, impromptu trajectory tracking of quadrotors
searchable_text:
Deep neural networks for improved, impromptu trajectory tracking of quadrotors High-precision, impromptu trajectory tracking for quadrotors, especially user-drawn “fly-as-you-draw” trajectories on mobile devices. A DNN add-on learns from experience to provide tailored reference inputs to a classical feedback controller, aiming to map desired trajectories to achieved quadrotor outputs. Instead of relying only on hand-tuned classical controllers, the system adds a learned DNN reference-shaping module to improve trajectory tracking without replacing the feedback controller. TITLE: Deep neural networks for improved, impromptu trajectory tracking of quadrotors

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Trajectory tracking control for quadrotors is important for applications ranging from surveying and inspection, to film making. However, designing and tuning classical controllers, such as proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controllers, to achieve high tracking precision can be time-consuming and difficult, due to hidden dynamics and other non-idealities.
