TITLE: Gossip Dual Averaging for Decentralized Optimization of Pairwise Functions

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In decentralized networks (of sensors, connected objects, etc.), there is an important need for efficient algorithms to optimize a global cost function, for instance to learn a global model from the local data collected by each computing unit. In this paper, we address the problem of decentralized minimization of pairwise functions of the data points, where these points are distributed over the nodes of a graph defining the communication topology of the network

title: Gradient-based Hyperparameter Optimization through Reversible Learning
searchable_text:
Gradient-based Hyperparameter Optimization through Reversible Learning Gradient-based tuning of learning-algorithm hyperparameters for cross-validation performance, including schedules, initialization distributions, regularization schemes, and neural network architectures. Chain derivatives backwards through the entire training procedure, exactly reversing stochastic gradient descent with momentum to compute hyperparameter gradients. Changed hyperparameter optimization from gradient-unavailable tuning to exact gradient computation through reversible learning dynamics. TITLE: Gradient-based Hyperparameter Optimization through Reversible Learning

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Tuning hyperparameters of learning algorithms is hard because gradients are usually unavailable. We compute exact gradients of cross-validation performance with respect to all hyperparameters by chaining derivatives backwards through the entire training procedure. These gradients allow us to optimize thousands of hyperparameters, including step-size and momentum schedules, weight initialization distributions, richly parameterized regularization schemes, and neural

title: Graph Attention Networks
searchable_text:
Graph Attention Networks Graph-structured data tasks, including transductive citation-network benchmarks and inductive protein-protein interaction graphs. Masked self-attentional graph layers let nodes attend over neighborhood features and assign different weights to different neighboring nodes. Replaced graph convolutions or their approximations with masked self-attentional layers, avoiding costly matrix operations and dependence on knowing graph structure upfront. TITLE: Graph Attention Networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or d

title: Graph Matching: Relax at Your Own Risk
searchable_text:
Graph Matching: Relax at Your Own Risk Aligning a pair of graphs to minimize edge disagreements in graph matching applications such as combinatorics, computer vision, and connectomics. Use an exact indefinite continuous relaxation for graph matching, practically initialized with the convex relaxation optimum to amalgamate the two approaches. Changed from unsupported heuristics and common convex relaxation to a theoretically analyzed indefinite relaxation plus convex-optimum initialization for improved practical performance. TITLE: Graph Matching: Relax at Your Own Risk

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Graph matching-aligning a pair of graphs to minimize their edge disagreements-has received wide-spread attention from both theoretical and applied communities over the past several decades, including combinatorics, computer vision, and connectomics. Its attention can be partially attributed to its computational difficulty. Although many heuristics have previously been proposed in the literature to approximately solve graph matching, very few have any theoretical support for their performance. A c

title: Gravitational waves from merging compact binaries: How accurately can one extract the binary's parameters from the inspiral waveform?
searchable_text:
Gravitational waves from merging compact binaries: How accurately can one extract the binary's parameters from the inspiral waveform? Accuracy of extracting source distance, component masses, and spins for NS-NS, NS-BH, and BH-BH compact-binary inspirals. Estimate binary parameters from inspiral gravitational-wave signals using the three-detector LIGO-VIRGO advanced-detector network and post-Newtonian phase dependence. Moves beyond lowest-order chirp-mass-only phase dependence by using post-1-Newtonian reduced-mass sensitivity to enable individual mass measurement. TITLE: Gravitational waves from merging compact binaries: How accurately can one extract the binary's parameters from the inspiral waveform?

YEAR: 1994

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The most promising source of gravitational waves for the planned kilometer-size laser-interferometer detectors LIGO and VIRGO are merging compact binaries, i.e., neutron-star\char21{}neutron-star (NS-NS), neutron-star\char21{}black-hole (NS-BH), and black-hole\char21{}black-hole (BH-BH) binaries. We investigate how accurately the distance to the source and the masses and spins of the two bodies will be

title: Gravity Spy: integrating advanced LIGO detector characterization, machine learning, and citizen science
searchable_text:
Gravity Spy: integrating advanced LIGO detector characterization, machine learning, and citizen science Categorizing and characterizing transient, non-Gaussian LIGO detector glitches across evolving time-frequency-amplitude morphologies that can obscure or mimic gravitational-wave signals. Combine Zooniverse citizen-science glitch image labels with machine-learning classifiers trained on human-classified time-frequency morphologies, then leverage both methods to improve classification efficiency and accuracy. Moves beyond LIGO-scientist-only detector characterization by integrating public crowdsourcing and trained machine-learning classifiers into a combined glitch-classification workflow. TITLE: Gravity Spy: integrating advanced LIGO detector characterization, machine learning, and citizen science

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
With the first direct detection of gravitational waves, the advanced laser interferometer gravitational-wave observatory (LIGO) has initiated a new field of astronomy by providing an alternative means of sensing the universe. The extreme sensitivity required to make such detections is achieved through exquisite isolation of all sensitive components of LIGO from non-gravitational-wave disturbances. Nonetheless, LIGO is still suscepti

title: Ground metric learning
searchable_text:
Ground metric learning Comparing histograms of features with optimal transport distances for retrieval and binary/multiclass classification. Learn the optimal-transport ground metric from labeled histograms by optimizing a difference of convex polyhedral functions over metric matrices. Replaces practitioner-specified ground metrics based on a priori feature knowledge with ground metrics learned from labeled histogram data. TITLE: Ground metric learning

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Optimal transport distances have been used for more than a decade in machine learning to compare histograms of features. They have one parameter: the ground metric, which can be any metric between the features themselves. As is the case for all parameterized distances, optimal transport distances can only prove useful in practice when this parameter is carefully chosen. To date, the only option available to practitioners to set the ground metric parameter was to rely on a priori knowledge of the features, which

title: GSNs : Generative Stochastic Networks
searchable_text:
GSNs : Generative Stochastic Networks Generative probabilistic modeling, including missing-input handling and conditional sampling of variable subsets on synthetic and image datasets. Learn the transition operator of a Markov chain whose stationary distribution estimates the data distribution, using conditional small-move transitions trainable by backprop. Generalizes denoising auto-encoders: noise-trained auto-encoders become special-case GSN generative models, trained by transition learning rather than maximum likelihood. TITLE: GSNs : Generative Stochastic Networks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a novel training principle for generative probabilistic models that is an alternative to maximum likelihood. The proposed Generative Stochastic Networks (GSN) framework generalizes Denoising Auto-Encoders (DAE) and is based on learning the transition operator of a Markov chain whose stationary distribution estimates the data distribution. The transition distribution is a conditional distribution that generally involves a small move, so it has fewer dominant modes and is unimodal in th

title: Guided Cost Learning: Deep Inverse Optimal Control via Policy Optimization
searchable_text:
Guided Cost Learning: Deep Inverse Optimal Control via Policy Optimization Learning behaviors from demonstrations for high-dimensional continuous robotic control, especially torque-controlled simulated and real-world manipulation tasks. Sample-based maximum-entropy inverse optimal control learns arbitrary nonlinear cost functions, including neural networks, and couples cost learning with policy optimization under unknown dynamics. Replaced feature-engineered IOC costs with arbitrary nonlinear costs and introduced an efficient sample-based approximation for MaxEnt IOC in unknown-dynamics continuous domains. TITLE: Guided Cost Learning: Deep Inverse Optimal Control via Policy Optimization

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Reinforcement learning can acquire complex behaviors from high-level specifications. However, defining a cost function that can be optimized effectively and encodes the correct task is challenging in practice. We explore how inverse optimal control (IOC) can be used to learn behaviors from demonstrations, with applications to torque control of high-dimensional robotic systems. Our method addresses two key challenges in inverse optimal control: first, the need f

title: GW170814: A Three-Detector Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Coalescence.
searchable_text:
GW170814: A Three-Detector Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Coalescence. Detection and astrophysical characterization of gravitational waves from a stellar-mass binary black-hole coalescence. Coherent three-detector Advanced LIGO–Virgo matched-filter observation of a transient gravitational-wave signal from binary black-hole coalescence. Adding Advanced Virgo to the two Advanced LIGO detectors reduced sky localization from 1160 deg² to 60 deg² and enabled polarization tests. TITLE: GW170814: A Three-Detector Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Coalescence.

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
On August 14, 2017 at 10∶30:43 UTC, the Advanced Virgo detector and the two Advanced LIGO detectors coherently observed a transient gravitational-wave signal produced by the coalescence of two stellar mass black holes, with a false-alarm rate of ≲1 in 27 000 years. The signal was observed with a three-detector network matched-filter signal-to-noise ratio of 18. The inferred masses of the initial black holes are 30.5_{-3.0}^{+5.7}M_{⊙} a

title: Hafez: an Interactive Poetry Generation System
searchable_text:
Hafez: an Interactive Poetry Generation System Generating and interactively revising sonnets from arbitrary user-provided topics. Combines a Recurrent Neural Network with a Finite State Acceptor, plus user-adjustable style polishing and feedback-driven parameter tuning. Adds interactive style-configuration polishing and web-feedback-based parameter adjustment to automatic topic-conditioned sonnet generation. TITLE: Hafez: an Interactive Poetry Generation System

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Hafez is an automatic poetry generation system that integrates a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) with a Finite State Accep-tor (FSA). It generates sonnets given arbitrary topics. Furthermore, Hafez enables users to revise and polish generated poems by adjusting various style conﬁgurations. Experiments demonstrate that such “pol-ish” mechanisms consider the user’s intention and lead to a better poem. For evaluation, we build a web interface where users can rate the quality of each poem from

title: Hamiltonian Simulation by Qubitization
searchable_text:
Hamiltonian Simulation by Qubitization Approximating Hamiltonian time evolution for Hamiltonians given as projections of unitary oracles onto oracle-prepared states, including sparse, linear-combination-of-unitaries, and density-matrix instances. Use controlled oracle access to qubitize a projected-unitary Hamiltonian, embedding it in invariant SU(2) subspaces so operator functions implement time evolution with optimal queries. Compared with prior sparse-Hamiltonian and linear-combination-of-unitaries simulation, it subsumes both models and improves space and gate complexity, including quadratic precision-simulation speed-up. TITLE: Hamiltonian Simulation by Qubitization

title: HEALPix: A Framework for High-Resolution Discretization and Fast Analysis of Data Distributed on the Sphere
searchable_text:
HEALPix: A Framework for High-Resolution Discretization and Fast Analysis of Data Distributed on the Sphere Processing and analyzing large astronomical datasets distributed on the sphere, especially cosmic microwave background and future mission sky maps. Hierarchical equal-area, iso-latitude pixelization with hierarchical indexation plus algorithms/visualization for direct fast analysis and synthesis on discretized spherical maps.  TITLE: HEALPix: A Framework for High-Resolution Discretization and Fast Analysis of Data Distributed on the Sphere

ABSTRACT:
HEALPix—the Hierarchical Equal Area isoLatitude Pixelization—is a versatile structure for the pixelization of data on the sphere. An associated library of computational algorithms and visualization software supports fast scientific applications executable directly on discretized spherical maps generated from very large volumes of astronomical data. Originally developed to address the data processing and analysis needs of the pre

title: Hierarchical modeling of molecular energies using a deep neural network.
searchable_text:
Hierarchical modeling of molecular energies using a deep neural network. Modeling molecular properties, especially molecular energies, from datasets of quantum calculations for organic molecules and molecular dynamics trajectories. HIP-NN decomposes molecular properties into sums over hierarchical terms generated by a deep neural network acting on a molecular representation. It adds a many-body-inspired hierarchical decomposition of properties to neural-network molecular energy modeling. TITLE: Hierarchical modeling of molecular energies using a deep neural network.

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce the Hierarchically Interacting Particle Neural Network (HIP-NN) to model molecular properties from datasets of quantum calculations. Inspired by a many-body expansion, HIP-NN decomposes properties, such as energy, as a sum over hierarchical terms. These terms are generated from a neural network-a composition of many nonlinear transformations-acting on a representation of the molecule. HIP-NN achieves the state-of-the-art performance on a dataset of 1

title: Hierarchical Representations for Efficient Architecture Search
searchable_text:
Hierarchical Representations for Efficient Architecture Search Neural architecture search for image classification models on CIFAR-10 and transfer to ImageNet. A simple evolutionary architecture search using a hierarchical genetic representation that imitates modular human design patterns within an expressive complex-topology search space. Replaced manual architecture design with evolutionary search over a hierarchical modular genetic representation and expressive topology space. TITLE: Hierarchical Representations for Efficient Architecture Search

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We explore efficient neural architecture search methods and show that a simple yet powerful evolutionary algorithm can discover new architectures with excellent performance. Our approach combines a novel hierarchical genetic representation scheme that imitates the modularized design pattern commonly adopted by human experts, and an expressive search space that supports complex topologies. title: High-Confidence Off-Policy Evaluation
searchable_text:
High-Confidence Off-Policy Evaluation Evaluating the performance of a new reinforcement-learning policy without executing it, when bad policy execution may be costly or dangerous. Compute a lower confidence bound on a target policy’s expected return using off-policy trajectories collected from other policies. Adds confidence-bound estimation to off-policy evaluation, specifically a lower confidence bound on expected return. TITLE: High-Confidence Off-Policy Evaluation

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 Many reinforcement learning algorithms use trajectories collected from the execution of one or more policies to propose a new policy. Because execution of a bad policy can be costly or dangerous, techniques for evaluating the performance of the new policy without requiring its execution have been of recent interest in industry. Such off-policy evaluation methods, which estimate the performance of a policy using trajectories collected from the execution of other policies, heretofore have not pr

title: High-Dimensional Continuous Control Using Generalized Advantage Estimation
searchable_text:
High-Dimensional Continuous Control Using Generalized Advantage Estimation Model-free reinforcement learning for high-dimensional continuous control, especially challenging 3D locomotion tasks for simulated bipedal and quadrupedal robots. Estimate advantages with an exponentially weighted TD(lambda)-like value-function estimator and optimize neural-network policy/value functions with trust-region procedures. Replaced hand-crafted policy representations with neural-network policies mapping raw kinematics directly to joint torques, plus generalized advantage estimation and trust-region optimization. TITLE: High-Dimensional Continuous Control Using Generalized Advantage Estimation

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Policy gradient methods are an appealing approach in reinforcement learning because they directly optimize the cumulative reward and can straightforwardly be used with nonlinear function approximators such as neural networks. The two main challenges are the large number of samples typically required, and the difficulty of obtaining stable and steady improvement despite the nonstationarity of the incoming data. We address the first challenge by using value funct

title: Holistically-Nested Edge Detection
searchable_text:
Holistically-Nested Edge Detection Edge and object boundary detection in images, especially benchmark datasets such as BSDS500, NYU Depth, Multicue, and PASCAL-Context. Image-to-image edge prediction using a fully convolutional, deeply supervised network with side responses to learn hierarchical multi-scale, multi-level features. Compared with earlier CNN edge detectors, HED introduced holistic image-to-image training/prediction with deeply supervised multi-level side outputs and much faster inference. TITLE: Holistically-Nested Edge Detection

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We develop a new edge detection algorithm that addresses two important issues in this long-standing vision problem: (1) holistic image training and prediction; and (2) multi-scale and multi-level feature learning. Our proposed method, holistically-nested edge detection (HED), performs image-to-image prediction by means of a deep learning model that leverages fully convolutional neural networks and deeply-supervised nets. HED automatically learns rich hierarchical representations (guided by deep super

title: HotFlip: White-Box Adversarial Examples for Text Classification
searchable_text:
HotFlip: White-Box Adversarial Examples for Text Classification White-box adversarial example generation for text classification, primarily character-level neural classifiers and adaptable to word-level classifiers. Gradient-guided atomic flip operation swaps one token for another using gradients of one-hot input vectors to craft white-box adversarial text examples. Introduces an efficient gradient-based token flip attack for text classifiers, enabling adversarial training and extension from character-level to constrained word-level attacks. TITLE: HotFlip: White-Box Adversarial Examples for Text Classification

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose an efficient method to generate white-box adversarial examples to trick a character-level neural classifier. We find that only a few manipulations are needed to greatly decrease the accuracy. Our method relies on an atomic flip operation, which swaps one token for another, based on the gradients of the one-hot input vectors. Due to efficiency of our method, we can perform adversarial training which makes the model more robust to attacks at test time. With the u

title: How do Humans Evaluate Machine Translation
searchable_text:
How do Humans Evaluate Machine Translation Human evaluation of machine translation, specifically how monolingual versus bilingual evaluators use target-language and other information sources. A glass-box MT evaluation study using eye-tracking to compare evaluator background and available information sources, judged by time and consistency. Moves MT evaluation analysis toward a glass-box eye-tracking view of evaluator behavior and information access. TITLE: How do Humans Evaluate Machine Translation

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we take a closer look at the MT evaluation process from a glass-box perspective using eye-tracking. We analyze two aspects of the evaluation task ‐ the background of evaluators (monolingual or bilingual) and the sources of information available, and we evaluate them using time and consistency as criteria. Our findings show that monolinguals are slower but more consistent than bilinguals, especially when only target language information is available. When exposed to various sour

title: HOW ROCKY ARE THEY? THE COMPOSITION DISTRIBUTION OF KEPLER’S SUB-NEPTUNE PLANET CANDIDATES WITHIN 0.15 AU
searchable_text:
HOW ROCKY ARE THEY? THE COMPOSITION DISTRIBUTION OF KEPLER’S SUB-NEPTUNE PLANET CANDIDATES WITHIN 0.15 AU Composition distribution and gaseous/rocky transition of Kepler sub-Neptune planet candidates within 0.15 AU, especially 1–4 Earth-radius planets lacking solar-system analogues. Hierarchical Bayesian modeling of a complete Kepler PC subsample, combined with interior-structure models for thermally evolved H+He envelopes around rocky cores. Replaced individual precise mass-based characterization with population-level statistical inference using completeness-selected Kepler candidates and evolved envelope structure models. TITLE: HOW ROCKY ARE THEY? THE COMPOSITION DISTRIBUTION OF KEPLER’S SUB-NEPTUNE PLANET CANDIDATES WITHIN 0.15 AU

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Kepler Mission has found thousands of planetary candidates with radii between 1 and 4 R ⨁ ?> . These planets have no analogues in our own solar system, providing an unprecedented opportunity to understand the range and distribution of planetary compositions allowed by planet formation and evolution. A precise mass measurement is usually required to constrain the possible composition of an individual super-Earth-sized planet, b

title: HyperNetworks
searchable_text:
HyperNetworks Sequence modelling tasks, including character-level language modelling, handwriting generation, and neural machine translation. Use a hypernetwork trained end-to-end with the main network to generate adaptive, non-shared weights for recurrent networks such as LSTMs. Replaces fixed recurrent weight-sharing with a relaxed scheme where another network generates layer- or time-adaptive weights. TITLE: HyperNetworks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This work explores hypernetworks: an approach of using one network, also known as a hypernetwork, to generate the weights for another network. We apply hypernetworks to generate adaptive weights for recurrent networks. In this case, hypernetworks can be viewed as a relaxed form of weight-sharing across layers. In our implementation, hypernetworks are are trained jointly with the main network in an end-to-end fashion. Our main result is that hypernetworks can generate non-shared weights for LSTM and achieve state-of-the-a

title: Hyperparameter optimization with approximate gradient
searchable_text:
Hyperparameter optimization with approximate gradient Selection of continuous hyperparameters controlling model complexity in machine learning, especially regularization constants for L2-logistic regression and kernel Ridge regression. Optimize continuous hyperparameters using inexact gradient information, allowing hyperparameter updates before model parameters fully converge. Unlike methods requiring fully converged model parameters, this method updates hyperparameters before model parameters have fully converged. TITLE: Hyperparameter optimization with approximate gradient

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most models in machine learning contain at least one hyperparameter to control for model complexity. Choosing an appropriate set of hyperparameters is both crucial in terms of model accuracy and computationally challenging. In this work we propose an algorithm for the optimization of continuous hyperparameters using inexact gradient information. An advantage of this method is that hyperparameters can be updated before model parameters have fully converged. We also give sufficient c

title: Identifying boosted objects with N-subjettiness
searchable_text:
Identifying boosted objects with N-subjettiness Tagging boosted hadronically-decaying objects such as electroweak bosons and top quarks while rejecting large-invariant-mass QCD jet backgrounds. Use N-subjettiness, a new jet shape, combined with a jet invariant mass cut to discriminate boosted-object jets from QCD jets. Introduces N-subjettiness as a jet-shape variable that combines jet-shape advantages with discriminating power from prior jet substructure algorithms. TITLE: Identifying boosted objects with N-subjettiness

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a new jet shape — N-subjettiness — designed to identify boosted hadronically-decaying objects like electroweak bosons and top quarks. Combined with a jet invariant mass cut, N-subjettiness is an effective discriminating variable for tagging boosted objects and rejecting the background of QCD jets with large invariant mass. In efficiency studies of boosted W bosons and top quarks, we find tagging efficiencies of 30% are achievable with fake rates of 1%. We also consider the d

title: Identifying the source of perytons at the Parkes radio telescope
searchable_text:
Identifying the source of perytons at the Parkes radio telescope Identifying the terrestrial source of Parkes perytons and separating them from genuine fast radio bursts such as FRB 010724. Associate peryton events with 2.3--2.5 GHz out-of-band emission, then reproduce 1.4 GHz events by prematurely opening microwave ovens at suitable telescope angles. The source changed from an unexplained dispersion-mimicking mystery that shadowed FRB interpretation to an identified microwave-oven artifact distinct from FRB 010724. TITLE: Identifying the source of perytons at the Parkes radio telescope

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
"Perytons" are millisecond-duration transients of terrestrial origin, whose frequency-swept emission mimics the dispersion of an astrophysical pulse that has propagated through tenuous cold plasma. In fact, their similarity to FRB 010724 had previously cast a shadow over the interpretation of "fast radio bursts," which otherwise appear to be of extragalactic origin. Until now, the physical origin of the dispersion-mimicking perytons had remained a mystery. We have identi

title: Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks
searchable_text:
Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks Training and improving very deep residual neural networks for image classification on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet. Use identity mappings as residual skip connections with after-addition activation to enable direct forward and backward signal propagation across blocks. Compared with prior residual blocks, the residual unit emphasizes identity skip connections and after-addition activation to improve signal propagation and optimization. TITLE: Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep residual networks have emerged as a family of extremely deep architectures showing compelling accuracy and nice convergence behaviors. In this paper, we analyze the propagation formulations behind the residual building blocks, which suggest that the forward and backward signals can be directly propagated from one block to any other block, when using identity mappings as the skip connections and after-addition activation. A series of ablation experiments support the importance of these i

title: IEEE Access Special Section Editorial: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Networking
searchable_text:
IEEE Access Special Section Editorial: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Networking Optimization and management of increasingly dynamic, heterogeneous, and complex computer networks, including cloud, NFV, SDN, 5G, and future cognitive networks. Deploy AI-based techniques spanning machine learning, optimization, game theory, control theory, and meta-heuristics to optimize and manage computer networks. Moves from applying AI in diverse network optimization settings toward architecturally integrated cognitive networks with network-wide intelligent behavior. TITLE: IEEE Access Special Section Editorial: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Networking

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
With today’s computer networks becoming increasingly dynamic, heterogeneous, and complex, there is great interest in deploying artificial intelligence (AI) based techniques for optimization and management of computer networks. AI techniques—that subsume multidisciplinary techniques from machine learning, optimization theory, game theory, control theory, and meta-heuristics—have long been applied to optimize computer networks in many diverse settings. Suc

title: Image-to-Image Translation with Conditional Adversarial Networks
searchable_text:
Image-to-Image Translation with Conditional Adversarial Networks General-purpose image-to-image translation, including synthesizing photos from label maps, reconstructing objects from edge maps, and colorizing images. Use conditional adversarial networks to learn both an input-to-output image mapping and the loss function for training that mapping. Replaced task-specific hand-engineered loss formulations with a generic framework that learns the loss function along with the mapping. TITLE: Image-to-Image Translation with Conditional Adversarial Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We investigate conditional adversarial networks as a general-purpose solution to image-to-image translation problems. These networks not only learn the mapping from input image to output image, but also learn a loss function to train this mapping. This makes it possible to apply the same generic approach to problems that traditionally would require very different loss formulations. We demonstrate that this approach is effective at synthesizing photos from label maps, rec

title: ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge
searchable_text:
ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge Large-scale visual object category classification and detection on hundreds of object categories and millions of images. An annually run benchmark dataset/challenge with large-scale ground-truth annotation for object category classification and detection across many categories and images. Introduced a large-scale standardized visual recognition benchmark dataset spanning hundreds of categories and millions of images, run annually from 2010 onward. TITLE: ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge is a benchmark in object category classification and detection on hundreds of object categories and millions of images. The challenge has been run annually from 2010 to present, attracting participation from more than fifty institutions. This paper describes the creation of this benchmark dataset and the advances in object recognition that have been possible as a result. We discuss the challenges of collecting large-scale ground tru

title: Imagination-Augmented Agents for Deep Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
Imagination-Augmented Agents for Deep Reinforcement Learning Deep reinforcement learning agents needing data-efficient, high-performing policies under imperfect learned environment models. Deep RL architecture that combines model-free and model-based components, learning to use predictions from a learned environment model as context for deep policy networks. Instead of prescribing model-based planning, I2As learn how to interpret model predictions and construct implicit plans in arbitrary ways. TITLE: Imagination-Augmented Agents for Deep Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce Imagination-Augmented Agents (I2As), a novel architecture for deep reinforcement learning combining model-free and model-based aspects. In contrast to most existing model-based reinforcement learning and planning methods, which prescribe how a model should be used to arrive at a policy, I2As learn to interpret predictions from a learned environment model to construct implicit plans in arbitrary ways, by using the predictions as additional context in deep policy

title: Improved Adversarial Systems for 3D Object Generation and Reconstruction
searchable_text:
Improved Adversarial Systems for 3D Object Generation and Reconstruction 3D object generation, 3D reconstruction from 2D images, and shape completion from occluded 2.5D range scans across many categories and orientations. Train 3D GANs using Wasserstein distance normalized with gradient penalization as the adversarial objective. Extends prior 3D GAN work by replacing earlier objectives with Wasserstein distance plus gradient penalization. TITLE: Improved Adversarial Systems for 3D Object Generation and Reconstruction

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper describes a new approach for training generative adversarial networks (GAN) to understand the detailed 3D shape of objects. While GANs have been used in this domain previously, they are notoriously hard to train, especially for the complex joint data distribution over 3D objects of many categories and orientations. Our method extends previous work by employing the Wasserstein distance normalized with gradient penalization as a training objective. This

title: Improved Semantic Representations From Tree-Structured Long Short-Term Memory Networks
searchable_text:
Improved Semantic Representations From Tree-Structured Long Short-Term Memory Networks Predicting semantic relatedness between sentence pairs and sentiment classification of natural-language sentences. Generalize LSTM recurrent units from linear chains to tree-structured network topologies that compose words into phrases. Changed the underlying LSTM topology from the previously explored linear chain to a tree-structured network. TITLE: Improved Semantic Representations From Tree-Structured Long Short-Term Memory Networks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Because of their superior ability to preserve sequence information over time, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, a type of recurrent neural network with a more complex computational unit, have obtained strong results on a variety of sequence modeling tasks. The only underlying LSTM structure that has been explored so far is a linear chain. However, natural language exhibits syntactic properties that would naturally combine words to phrases. We

title: Improved Simulation of Stabilizer Circuits
searchable_text:
Improved Simulation of Stabilizer Circuits Efficient classical simulation and analysis of quantum stabilizer circuits built from CNOT, Hadamard, and phase gates, including large-qubit implementations and related stabilizer-state computations. Tableau-style stabilizer simulation that avoids Gaussian elimination, trading doubled state-representation bits for faster updates and supporting canonicalization, inner products, mixed states, and limited nonstabilizer extensions. Compared with the Gottesman-Knill simulation approach, it removes Gaussian elimination, adds a freely available CHP implementation, proves ⊕L-completeness, and expands efficient stabilizer-state algorithms. TITLE: Improved Simulation of Stabilizer Circuits

YEAR: 2004

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Gottesman-Knill theorem says that a stabilizer circuit\char22{}that is, a quantum circuit consisting solely of controlled-NOT (CNOT), Hadamard, and phase gates\char22{}can be simulated efficiently on a classical computer. This paper improves that theorem in several directions. First, by removing the need for Gaussian elimination, we make the simulation algorithm much faster at the cost of a factor of 2 increase in the number of bits needed to represent a state. We have implemented the imp

title: Improved Techniques for Training GANs
searchable_text:
Improved Techniques for Training GANs Semi-supervised learning and visually realistic image generation with GANs. New architectural features and training procedures applied to the GAN framework. Compared with prior GAN work, it adds a variety of new architectural features and training procedures. TITLE: Improved Techniques for Training GANs

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a variety of new architectural features and training procedures that we apply to the generative adversarial networks (GANs) framework. We focus on two applications of GANs: semi-supervised learning, and the generation of images that humans find visually realistic. Unlike most work on generative models, our primary goal is not to train a model that assigns high likelihood to test data, nor do we require the model to be able to learn well without using any labels. Using our new techniques

title: Improved Variational Inference with Inverse Autoregressive Flow
searchable_text:
Improved Variational Inference with Inverse Autoregressive Flow Flexible variational inference of posteriors over latent variables, especially in high-dimensional latent spaces and variational autoencoders for natural images. A chain of invertible normalizing-flow transformations based on autoregressive neural networks, forming inverse autoregressive flow for flexible variational posterior inference. Unlike earlier published flows, IAF uses inverse autoregressive transformations that scale well to high-dimensional latent spaces. TITLE: Improved Variational Inference with Inverse Autoregressive Flow

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The framework of normalizing flows provides a general strategy for flexible variational inference of posteriors over latent variables. We propose a new type of normalizing flow, inverse autoregressive flow (IAF), that, in contrast to earlier published flows, scales well to high-dimensional latent spaces. The proposed flow consists of a chain of invertible transformations, where each transformation is based on an autoregressive neural network. In experiments, we show that

title: Improving Neural Language Models with a Continuous Cache
searchable_text:
Improving Neural Language Models with a Continuous Cache Neural network language modeling that adapts word prediction to recent history across language model datasets. Stores past hidden activations as memory and retrieves them via dot product with the current hidden activation to adapt neural language model predictions to recent history. Replaces more complex memory-augmented networks with a simplified continuous cache using past hidden activations, while connecting neural external memory to count-based cache language models. TITLE: Improving Neural Language Models with a Continuous Cache

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose an extension to neural network language models to adapt their prediction to the recent history. Our model is a simplified version of memory augmented networks, which stores past hidden activations as memory and accesses them through a dot product with the current hidden activation. This mechanism is very efficient and scales to very large memory sizes. We also draw a link between the use of external memory in neural network and cache models used with count based langu

title: In-datacenter performance analysis of a tensor processing unit
searchable_text:
In-datacenter performance analysis of a tensor processing unit Datacenter neural-network inference workloads, including production MLPs, CNNs, and LSTMs that dominate Google datacenter inference demand. Custom inference ASIC with a 65,536 8-bit MAC matrix unit, 28 MiB software-managed on-chip memory, and deterministic execution for latency-sensitive neural-network inference. Compared with deployed Intel Haswell CPUs and Nvidia K80 GPUs, it replaced general-purpose, time-varying optimization hardware with a small, low-power domain-specific ASIC for deterministic NN inference. TITLE: In-datacenter performance analysis of a tensor processing unit

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many architects believe that major improvements in cost-energy-performance must now come from domain-specific hardware. This paper evaluates a custom ASIC-called a Tensor Processing Unit (TPU)-deployed in datacenters since 2015 that accelerates the inference phase of neural networks (NN). The heart of the TPU is a 65,536 8-bit MAC matrix multiply unit that offers a peak throughput of 92 TeraOps/second (TOPS) and a large (28 MiB) software-managed on-chip memory. The TPU's d

title: Inclusive search for standard model Higgs boson production in the WW decay channel using the CDF II detector.
searchable_text:
Inclusive search for standard model Higgs boson production in the WW decay channel using the CDF II detector. Standard model Higgs boson production in the WW decay channel for Higgs masses from 110 to 200 GeV. Inclusive CDF II search using 4.8 fb(-1) of 1.96 TeV pp data across significant Higgs production modes with decays to two W bosons.  TITLE: Inclusive search for standard model Higgs boson production in the WW decay channel using the CDF II detector.

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a search for standard model (SM) Higgs boson production using pp collision data at square root(s) = 1.96 TeV, collected with the CDF II detector and corresponding to an integrated luminosity of 4.8 fb(-1). We search for Higgs bosons produced in all processes with a significant production rate and decaying to two W bosons. We find no evidence for SM Higgs boson production and place upper limits at the 95% confidence

title: Incremental Learning Through Deep Adaptation
searchable_text:
Incremental Learning Through Deep Adaptation Incremental learning of new capabilities or domains in existing neural networks without degrading performance on previously learned domains. Deep Adaptation Modules constrain newly learned filters to be linear combinations of existing filters in a trained neural network. Instead of joint training, full fine-tuning, or adding a full network per domain, DAM adds compact adaptation modules built from existing filters. TITLE: Incremental Learning Through Deep Adaptation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Given an existing trained neural network, it is often desirable to learn new capabilities without hindering performance of those already learned. Existing approaches either learn sub-optimal solutions, require joint training, or incur a substantial increment in the number of parameters for each added domain, typically as many as the original network. We propose a method called Deep Adaptation Modules (DAM) that constrains newly learned filters to be linear combinations of existing ones. DAM

title: Inductive Conformal Martingales for Change-Point Detection
searchable_text:
Inductive Conformal Martingales for Change-Point Detection Quickest change-point detection in data streams under general applied conditions where pre- and post-change distributions are not precisely known. Use Inductive Conformal Martingales for change-point detection, relying only on independent and identically distributed observations rather than a known change-point model. Changed from model-specific optimal procedures such as CUSUM, Shiryaev-Roberts, and Posterior Probability statistics to an ICM approach requiring only IID observations. TITLE: Inductive Conformal Martingales for Change-Point Detection

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider the problem of quickest change-point detection in data streams. Classical change-point detection procedures, such as CUSUM, Shiryaev-Roberts and Posterior Probability statistics, are optimal only if the change-point model is known, which is an unrealistic assumption in typical applied problems. title: Inductive Conformal Prediction: Theory and Application to Neural Networks
searchable_text:
Inductive Conformal Prediction: Theory and Application to Neural Networks Risk-sensitive pattern recognition applications needing per-prediction confidence, including medical diagnosis, financial analysis, and human-computer interaction. Inductive conformal prediction for attaching valid confidence information to neural-network predictions. Shifts from Bayesian/PAC-style confidence estimates toward inductive conformal prediction with confidence values intended to bound expected error rates. TITLE: Inductive Conformal Prediction: Theory and Application to Neural Networks

YEAR: 2008

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Traditional machine learning algorithms for pattern recognition just output simple predictions, without any associated confidence values. Confidence values are an indication of how likely each prediction is of being correct. In the ideal case, a confidence of 99% or higher for all examples in a set, means that the percentage of erroneous predictions in that set will not exceed 1%. Knowing the likelihood of each prediction enables us to assess the extent to which

title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs
searchable_text:
Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs Inductive node representation learning for large graphs, especially prediction on previously unseen nodes and unseen graphs. Learn a function that generates node embeddings by sampling and aggregating feature information from each node's local neighborhood. Replaces transductive per-node embedding training with an inductive embedding-generating function using node features and sampled neighborhood aggregation. TITLE: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. title: Inference in generative models using the Wasserstein distance
searchable_text:
Inference in generative models using the Wasserstein distance Parameter inference for purely generative models where simulation is possible but likelihood evaluation may be unavailable. Estimate parameters by comparing observed and synthetic empirical distributions with Wasserstein distances, extending to dependent data via reconstructions and to large datasets via Hilbert space-filling curves. Moved Wasserstein inference from mainly theoretical interest to practical point estimators and quasi-Bayesian distributions enabled by numerical transport advances. TITLE: Inference in generative models using the Wasserstein distance

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In purely generative models, one can simulate data given parameters but not necessarily evaluate the likelihood. We use Wasserstein distances between empirical distributions of observed data and empirical distributions of synthetic data drawn from such models to estimate their parameters. title: Inferring Algorithmic Patterns with Stack-Augmented Recurrent Nets
searchable_text:
Inferring Algorithmic Patterns with Stack-Augmented Recurrent Nets Algorithmically generated sequence prediction problems requiring counting and sequence memorization. A recurrent network augmented with trainable memory to grow model complexity in a structured way for sequence learning. Moves beyond standard recurrent networks by adding trainable memory, enabling learning of algorithmic patterns requiring counting and memorization. TITLE: Inferring Algorithmic Patterns with Stack-Augmented Recurrent Nets

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Despite the recent achievements in machine learning, we are still very far from achieving real artificial intelligence. In this paper, we discuss the limitations of standard deep learning approaches and show that some of these limitations can be overcome by learning how to grow the complexity of a model in a structured way. Specifically, we study the simplest sequence prediction problems that are beyond the scope of what is learnable with standard recurrent networks, a

title: INFLUENCE OF STELLAR MULTIPLICITY ON PLANET FORMATION. IV. ADAPTIVE OPTICS IMAGING OF KEPLER STARS WITH MULTIPLE TRANSITING PLANET CANDIDATES
searchable_text:

ADAPTIVE OPTICS IMAGING OF KEPLER STARS WITH MULTIPLE TRANSITING PLANET CANDIDATES Stellar multiplicity among Kepler multiple transiting planet systems and its relationship to multi-planet formation and orbital architecture. DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Kepler mission provides a wealth of multiple transiting planet systems (MTPSs). The formation and evolution of multi-planet systems are likely to be influenced by companion stars given the abundance of multiple stellar systems. We study the influence of stellar companions by measuring the stellar multiplicity rate of MTPSs. We select 138 bright (KP < 13.5) Kepler MTPSs and search for stellar

title: Information geometry connecting Wasserstein distance and Kullback–Leibler divergence via the entropy-relaxed transportation problem
searchable_text:
Information geometry connecting Wasserstein distance and Kullback–Leibler divergence via the entropy-relaxed transportation problem Geometry of discrete probability distributions connecting Wasserstein distance and Kullback–Leibler divergence on the probability simplex. Entropy-relaxed optimal transportation plans are given an information-geometric divergence structure, then projected into the probability simplex as a one-parameter divergence family. Compared with Cuturi’s entropy-relaxed transport, this work introduces divergence geometry over optimal transportation plans and derives simplex divergences related to both Wasserstein and KL. ABSTRACT:
Two geometrical structures have been extensively studied for a manifold of probability distributions. One is based on the Fisher information metric, which is invariant under reversible transformations of random variables, while the other is based on the Wasserstein distance of optimal transportation, which reflects the structure of the distance between underlying random variables. title: Initial Conditions to Cosmological N-Body Simulations, or, How to Run an Ensemble of Simulations
searchable_text:
Initial Conditions to Cosmological N-Body Simulations, or, How to Run an Ensemble of Simulations Initial conditions for cosmological N-body simulations, especially preserving real-space statistical properties in finite simulation boxes. Generate N-body initial conditions that model real-space statistics while allowing nonzero DC-mode power, requiring ensembles of simulations. Unlike the conventional method, it does not assume zero DC-mode power and targets accurate real-space statistics rather than biased finite-box estimates. TITLE: Initial Conditions to Cosmological N-Body Simulations, or, How to Run an Ensemble of Simulations

ABSTRACT:
The conventional method of generating initial conditions for cosmological N-body simulations introduces a significant error in the real-space statistical properties of the particles. More specifically, the finite box size leads to a significant underestimate of σ8, the correlation function, and nonlinear effects. title: Integrative analysis of 111 reference human epigenomes
searchable_text:
Integrative analysis of 111 reference human epigenomes Reference epigenomic resource for interpreting gene regulation, cellular differentiation, disease-associated variation and the molecular basis of human disease across primary human cells and tissues. Integrative analysis of 111 reference human epigenomes profiled across histone modifications, DNA accessibility, DNA methylation and RNA expression to map regulatory elements, modules, activators and repressors. Moved from no comparable epigenomic reference to the largest collection so far: 111 primary-cell and tissue epigenomes with multi-assay regulatory annotations. TITLE: Integrative analysis of 111 reference human epigenomes

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The reference human genome sequence set the stage for studies of genetic variation and its association with human disease, but epigenomic studies lack a similar reference. To address this need, the NIH Roadmap Epigenomics Consortium generated the largest collection so far of human epigenomes for primary cells and tissues. Here we describe the integrative analysis of 111 reference human epigenomes generated as part of the programme, profiled for histone modification patterns, DNA a

title: Interaction Networks for Learning about Objects, Relations and Physics
searchable_text:
Interaction Networks for Learning about Objects, Relations and Physics Reasoning and prediction for objects, relations, and physics in complex physical systems including n-body, rigid-body collision, and non-rigid dynamics. Graph-input interaction networks perform object- and relation-centric reasoning analogous to simulation, implemented with deep neural networks. Introduces the first general-purpose learnable physics engine based on interaction networks for object- and relation-centric reasoning. TITLE: Interaction Networks for Learning about Objects, Relations and Physics

YEAR: 2016

ABSTRACT:
Reasoning about objects, relations, and physics is central to human intelligence, and a key goal of artificial intelligence. Here we introduce the interaction network, a model which can reason about how objects in complex systems interact, supporting dynamical predictions, as well as inferences about the abstract properties of the system. Our model takes graphs as input, performs object- and relation-centric reasoning in a way that is analogous to a simulation, and

title: Intercomparison of machine learning methods for statistical downscaling: the case of daily and extreme precipitation
searchable_text:
Intercomparison of machine learning methods for statistical downscaling: the case of daily and extreme precipitation Statistical downscaling of GCM daily and extreme precipitation to study local climate change effects. Compare Perfect Prognosis regressions, SVM, MSSL, autoencoder neural networks, plus a hybrid BCSD residual model using MSSL for statistical downscaling. Added recent machine learning methods and introduced a hybrid Model Output Statistics plus Perfect Prognosis approach modeling BCSD residuals with MSSL. TITLE: Intercomparison of machine learning methods for statistical downscaling: the case of daily and extreme precipitation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Statistical downscaling of Global Climate Models (GCMs) allows researchers to study local climate change effects decades into the future. A wide range of statistical models have been applied to downscaling GCMs but recent advances in machine learning have not been explored compared to traditional approaches. In this paper, we compare five Perfect Prognosis (PP) approaches, Ordinary Least Squares, Elastic-Net, and Suppor

title: Interface dynamics in planar neural field models
searchable_text:
Interface dynamics in planar neural field models Pattern formation and interface dynamics of high- versus low-activity regions in two-dimensional neural field models, including spots, rings, stripes, fronts, and adaptation-driven instabilities. Derive interface equations for planar neural fields with Heaviside firing rates, expressing normal velocity via non-local Biot-Savart-type boundary interactions, plus linear stability/adaptation analysis. Replaced direct simulation of the full nonlinear integral neural field with exact, dimensionally reduced boundary-interface dynamics and stability analysis. TITLE: Interface dynamics in planar neural field models

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Neural field models describe the coarse-grained activity of populations of interacting neurons. Because of the laminar structure of real cortical tissue they are often studied in two spatial dimensions, where they are well known to generate rich patterns of spatiotemporal activity. Such patterns have been interpreted in a variety of contexts ranging from the understanding of visual hallucinations to the generation of electroencephalographic signals. Typical patterns include localized solutions in

title: Interpretable Learning for Self-Driving Cars by Visualizing Causal Attention
searchable_text:
Interpretable Learning for Self-Driving Cars by Visualizing Causal Attention Explainable visual rationales for self-driving car perception/control networks predicting steering from driving images. Train an end-to-end visual-attention CNN for steering, then causally filter highlighted image regions to keep only regions that actually influence steering output. Moves beyond attention-only visualization by adding a causal filtering step that distinguishes merely highlighted regions from regions that actually influence steering. TITLE: Interpretable Learning for Self-Driving Cars by Visualizing Causal Attention

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep neural perception and control networks are likely to be a key component of self-driving vehicles. These models need to be explainable - they should provide easy-tointerpret rationales for their behavior - so that passengers, insurance companies, law enforcement, developers etc., can understand what triggered a particular behavior. Here we explore the use of visual explanations. These explanations take the form of real-time highlighted regions of an image

title: Intriguing properties of neural networks
searchable_text:
Intriguing properties of neural networks Understanding semantic representations and input-output robustness in deep neural networks for speech and visual recognition tasks. Analyze high-level units and random linear combinations, and find imperceptible perturbations by maximizing the network's prediction error to test misclassification and transfer across networks. Introduced comparison of individual units with random linear combinations and demonstrated transferable imperceptible perturbations found by maximizing prediction error. TITLE: Intriguing properties of neural networks

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep neural networks are highly expressive models that have recently achieved state of the art performance on speech and visual recognition tasks. While their expressiveness is the reason they succeed, it also causes them to learn uninterpretable solutions that could have counter-intuitive properties. In this paper we report two such properties. 
First, we find that there is no distinction between individual high level units and random linear combinations of high level units, according to vario

title: Intrinsic Motivation and Automatic Curricula via Asymmetric Self-Play
searchable_text:
Intrinsic Motivation and Automatic Curricula via Asymmetric Self-Play Unsupervised environment exploration and pretraining for reinforcement-learning agents in nearly reversible or resettable environments. Two versions of one agent play asymmetric self-play: Alice generates tasks through action sequences, and Bob learns by undoing or repeating them under a reward structure that creates an automatic curriculum.  TITLE: Intrinsic Motivation and Automatic Curricula via Asymmetric Self-Play

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We describe a simple scheme that allows an agent to learn about its environment in an unsupervised manner. Our scheme pits two versions of the same agent, Alice and Bob, against one another. Alice proposes a task for Bob to complete; and then Bob attempts to complete the task. In this work we will focus on two kinds of environments: (nearly) reversible environments and environments that can be reset. Alice will "propose" the task by doing a sequence of actions and t

title: IQA: Visual Question Answering in Interactive Environments
searchable_text:
IQA: Visual Question Answering in Interactive Environments Interactive Question Answering: answering visual questions that require an autonomous agent to navigate and manipulate objects in dynamic indoor scenes. Hierarchical Interactive Memory Network: a factorized set of controllers operating at multiple temporal abstraction levels for question-conditioned navigation, perception, interaction, and action planning. Replaces single-controller reinforcement learning baselines with a hierarchical, factorized controller architecture and introduces IQUAD V1 for evaluating interactive visual question answering. TITLE: IQA: Visual Question Answering in Interactive Environments

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce Interactive Question Answering (IQA), the task of answering questions that require an autonomous agent to interact with a dynamic visual environment. IQA presents the agent with a scene and a question, like: "Are there any apples in the fridge?" The agent must navigate around the scene, acquire visual understanding of scene elements, interact with objects (e.g. open refrigerators) and plan for a series of actions conditioned on the question. Popular reinforcement

title: ITEM2VEC: Neural item embedding for collaborative filtering
searchable_text:
ITEM2VEC: Neural item embedding for collaborative filtering Item-based collaborative filtering: modeling item-item relations to produce item similarities, including cases without user information. Use SGNS/word2vec-style neural embedding to learn latent item vectors for item-based collaborative filtering. Recasts item-based collaborative filtering in the neural word-embedding framework, replacing words with items to infer item-item relations. TITLE: ITEM2VEC: Neural item embedding for collaborative filtering

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many Collaborative Filtering (CF) algorithms are item-based in the sense that they analyze item-item relations in order to produce item similarities. Recently, several works in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP) suggested to learn a latent representation of words using neural embedding algorithms. Among them, the Skip-gram with Negative Sampling (SGNS), also known as word2vec, was shown to provide state-of-the-art results on various linguistics tasks. In this pape

title: Iterative Bregman Projections for Regularized Transportation Problems
searchable_text:
Iterative Bregman Projections for Regularized Transportation Problems Regularized numerical approximation of linear programs related to optimal transport, including barycenters, tomographic reconstruction, multi-marginal transport, unbalanced transport, and capacity-constrained transport. Entropically regularize optimal-transport linear programs as KL Bregman projections, then solve via iterative Bregman projections or Bregman-Dykstra over decomposed simple constraints. Replaces direct solution of the initial optimal-transport linear program with entropic regularization formulated as KL Bregman projection and decomposed iterative projections. TITLE: Iterative Bregman Projections for Regularized Transportation Problems

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This article details a general numerical framework to approximate so-lutions to linear programs related to optimal transport. The general idea is to introduce an entropic regularization of the initial linear program. This regularized problem corresponds to a Kullback-Leibler Bregman di-vergence projection of a vector (representing some initial joint distribu-tion) on the polytope of constraints. We show that for many problems related to optimal transport, the set of

title: Jet-images — deep learning edition
searchable_text:
Jet-images — deep learning edition Identifying highly boosted W bosons in particle-physics jet images. Train modern deep learning architectures on jet images, treating particle physics detectors as cameras and jets as images, with visualization methods for learned features. Replaces hand-designed, physically motivated jet-tagging features with supervised deep learning trained directly on jet images. TITLE: Jet-images — deep learning edition

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Building on the notion of a particle physics detector as a camera and the collimated streams of high energy particles, or jets, it measures as an image, we investigate the potential of machine learning techniques based on deep learning architectures to identify highly boosted W bosons. Modern deep learning algorithms trained on jet images can out-perform standard physically-motivated feature driven approaches to jet tagging. We develop techniques for visualizing how these features are learned by the

title: Jet Substructure at the Large Hadron Collider
searchable_text:
Jet Substructure at the Large Hadron Collider Jet substructure at the Large Hadron Collider, especially calculations, simulated-event phenomenology, heavy-particle searches with jets, and jet-analysis software. Apply soft-collinear effective theory for factorization/resummation, use jet pruning for substructure searches, and implement/compare jet analyses in SpartyJet. Greatly expands earlier jet-pruning material and adds broader simulated-event phenomenology, software overview, detailed calculations, and software examples. TITLE: Jet Substructure at the Large Hadron Collider

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
I explore many aspects of jet substructure at the Large Hadron Collider, ranging from theoretical techniques for jet calculations, to phenomenological tools for better searches with jets, to software for implementing and comparing such tools. I begin with an application of soft-collinear effective theory, an effective theory of QCD applied to high-energy quarks and gluons. This material is taken from Ref. 1, in which we demonstrate factorization and logarithmic resummation for a certain cl

title: Kepler-16: A Transiting Circumbinary Planet
searchable_text:
Kepler-16: A Transiting Circumbinary Planet Detection and characterization of a Saturn-like planet orbiting an eclipsing pair of low-mass stars. Use Kepler photometry of planetary transits across both stars plus stellar mutual eclipses to constrain the dimensions and orbital architecture of a three-body circumbinary system. The reported exoplanet is not orbiting a single star but surrounds a pair of stars, with transits observed across both stellar components. TITLE: Kepler-16: A Transiting Circumbinary Planet

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
An exoplanet has been observed, comparable in size and mass to Saturn, that orbits a pair of stars. We report the detection of a planet whose orbit surrounds a pair of low-mass stars. Data from the Kepler spacecraft reveal transits of the planet across both stars, in addition to the mutual eclipses of the stars, giving precise constraints on the absolute dimensions of all three bodies. The planet is comparable to Saturn in mass and size and is on a nearly circular 229-day orbit around its tw

title: Kepler Presearch Data Conditioning I—Architecture and Algorithms for Error Correction in Kepler Light Curves
searchable_text:
Kepler Presearch Data Conditioning I—Architecture and Algorithms for Error Correction in Kepler Light Curves Error correction for Kepler stellar light curves before transit searches and stellar activity or rotation analysis. Bayesian removal of systematics in a redesigned PDC module to correct Kepler light-curve errors while preserving transits and astrophysical signals. Replaced the original PDC with SOC 8.0’s completely new Bayesian systematics-correction architecture and algorithms. TITLE: Kepler Presearch Data Conditioning I—Architecture and Algorithms for Error Correction in Kepler Light Curves

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Kepler provides light curves of 156,000 stars with unprecedented precision. However, the raw data as they come from the spacecraft contain significant systematic and stochastic errors. These errors, which include discontinuities, systematic trends, and outliers, obscure the astrophysical signals in the light curves. To correct these errors is the task of the Presearch Data Conditioning (PDC) module of the Kepler data analysis p

title: Know-Evolve: Deep Temporal Reasoning for Dynamic Knowledge Graphs
searchable_text:
Know-Evolve: Deep Temporal Reasoning for Dynamic Knowledge Graphs Temporal reasoning in dynamically evolving knowledge graphs with timestamped edges, including predicting occurrence or recurrence times of facts. Learns non-linearly evolving entity representations over time and models each fact occurrence as a multivariate point process with intensity modulated by embedding-based fact scores. Compared with prior multi-relational reasoning approaches, it adds deep evolutionary entity embeddings and point-process modeling to predict fact timing, not only relational plausibility. TITLE: Know-Evolve: Deep Temporal Reasoning for Dynamic Knowledge Graphs

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The availability of large scale event data with time stamps has given rise to dynamically evolving knowledge graphs that contain temporal information for each edge. Reasoning over time in such dynamic knowledge graphs is not yet well understood. To this end, we present Know-Evolve, a novel deep evolutionary knowledge network that learns non-linearly evolving entity representations over time. The occurrence of a fact (edge) is modeled as a multivariate point process whos

title: Large-Scale Evolution of Image Classifiers
searchable_text:
Large-Scale Evolution of Image Classifiers Automatic neural network architecture design for image classification on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100. Large-scale evolutionary architecture search using simple evolutionary techniques and novel mutation operators, starting from trivial initial conditions to automatically produce fully trained image classifiers. Compared with earlier neural architecture design, it scales simple evolutionary techniques to unprecedented levels and removes human participation once evolution starts. TITLE: Large-Scale Evolution of Image Classifiers

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Neural networks have proven effective at solving difficult problems but designing their architectures can be challenging, even for image classification problems alone. Our goal is to minimize human participation, so we employ evolutionary algorithms to discover such networks automatically. Despite significant computational requirements, we show that it is now possible to evolve models with accuracies within the range of those published in the last year. Specifically, we employ simple evolutio

title: Large Scale Optimal Transport and Mapping Estimation
searchable_text:
Large Scale Optimal Transport and Mapping Estimation Learning an optimal map from one distribution to another, with applications to domain adaptation and generative modeling. Two-step method: learn a regularized optimal transport plan via stochastic dual optimization, then train a deep neural network Monge map by approximating its barycentric projection. Replaces a prior large-sample OT approach with a stochastic dual regularized OT estimator and adds neural barycentric-projection learning for generalizable Monge maps. TITLE: Large Scale Optimal Transport and Mapping Estimation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents a novel two-step approach for the fundamental problem of learning an optimal map from one distribution to another. First, we learn an optimal transport (OT) plan, which can be thought as a one-to-many map between the two distributions. To that end, we propose a stochastic dual approach of regularized OT, and show empirically that it scales better than a recent related approach when the amount of samples is very large. Second, we estimate a \textit{Monge map} as a

title: Large-scale probabilistic predictors with and without guarantees of validity
searchable_text:
Large-scale probabilistic predictors with and without guarantees of validity Large-scale probabilistic prediction requiring calibrated probabilities for machine-learning outputs. Turns machine-learning algorithms into computationally efficient probabilistic predictors with automatic validity, producing imprecise probabilities that can be merged into precise probabilities. Compared with existing methods, it introduces valid imprecise probabilistic predictors and a merge-to-precise variant that improves empirical accuracy while sacrificing guaranteed calibration. TITLE: Large-scale probabilistic predictors with and without guarantees of validity

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper studies theoretically and empirically a method of turning machine-learning algorithms into probabilistic predictors that automatically enjoys a property of validity (perfect calibration) and is computationally efficient. The price to pay for perfect calibration is that these probabilistic predictors produce imprecise (in practice, almost precise for large data sets) probabilities. When these imprecise probabilities are merged into precise probabili

title: Large-scale Simple Question Answering with Memory Networks
searchable_text:
Large-scale Simple Question Answering with Memory Networks Large-scale simple question answering where answering is easy if the system retrieves the correct evidence for a question. Apply multitask and transfer learning within the Memory Networks framework, combining a new 100k-question dataset with existing benchmarks for large-scale simple question answering. Introduces a new 100k-question dataset and studies multitask and transfer learning for simple QA using Memory Networks with existing benchmarks. TITLE: Large-scale Simple Question Answering with Memory Networks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Training large-scale question answering systems is complicated because training sources usually cover a small portion of the range of possible questions. This paper studies the impact of multitask and transfer learning for simple question answering; a setting for which the reasoning required to answer is quite easy, as long as one can retrieve the correct evidence given a question, which can be difficult in large-scale conditions. To this end, we introduce a new dataset of 100

title: Latent Predictor Networks for Code Generation
searchable_text:
Latent Predictor Networks for Code Generation Generating programming code from mixed natural language and structured specifications. Neural sequence generator conditioned on arbitrary input functions, marginalising conditioning context and generation granularity. Moves beyond fixed conditioning or granularity by allowing both conditioning context choice and generation granularity to be marginalised. TITLE: Latent Predictor Networks for Code Generation

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many language generation tasks require the production of text conditioned on both structured and unstructured inputs. We present a novel neural network architecture which generates an output sequence conditioned on an arbitrary number of input functions. Crucially, our approach allows both the choice of conditioning context and the granularity of generation, for example characters or tokens, to be marginalised, thus permitting scalable and effective training.
