title: Positivity and Transportation
searchable_text:
Positivity and Transportation Kernel computation for integral histograms r and c of equal sum using weighted transportation matrices. Positive definite transportation kernel computed by sampling Northwestern corner vertices of the transportation polytope instead of enumerating all integral transportation matrices. Replaces consideration of all matrices in the transportation polytope with a sub-sample of vertices known as Northwestern corner solutions. TITLE: Positivity and Transportation

YEAR: 2012

ABSTRACT:
We prove in this paper that the weighted volume of the set of integral transportation matrices between two integral histograms r and c of equal sum is a positive definite kernel of r and c when the set of considered weights forms a positive definite matrix. The computation of this quantity, despite being the subject of a significant research effort in algebraic statistics, remains an intractable challenge for histograms of even modest dimensions. title: PowerPlay: Training an Increasingly General Problem Solver by Continually Searching for the Simplest Still Unsolvable Problem
searchable_text:
PowerPlay: Training an Increasingly General Problem Solver by Continually Searching for the Simplest Still Unsolvable Problem Unsupervised training of increasingly general problem solvers over computable tasks with possibly computable solutions. Continually search pairs of self-invented tasks and solver modifications, validating the first compact/fast pair where the modified solver solves all old tasks plus a new one that its predecessor cannot. Instead of solving given tasks, PowerPlay automatically invents the simplest still-unsolvable task and corresponding solver modification while preserving all prior learned abilities. TITLE: PowerPlay: Training an Increasingly General Problem Solver by Continually Searching for the Simplest Still Unsolvable Problem

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most of computer science focuses on automatically solving given computational problems. I focus on automatically inventing or discovering problems in a way inspired by the playful behavior of animals and humans, to train a more and more general problem solver from scratch in an unsupervised fashion. title: Practical Block-Wise Neural Network Architecture Generation
searchable_text:
Practical Block-Wise Neural Network Architecture Generation Automatic generation of high-performance convolutional neural network architectures for image classification, reducing reliance on expert hand-crafted design. BlockQNN uses Q-learning with epsilon-greedy exploration to sequentially choose component layers, learn an optimal network block, and stack that block into a full CNN. Compared with prior auto-generated networks, it searches for reusable blocks rather than whole architectures, shrinking the search space while improving CIFAR-10 performance. TITLE: Practical Block-Wise Neural Network Architecture Generation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Convolutional neural networks have gained a remarkable success in computer vision. However, most usable network architectures are hand-crafted and usually require expertise and elaborate design. In this paper, we provide a block-wise network generation pipeline called BlockQNN which automatically builds high-performance networks using the Q-Learning paradigm with epsilon-greedy exploration strategy. title: Practical Quantum Error Mitigation for Near-Future Applications
searchable_text:
Practical Quantum Error Mitigation for Near-Future Applications Reducing output errors in near-future quantum devices that lack resources for full fault tolerance, including SWAP test circuits up to 19 qubits. Systematically measure error effects, then design efficient QEM circuits using single-qubit Clifford gate insertion/replacement, measurements, and an exponential extrapolation variant. Compared with prior error extrapolation and quasi-probability decomposition, it accounts for imperfect error-model knowledge, adds a measurement protocol, and introduces exponential extrapolation. TITLE: Practical Quantum Error Mitigation for Near-Future Applications

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
It is vital to minimise the impact of errors for near-future quantum devices that will lack the resources for full fault tolerance. Two quantum error mitigation (QEM) techniques have been introduced recently, namely error extrapolation [Li 2017,Temme 2017] and quasi-probability decomposition [Temme 2017]. title: Practical Weak-lensing Shear Measurement with Metacalibration
searchable_text:
Practical Weak-lensing Shear Measurement with Metacalibration Accurate weak gravitational lensing shear measurement from available imaging data without galaxy priors or simulation-based calibration. Distort observed images by a small known shear, measure estimator response, and correct shear response, selection biases, and low-SNR correlated-noise bias. Extends prior metacalibration beyond moderate high-SNR simulations without significant selection effects by adding selection-bias correction, shear-response formalism, and empirical correlated-noise correction. TITLE: Practical Weak-lensing Shear Measurement with Metacalibration

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Metacalibration is a recently introduced method to accurately measure weak gravitational lensing shear using only the available imaging data, without need for prior information about galaxy properties or calibration from simulations. The method involves distorting the image with a small known shear, and calculating the response of a shear estimator to that applied shear. title: Preconditioned quantum linear system algorithm.
searchable_text:
Preconditioned quantum linear system algorithm. Quantum linear-system solving for arbitrary problem specifications, including electromagnetic scattering cross sections of arbitrary targets. Generalize HHL with generic state preparation, ancilla-measurement observables, and a quantum-compatible preconditioner for broader linear-system problem specifications. Compared with Harrow et al. (2009), it generalizes the algorithm to arbitrary problem specifications and adds generic initialization, ancilla readout, and preconditioning. TITLE: Preconditioned quantum linear system algorithm.

YEAR: 2013

ABSTRACT:
We describe a quantum algorithm that generalizes the quantum linear system algorithm [Harrow et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 103, 150502 (2009)] to arbitrary problem specifications. title: Predicting Surgery Targets in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy through Structural Connectome Based Simulations
searchable_text:
Predicting Surgery Targets in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy through Structural Connectome Based Simulations Predicting surgical targets and post-surgery seizure likelihood for individuals with drug-resistant left temporal lobe epilepsy. Apply a computational epilepsy model to patient-specific DTI structural connectomes, simulate seizure onset, then perform in silico resections by removing network nodes. Moves from predefined routine clinical resections toward individualized, model-prompted resection areas selected through patient-specific structural connectome simulations. TITLE: Predicting Surgery Targets in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy through Structural Connectome Based Simulations

ABSTRACT:
Temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) is a prevalent neurological disorder resulting in disruptive seizures. In the case of drug resistant epilepsy resective surgery is often considered. This is a procedure hampered by unpredictable success rates, with many patients continuing to have seizures even after surgery. title: Premise Selection for Mathematics by Corpus Analysis and Kernel Methods
searchable_text:
Premise Selection for Mathematics by Corpus Analysis and Kernel Methods Smart premise selection for automated reasoning in large-theory formal mathematical proof development. Fine-grained proof dependency analysis builds training data, combined with a kernel-method machine learning algorithm for premise selection. Adds fine-grained dependency-derived knowledge and a new kernel-method premise selector, evaluated on an extension of the older MPTP Challenge benchmark. ABSTRACT:
Smart premise selection is essential when using automated reasoning as a tool for large-theory formal proof development. This work develops learning-based premise selection in two ways. title: Prevalence of Earth-size planets orbiting Sun-like stars
searchable_text:
Prevalence of Earth-size planets orbiting Sun-like stars Estimating how common Earth-size planets in habitable zones are around Sun-like stars. Survey Kepler brightness measurements of 42,000 Sun-like stars for periodic transit dimmings, then inject synthetic planet-caused dimmings to measure detectability and correct occurrence estimates.  TITLE: Prevalence of Earth-size planets orbiting Sun-like stars

ABSTRACT:
Significance A major question is whether planets suitable for biochemistry are common or rare in the universe. Small rocky planets with liquid water enjoy key ingredients for biology. We used the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Kepler telescope to survey 42,000 Sun-like stars for periodic dimmings that occur when a planet crosses in front of its host star. title: Principal Geodesic Analysis for Probability Measures under the Optimal Transport Metric
searchable_text:
Principal Geodesic Analysis for Probability Measures under the Optimal Transport Metric Summarizing families of probability measures on a Hilbert space by identifying efficient representative curves in probability-measure space. Principal geodesic analysis of probability measures under Wasserstein geometry, using Wasserstein means, differential-geometric analogues of PCA concepts, relaxed geodesics, and regularized optimal transport distances. Recasts PCA-style centering, orthogonality, and principal directions from Euclidean geometry into optimal transport geometry over probability measures. TITLE: Principal Geodesic Analysis for Probability Measures under the Optimal Transport Metric

ABSTRACT:
Given a family of probability measures in P(X), the space of probability measures on a Hilbert space X, our goal in this paper is to highlight one ore more curves in P(X) that summarize efficiently that family. We propose to study this problem under the optimal transport (Wasserstein) geometry, using curves that are restricted to be geodesic segments under that metric. title: Prio: Private, Robust, and Scalable Computation of Aggregate Statistics
searchable_text:
Prio: Private, Robust, and Scalable Computation of Aggregate Statistics Privacy-preserving collection and computation of aggregate statistics over many clients’ private data values. Clients secret-share private values to multiple servers, which compute aggregates using secret-shared non-interactive proofs to ensure validity while preserving privacy if one server is honest. Replaces conventional zero-knowledge validation with secret-shared non-interactive proofs and extends classic private aggregation to broader statistics such as high-dimensional least-squares regression. TITLE: Prio: Private, Robust, and Scalable Computation of Aggregate Statistics

YEAR: 2017

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents Prio, a privacy-preserving system for the collection of aggregate statistics. Each Prio client holds a private data value (e.g., its current location), and a small set of servers compute statistical functions over the values of all clients (e.g., the most popular location). As long as at least one server is honest, the Prio servers learn nearly nothing about the clients' private data, except what they can infer from the aggregate statistics tha

title: Privacy Risk in Machine Learning: Analyzing the Connection to Overfitting
searchable_text:
Privacy Risk in Machine Learning: Analyzing the Connection to Overfitting Privacy leakage from machine learning models trained on sensitive data via training set membership inference and attribute inference. Formal and empirical analysis of how overfitting and influence affect membership inference and attribute inference attacks on machine learning models. Moved beyond anecdotal links between overfitting, influence, and privacy leakage by giving formal and empirical analyses and deriving new attacks connecting membership and attribute inference. TITLE: Privacy Risk in Machine Learning: Analyzing the Connection to Overfitting

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Machine learning algorithms, when applied to sensitive data, pose a distinct threat to privacy. A growing body of prior work demonstrates that models produced by these algorithms may leak specific private information in the training data to an attacker, either through the models' structure or their observable behavior. However, the underlying cause of this privacy risk is not well understood beyond a handful of anecdotal accounts that suggest overfitting and inf

title: Private federated learning on vertically partitioned data via entity resolution and additively homomorphic encryption
searchable_text:
Private federated learning on vertically partitioned data via entity resolution and additively homomorphic encryption Joint linear-model learning for two providers with vertically split private feature sets about common but unlinked entities, where only one provider has labels. Three-party pipeline combining privacy-preserving entity resolution with federated logistic regression over additively homomorphic encrypted messages under honest-but-curious security. Moves beyond typical distributed learning by handling vertical partitions, labels at only one party, unlinked entities, and adding a first formal analysis of entity-resolution error effects. TITLE: Private federated learning on vertically partitioned data via entity resolution and additively homomorphic encryption

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Consider two data providers, each maintaining private records of different feature sets about common entities. They aim to learn a linear model jointly in a federated setting, namely, data is local and a shared model is trained from locally computed updates. In contrast with most work on distributed learning, in this scenario (i) data is split vertically, i.e. by features, (ii) only one data provider knows the target v

title: Procedural Content Generation via Machine Learning (PCGML)
searchable_text:
Procedural Content Generation via Machine Learning (PCGML) Functional game content generation and support tasks for platformer levels, maps, interactive fiction stories, and collectible card game cards. Generate game content using machine learning models trained on existing game content, spanning neural networks, Markov models, clustering, and matrix factorization. It reframes procedural content generation around machine learning rather than search-based, solver-based, or constructive generation methods. TITLE: Procedural Content Generation via Machine Learning (PCGML)

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This survey explores procedural content generation via machine learning (PCGML), defined as the generation of game content using machine learning models trained on existing content. As the importance of PCG for game development increases, researchers explore new avenues for generating high-quality content with or without human involvement; this paper addresses the relatively new paradigm of using machine learning (in contrast with search-based, solver-based, and constructive m

title: Professor Forcing: A New Algorithm for Training Recurrent Networks
searchable_text:
Professor Forcing: A New Algorithm for Training Recurrent Networks Training recurrent networks for sequence generation tasks including language modeling, raw-waveform vocal synthesis, handwriting generation, image generation, character-level Penn Treebank, and sequential MNIST. Use adversarial domain adaptation to align recurrent-network dynamics between teacher-forced training and multi-step free-running sampling. Compared with Teacher Forcing, it adds an adversarial objective that encourages identical dynamics during observed-input training and self-generated multi-step sampling. TITLE: Professor Forcing: A New Algorithm for Training Recurrent Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Teacher Forcing algorithm trains recurrent networks by supplying observed sequence values as inputs during training and using the network’s own one-step-ahead predictions to do multi-step sampling. We introduce the Professor Forcing algorithm, which uses adversarial domain adaptation to encourage the dynamics of the recurrent network to be the same when training the network and when sampling from the network over multiple time steps. We apply Professor Forcing to l

title: Program Induction by Rationale Generation: Learning to Solve and Explain Algebraic Word Problems
searchable_text:
Program Induction by Rationale Generation: Learning to Solve and Explain Algebraic Word Problems Solving algebraic word problems that require executing arithmetic-operation programs to obtain final answers. Generate answer rationales: natural-language and human-readable mathematical-expression sequences that scaffold intermediate steps for inducing arithmetic programs. Replaces direct program induction from question-answer pairs with indirect supervision via generated answer rationales and intermediate milestones. TITLE: Program Induction by Rationale Generation: Learning to Solve and Explain Algebraic Word Problems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Solving algebraic word problems requires executing a series of arithmetic operations—a program—to obtain a final answer. However, since programs can be arbitrarily complicated, inducing them directly from question-answer pairs is a formidable challenge. To make this task more feasible, we solve these problems by generating answer rationales, sequences of natural language and human-readable mathematical expressions that derive the final ans

title: Progressive Neural Architecture Search
searchable_text:
Progressive Neural Architecture Search Learning convolutional neural network architectures for image classification benchmarks such as CIFAR-10 and ImageNet. Sequential model-based optimization over CNN structures, searching in increasing complexity while learning a surrogate model to guide structure-space exploration. Replaced reinforcement-learning or evolutionary NAS with progressive SMBO that orders candidate structures by increasing complexity and uses a learned surrogate for guidance. TITLE: Progressive Neural Architecture Search

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a new method for learning the structure of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) that is more efficient than recent state-of-the-art methods based on reinforcement learning and evolutionary algorithms. Our approach uses a sequential model-based optimization (SMBO) strategy, in which we search for structures in order of increasing complexity, while simultaneously learning a surrogate model to guide the search through structure space. Direct comparison under the same search space shows th

title: Proposed search for the detection of gravitational waves from eccentric binary black holes
searchable_text:
Proposed search for the detection of gravitational waves from eccentric binary black holes Detection of gravitational waves from compact binary black holes that retain orbital eccentricity into the ground-based detector sensitivity band. A dedicated search method for gravitational waves from eccentric binary black-hole coalescences, evaluated via sensitivity and false-alarm rates on LIGO/Virgo joint science-run data. Moves beyond quasicircular binary searches by proposing an eccentric binary black-hole search tailored to signals missed or poorly recovered by existing algorithms. TITLE: Proposed search for the detection of gravitational waves from eccentric binary black holes

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most compact binary systems are expected to circularize before the frequency of emitted gravitational waves (GWs) enters the sensitivity band of the ground based interferometric detectors. However, several mechanisms have been proposed for the formation of binary systems, which retain eccentricity throughout their lifetimes. Since no matched-filtering algorithm has been developed to extract continuous GW signals from compact binaries on orbits w

title: Prototypical Networks for Few-shot Learning
searchable_text:
Prototypical Networks for Few-shot Learning Few-shot classification: generalizing to new classes unseen during training from only a small number of examples per new class. Learn a metric space where few-shot classification is done by computing distances to prototype representations of each class. Replaced complicated architectural choices and meta-learning with a simpler prototype-based metric-space inductive bias. TITLE: Prototypical Networks for Few-shot Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose Prototypical Networks for the problem of few-shot classification, where a classifier must generalize to new classes not seen in the training set, given only a small number of examples of each new class. Prototypical Networks learn a metric space in which classification can be performed by computing distances to prototype representations of each class. Compared to recent approaches for few-shot learning, they reflect a simpler inductive bias that is beneficial in this limited-data

title: Pulsar Candidate Identification with Artificial Intelligence Techniques
searchable_text:
Pulsar Candidate Identification with Artificial Intelligence Techniques Automatic pulsar candidate identification in radio astronomy, separating potential pulsars from radio-frequency interference and other non-pulsar signals. Combine DCGAN for sample generation and feature learning with SVM classification for pulsar candidate labels. Replaces DCNN-only or hand-crafted feature approaches with DCGAN-based sample generation and learned discriminative features plus SVM inference. TITLE: Pulsar Candidate Identification with Artificial Intelligence Techniques

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Discovering pulsars is a significant and meaningful research topic in the field of radio astronomy. With the advent of astronomical instruments such as he Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical Telescope (FAST) in China, data volumes and data rates are exponentially growing. This fact necessitates a focus on artificial intelligence (AI) technologies that can perform the automatic pulsar candidate identification to mine large astronomical data sets. Automatic pulsar

title: Pulsar Searches with the SKA
searchable_text:
Pulsar Searches with the SKA Pulsar searches with the Square Kilometre Array, targeting discovery of Galactic pulsars visible from Earth. A real-time SKA pulsar search engine using a high-throughput signal-processing pipeline to handle massive daily data volumes and computational loads. Compared with the current known population, SKA1 is projected to increase discoveries by a factor of four; full SKA aims to detect all Earth-visible Galactic pulsars. TITLE: Pulsar Searches with the SKA

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Abstract The Square Kilometre Array will be an amazing instrument for pulsar astronomy. While the full SKA will be sensitive enough to detect all pulsars in the Galaxy visible from Earth, already with SKA1, pulsar searches will discover enough pulsars to increase the currently known population by a factor of four, no doubt including a range of amazing unknown sources. Real time processing is needed to deal with the 60 PB of pulsar search data collected per day, using a signal processing pipeline required t

title: Purification of noisy entanglement and faithful teleportation via noisy channels.
searchable_text:
Purification of noisy entanglement and faithful teleportation via noisy channels. Faithful transmission of unknown quantum states through noisy quantum channels using shared noisy entanglement. Apply local operations to shared not-too-impure entangled states to distill fewer, arbitrarily high-purity entangled pairs usable for teleportation. Replaces direct use of noisy channels with entanglement purification followed by teleportation through near-perfect distilled singlets. TITLE: Purification of noisy entanglement and faithful teleportation via noisy channels.

YEAR: 1995

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Two separated observers, by applying local operations to a supply of not-too-impure entangled states ({\em e.g.} singlets shared through a noisy channel), can prepare a smaller number of entangled pairs of arbitrarily high purity ({\em e.g.} near-perfect singlets). These can then be used to faithfully teleport unknown quantum states from one observer to the other, thereby achieving faithful transfrom one observer to the other, thereby achieving faithful

title: PyCosmo: An integrated cosmological Boltzmann solver
searchable_text:
PyCosmo: An integrated cosmological Boltzmann solver Accurate, fast theoretical predictions of linear cosmological perturbation transfer functions and related cosmological quantities for precision survey-era cosmology. Solve Einstein-Boltzmann equations with symbolic manipulation, generated C++ kernels, sparsity optimization, and consistency-relation adaptive timestepping, avoiding physical approximations for speed. Compared with existing Boltzmann solvers, PyCosmo introduces a Python-based symbolic equation framework with automatic C++ generation and sparsity optimization as an independent complementary implementation. TITLE: PyCosmo: An integrated cosmological Boltzmann solver

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
As wide-field surveys yield ever more precise measurements, cosmology has entered a phase of high precision requiring highly accurate and fast theoretical predictions. At the heart of most cosmological model predictions is a numerical solution of the Einstein-Boltzmann equations governing the evolution of linear perturbations in the Universe. We present PyCosmo, a new Python-based framework to solve this set of equations using a special pur- pose solver based on symbolic manipulatio

title: QSGD: Communication-Optimal Stochastic Gradient Descent, with Applications to Training Neural Networks
searchable_text:
QSGD: Communication-Optimal Stochastic Gradient Descent, with Applications to Training Neural Networks Reducing communication cost in parallel stochastic gradient descent for large-scale neural network training across multiple compute nodes. Quantized SGD compresses gradient updates at each node using a family of quantization schemes that trade compression against convergence time while preserving convergence guarantees. Replaced heuristic quantized-gradient communication with a provably convergent, communication-optimal quantization family offering tunable compression-convergence tradeoffs. TITLE: QSGD: Communication-Optimal Stochastic Gradient Descent, with Applications to Training Neural Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Parallel implementations of stochastic gradient descent (SGD) have received significant research attention, thanks to excellent scalability properties of this algorithm, and to its efficiency in the context of training deep neural networks. A fundamental barrier for parallelizing large-scale SGD is the fact that the cost of communicating the gradient updates between nodes can be very large. Consequently, lossy compression heuristics

title: Quantitative Concentration Inequalities for Empirical Measures on Non-compact Spaces
searchable_text:
Quantitative Concentration Inequalities for Empirical Measures on Non-compact Spaces Empirical measures of many independent variables on non-compact spaces, with applications to particle simulation error bounds in a mean field model. Use coupling arguments plus regularity and moment estimates for diffusive PDE solutions to prove concentration estimates in transportation distances.  TITLE: Quantitative Concentration Inequalities for Empirical Measures on Non-compact Spaces

YEAR: 2005

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We establish quantitative concentration estimates for the empirical measure of many independent variables, in transportation distances. As an application, we provide some error bounds for particle simulations in a model mean field problem. The tools include coupling arguments, as well as regularity and moment estimates for solutions of certain diffusive partial differential equations.

title: Quantum algorithm for linear systems of equations.
searchable_text:
Quantum algorithm for linear systems of equations. Estimating x(-->)dagger Mx(-->) for sparse N x N linear systems Ax(-->) = b(-->) when the full solution vector is not required. A quantum algorithm estimates expectation values associated with a linear-system solution, exploiting sparsity to achieve runtime polynomial in log(N) and condition number kappa. Changed the scaling target from classical time roughly N square root(kappa) to quantum runtime polynomial in log(N) and kappa for expectation estimation. TITLE: Quantum algorithm for linear systems of equations.

YEAR: 2008

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Solving linear systems of equations is a common problem that arises both on its own and as a subroutine in more complex problems: given a matrix A and a vector b(-->), find a vector x(-->) such that Ax(-->) = b(-->). We consider the case where one does not need to know the solution x(-->) itself, but rather an approximation of the expectation value of some operator associated with x(-->), e.g., x(-->)(dagger) Mx(-->) for some matrix M. In this case, when A is sparse, N x N and has con

title: Quantum algorithm for simulating the wave equation
searchable_text:
Quantum algorithm for simulating the wave equation Quantum simulation of the wave equation under Dirichlet and Neumann boundary conditions. Uses Hamiltonian simulation and quantum linear system subroutines, exploiting factorizations of discretized Laplacian operators. Specializes quantum differential-equation simulation to discretized wave-equation Laplacians, improving scaling versus general-purpose linear differential equation algorithms. TITLE: Quantum algorithm for simulating the wave equation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a quantum algorithm for simulating the wave equation under Dirichlet and Neumann boundary conditions. The algorithm uses Hamiltonian simulation and quantum linear system algorithms as subroutines. It relies on factorizations of discretized Laplacian operators to allow for improved scaling in truncation errors and improved scaling for state preparation relative to general purpose linear differential equation algorithms. We also consider using Hamiltonian simulation for Klein

title: Quantum Algorithm for Systems of Linear Equations with Exponentially Improved Dependence on Precision
searchable_text:
Quantum Algorithm for Systems of Linear Equations with Exponentially Improved Dependence on Precision Quantum algorithms for outputting a quantum state proportional to the solution of sparse, well-conditioned linear systems. Implement the linear-system solution operator using suitable Fourier or Chebyshev series representations, bypassing quantum phase estimation. Changes precision dependence from polynomial in 1/ε to polynomial in log(1/ε), while keeping essentially the same dependence on other parameters. TITLE: Quantum Algorithm for Systems of Linear Equations with Exponentially Improved Dependence on Precision

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Harrow, Hassidim, and Lloyd [Phys. Rev. Lett., 103 (2009), 150502] showed that for a suitably specified $N \times N$ matrix $A$ and an $N$-dimensional vector $\vec{b}$, there is a quantum algorithm that outputs a quantum state proportional to the solution of the linear system of equations $A\vec{x} = \vec{b}$. If $A$ is sparse and well-conditioned, their algorithm runs in time ${poly}(\log N, 1/\epsilon)$, where $\epsilon$ is the desi

title: Quantum algorithms for Gibbs sampling and hitting-time estimation
searchable_text:
Quantum algorithms for Gibbs sampling and hitting-time estimation Thermal Gibbs state preparation for quantum systems and hitting-time estimation for sparse Markov chains. Apply Hamiltonian-simulation, spectral-gap-amplification, and linear-system-solving tools to quantum Gibbs sampling and Markov-chain hitting-time estimation. Changes prior approaches by achieving almost-linear runtimes in √(Nβ/Z) and 1/(εΔ^(3/2)), with improved ε, β, and Δ dependencies. TITLE: Quantum algorithms for Gibbs sampling and hitting-time estimation

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present quantum algorithms for solving two problems regarding stochastic processes. The first algorithm prepares the thermal Gibbs state of a quantum system and runs in time almost linear in $\sqrt{N \beta/{\cal Z}}$ and polynomial in $\log(1/\epsilon)$, where $N$ is the Hilbert space dimension, $\beta$ is the inverse temperature, ${\cal Z}$ is the partition function, and $\epsilon$ is the desired precision of the output state. title: Quantum Algorithms for Quantum Field Theories
searchable_text:
Quantum Algorithms for Quantum Field Theories Efficient digital simulation of relativistic particle scattering in quantum field theories with quartic self-interactions in four or fewer spacetime dimensions. Quantum algorithm for discretized massive ϕ4 quantum field theory computing relativistic scattering probabilities with controlled discretization error. Replaced fastest known classical scattering simulation with a polynomial-time quantum algorithm for ϕ4 theory, including strong-coupling cases previously scaling exponentially. TITLE: Quantum Algorithms for Quantum Field Theories

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Quantum Leap? Quantum computers are expected to be able to solve some of the most difficult problems in mathematics and physics. It is not known, however, whether quantum field theories (QFTs) can be simulated efficiently with a quantum computer. QFTs are used in particle and condensed matter physics and have an infinite number of degrees of freedom; discretization is necessary to simulate them digitally. Jordan et al. title: Quantum chemistry structures and properties of 134 kilo molecules
searchable_text:
Quantum chemistry structures and properties of 134 kilo molecules Consistent quantum-chemical property data for stable small organic molecules up to nine heavy atoms within GDB-17, supporting benchmarking, machine learning, and structure-property studies. Compute optimized geometries, vibrational, electronic, and thermodynamic properties for enumerated small CHONF molecules using B3LYP/6-31G(2df,p), plus G4MP2 atomization thermochemistry for C7H10O2 isomers. Moved from enumerated GDB-17 chemical space to computed quantum-chemical structures and properties for all 133,885 species with up to nine heavy atoms in the CHONF subset. TITLE: Quantum chemistry structures and properties of 134 kilo molecules

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Computational de novo design of new drugs and materials requires rigorous and unbiased exploration of chemical compound space. However, large uncharted territories persist due to its size scaling combinatorially with molecular size. We report computed geometric, energetic, electronic, and thermodynamic properties for 134k stable small organic molecules made up of CHONF. title: Quantum dynamics of thermalizing systems
searchable_text:
Quantum dynamics of thermalizing systems Dynamics simulation of strongly thermalizing 1D quantum systems across near-equilibrium and far-from-equilibrium initial states. DMT approximates density operators of 1D systems and is combined with TEBD time evolution to simulate dynamics. Adds DMT density-operator approximation to the standard TEBD time-evolution framework, enabling simulations of strongly thermalizing systems to arbitrary times. TITLE: Quantum dynamics of thermalizing systems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a method “DMT” for approximating density operators of 1D systems that, when combined with a standard framework for time evolution (TEBD), makes possible simulation of the dynamics of strongly thermalizing systems to arbitrary times. We demonstrate that the method performs well for both near-equilibrium initial states (Gibbs states with spatially varying temperatures) and far-from-equilibrium initial states, including quenches across phase transitions and pure states.

title: Quantum-Merlin-Arthur-complete problems for stoquastic Hamiltonians and Markov matrices
searchable_text:
Quantum-Merlin-Arthur-complete problems for stoquastic Hamiltonians and Markov matrices Eigenvalue complexity of 3-local symmetric stochastic matrices, energy complexity of stoquastic Hamiltonians, and universality of adiabatic quantum computation in these systems. Establish QMA-completeness and adiabatic universality through stoquastic and stochastic Hamiltonian constructions, including excited-state and frustration-free ground-state settings. Extends QMA-completeness to 3-local symmetric stochastic matrices and to highest-energy stoquastic Hamiltonian problems, while showing adiabatic universality in stoquastic/stochastic settings. TITLE: Quantum-Merlin-Arthur-complete problems for stoquastic Hamiltonians and Markov matrices

YEAR: 2009

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We show that finding the lowest eigenvalue of a 3-local symmetric stochastic matrix is Quantum-Merlin-Arthur-complete (QMA-complete). We also show that finding the highest energy of a stoquastic Hamiltonian is QMA-complete and that adiabatic quantum computation using certain excited states of a stoquastic Hamiltonian is universal. We also show that adiabatic evolution in the ground state of a stochastic frustration-free Hamiltonian is universal. Ou

title: Quark and gluon jet substructure
searchable_text:
Quark and gluon jet substructure Event-by-event discrimination of quark-initiated jets from gluon-initiated jets for LHC searches and QCD tests. Comprehensive simulation study of quark/gluon jet variables using a semi-automated multivariate approach, comparing generators and charged-track-only variants.  TITLE: Quark and gluon jet substructure

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Distinguishing quark-initiated jets from gluon-initiated jets has the potential to significantly improve the reach of many beyond-the-standard model searches at the Large Hadron Collider and to provide additional tests of QCD. To explore whether quark and gluon jets could possibly be distinguished on an event-by-event basis, we perform a comprehensive simulation-based study. We explore a variety of motivated and unmotivated variables with a semi-automated multivariate approach. General conclusions are

title: Query-Efficient Imitation Learning for End-to-End Simulated Driving
searchable_text:
Query-Efficient Imitation Learning for End-to-End Simulated Driving End-to-end simulated autonomous driving policies mapping sensory input, such as front-facing camera frames, to driving actions via imitation learning. SafeDAgger extends DAgger to make imitation learning query-efficient for end-to-end autonomous driving by reducing reliance on expensive reference-policy queries. Compared with DAgger, SafeDAgger specifically changes the imitation-learning loop to be more query-efficient and better suited to end-to-end autonomous driving. TITLE: Query-Efficient Imitation Learning for End-to-End Simulated Driving

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 One way to approach end-to-end autonomous driving is to learn a policy that maps from a sensory input, such as an image frame from a front-facing camera, to a driving action, by imitating an expert driver, or a reference policy. This can be done by supervised learning, where a policy is tuned to minimize the difference between the predicted and ground-truth actions. title: Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset
searchable_text:
Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset Human action video classification on large-scale realistic YouTube videos and transfer to smaller benchmarks such as HMDB-51 and UCF-101. Two-Stream Inflated 3D ConvNet (I3D): inflate very deep 2D ConvNet filters and pooling kernels into 3D to learn spatio-temporal video features while leveraging ImageNet architectures and parameters. Compared with prior 2D ConvNet designs, filters and pooling kernels are expanded into 3D and paired with Kinetics pre-training for video recognition. TITLE: Quo Vadis, Action Recognition? A New Model and the Kinetics Dataset

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The paucity of videos in current action classification datasets (UCF-101 and HMDB-51) has made it difficult to identify good video architectures, as most methods obtain similar performance on existing small-scale benchmarks. This paper re-evaluates state-of-the-art architectures in light of the new Kinetics Human Action Video dataset. Kinetics has two orders of magnitude more data, with 400 human action classes and over 400 clips per class, and is collected from reali

title: RACE: Large-scale ReAding Comprehension Dataset From Examinations
searchable_text:
RACE: Large-scale ReAding Comprehension Dataset From Examinations Benchmark evaluation of machine reading comprehension, especially understanding and reasoning over exam-style passages for students aged 12 to 18. A large-scale reading-comprehension benchmark built from middle/high-school English exams, with passages and multiple-choice questions generated by human English instructors. Compared with other reading-comprehension benchmarks, RACE increases scale and emphasizes human-expert exam questions requiring reasoning. TITLE: RACE: Large-scale ReAding Comprehension Dataset From Examinations

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present RACE, a new dataset for benchmark evaluation of methods in the reading comprehension task. Collected from the English exams for middle and high school Chinese students in the age range between 12 to 18, RACE consists of near 28,000 passages and near 100,000 questions generated by human experts (English instructors), and covers a variety of topics which are carefully designed for evaluating the students’ ability in understanding and reasoning. title: Ray: A Distributed Framework for Emerging AI Applications
searchable_text:
Ray: A Distributed Framework for Emerging AI Applications Emerging AI applications, especially reinforcement learning workloads, that continuously interact with environments and require high-performance, flexible distributed execution. A dynamic task graph distributed system combining task-parallel and actor models, logically centralized control state via sharded storage, and bottom-up distributed scheduling. Ray unifies task-parallel and actor programming in a dynamic task graph with a sharded control-state store and bottom-up distributed scheduler. TITLE: Ray: A Distributed Framework for Emerging AI Applications

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The next generation of AI applications will continuously interact with the environment and learn from these interactions. These applications impose new and demanding systems requirements, both in terms of performance and flexibility. In this paper, we consider these requirements and present Ray---a distributed system to address them. Ray implements a dynamic task graph computation model that supports both the task-parallel and the actor programming models. To meet the performan

title: Real-time dedispersion for fast radio transient surveys, using auto tuning on many-core accelerators
searchable_text:
Real-time dedispersion for fast radio transient surveys, using auto tuning on many-core accelerators Dedispersion processing for radio surveys searching for pulsars and fast radio transients. Parallelize real-time dedispersion on many-core accelerators and use auto-tuning to adapt settings across hardware, observations, and telescopes. Moves from fixed accelerator implementations to auto-tuned dedispersion across AMD/NVIDIA GPUs, Intel Xeon Phi, observations, and telescopes. YEAR: 2016

ABSTRACT:
Dedispersion, the removal of deleterious smearing of impulsive signals by the interstellar matter, is one of the most intensive processing steps in any radio survey for pulsars and fast transients. We here present a study of the parallelization of this algorithm on many-core accelerators, including GPUs from AMD and NVIDIA, and the Intel Xeon Phi. We find that dedispersion is inherently memory-bound. Even in a perfect scenario, hardwar

title: Real-time, fast radio transient searches with GPU de-dispersion
searchable_text:
Real-time, fast radio transient searches with GPU de-dispersion Blind-search survey identification and discovery of fast radio transients requiring high-throughput de-dispersion and processing. Use GPUs to perform de-dispersion for real-time fast radio transient discovery in a GPU-based transient search machine. Shift from generally off-line high-performance or hardware-based processing to real-time GPU-based de-dispersion for transient search. The identification and subsequent discovery of fast radio transients using blind-search surveys require a large amount of processing power, in worst cases scaling as . title: Real-time optimal control via Deep Neural Networks: study on landing problems
searchable_text:
Real-time optimal control via Deep Neural Networks: study on landing problems Spacecraft landing problems requiring real-time optimal control. Real-time optimal control via Deep Neural Networks for spacecraft landing-related control. Applies Deep Neural Networks to real-time optimal control in landing problems. title: Recent Trends in Deep Learning Based Natural Language Processing
searchable_text:
Recent Trends in Deep Learning Based Natural Language Processing Deep learning methods applied across numerous natural language processing tasks. Review and comparative synthesis of deep learning model designs and methods using hierarchical representations for NLP. Shifts from individual NLP models toward an evolutionary walk-through that summarizes, compares, and contrasts deep learning approaches in NLP. ABSTRACT:
Deep learning methods employ multiple processing layers to learn hierarchical representations of data and have produced state-of-the-art results in many domains. Recently, a variety of model designs and methods have blossomed in the context of natural language processing (NLP). In this paper, we review significant deep learning related models and methods that have been employed for numerous NLP tasks and provide a walk-through of their evolution. We also summarize, compa

title: Recurrent Environment Simulators
searchable_text:
Recurrent Environment Simulators Action-conditioned environment simulation for planning, exploration, and efficient acting in Atari games, 3D car racing, and complex 3D maze environments. Recurrent neural networks simulate environment dynamics from high-dimensional pixel observations, producing temporally and spatially coherent long-horizon predictions without generating full images at every time-step. Compared with previous pixel-based environment simulators, it adds recurrent neural networks for coherent predictions hundreds of time-steps into the future and avoids per-step high-dimensional image generation. TITLE: Recurrent Environment Simulators

title: Recurrent Highway Networks

Recurrent Highway Networks extend LSTM cells with highway-style gated deep transition functions across each recurrent step, enabling transition depths larger than one. In this paper, we consider the specific problem of word-level language modeling and investigate strategies for regularizing and optimizing LSTM-based models. title: Reinforcement Learning: A Survey
searchable_text:
Reinforcement Learning: A Survey Computer-science survey of reinforcement learning for machine-learning researchers: agents learning behavior in dynamic environments with exploration/exploitation, delayed rewards, modeling, generalization, hierarchy, and hidden state. Agents learn behavior through trial-and-error interactions with dynamic environments, grounded in Markov decision theory and methods for delayed reinforcement, empirical models, generalization, hierarchy, and hidden state. Compared with psychology-rooted work, this frames reinforcement learning from a computer-science perspective and grounds it in Markov decision theory. TITLE: Reinforcement Learning: A Survey

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper surveys the field of reinforcement learning from a computer-science perspective. It is written to be accessible to researchers familiar with machine learning. Both the historical basis of the field and a broad selection of current work are summarized. Reinforcement learning is the problem faced by an agent that learns behavior through trial-and-error interactions with a dynamic environment. title: Reinforcement learning in decentralized stochastic control systems with partial history sharing
searchable_text:

Convert decentralized control to a centralized POMDP via common information, then use Incrementally Expanding Representation to build a finite-state decentralized RL algorithm. Beyond the existing common information approach, it adds Incrementally Expanding Representation to remove complete-model dependence and enable finite-state RL. title: Reinforcement Learning with Deep Energy-Based Policies
searchable_text:

Learn maximum-entropy energy-based policies with soft Q-learning, representing the optimal policy as a Boltzmann distribution and training an amortized Stein variational sampler. Energy-based policy learning is extended from tabular domains to continuous states and actions using soft Q-learning and amortized Stein variational gradient descent. TITLE: Reinforcement Learning with Deep Energy-Based Policies

We propose a method for learning expressive energy-based policies for continuous states and actions, which has been feasible only in tabular domains before. We apply our method to learning maximum entropy policies, resulting into a new algorithm, called soft Q-learning, that expresses the optimal policy via a Boltzmann distribution. We use the recently proposed amortized Stein variational gradient descent to learn a stochastic sampling network that approximates samples from this d

title: Reinforcement Learning with Unsupervised Auxiliary Tasks
searchable_text:
Reinforcement Learning with Unsupervised Auxiliary Tasks Deep reinforcement learning in Atari and first-person 3D Labyrinth environments with sparse or varied training signals beyond extrinsic reward. A deep RL agent jointly maximises extrinsic reward and many pseudo-reward auxiliary tasks through a shared representation, with a mechanism to focus representation learning on extrinsic rewards. Instead of only directly maximising cumulative reward, the agent adds simultaneous unsupervised auxiliary pseudo-reward tasks and reward-focused representation adaptation. title: Remote Sensing Image Scene Classification: Benchmark and State of the Art
searchable_text:
Remote Sensing Image Scene Classification: Benchmark and State of the Art Remote sensing image scene classification datasets and methods. Introduce NWPU-RESISC45, a publicly available large-scale benchmark dataset, and evaluate representative scene-classification methods to provide baselines. Compared with prior datasets, NWPU-RESISC45 expands to 31,500 images across 45 classes, with broad variations, high within-class diversity, and between-class similarity. TITLE: Remote Sensing Image Scene Classification: Benchmark and State of the Art

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Remote sensing image scene classification plays an important role in a wide range of applications and hence has been receiving remarkable attention. During the past years, significant efforts have been made to develop various data sets or present a variety of approaches for scene classification from remote sensing images. However, a systematic review of the literature concerning data sets and methods for scene classification is still lacking. In addition, almost

title: Report of the Dark Energy Task Force
searchable_text:
Report of the Dark Energy Task Force Understanding the nature of dark energy and cosmic acceleration in physical cosmology. An ambitious observational program to constrain dark energy properties as well as possible.  TITLE: Report of the Dark Energy Task Force

Dark energy appears to be the dominant component of the physical Universe, yet there is no persuasive theoretical explanation for its existence or magnitude. title: Residual Gated Graph ConvNets
searchable_text:
Residual Gated Graph ConvNets Learning on variable-length graph-structured data for vertex classification, graph classification, graph regression, graph generation, subgraph matching, and graph clustering. Graph ConvNet architecture for arbitrary-size graphs using gated edges and residual connections. Graph-structured data such as social networks, functional brain networks, gene regulatory networks, communications networks have brought the interest in generalizing deep learning techniques to graph domains. In this paper, we are interested to design neural networks for graphs with variable length in order to solve learning problems such as vertex classification, graph classification, graph regression, and graph generative tasks. title: Resonant-plane locking and spin alignment in stellar-mass black-hole binaries: A diagnostic of compact-binary formation
searchable_text:

Model precessional dynamics of tidally aligned spinning black-hole binaries, using resonant-plane conditions ΔΦ=0° or ±180° to link spin-angle distributions to formation scenarios. Moves from generic spin-precession studies to a formation diagnostic based on resonant-plane locking and the population distribution of ΔΦ. title: RESONANT POST-NEWTONIAN ECCENTRICITY EXCITATION IN HIERARCHICAL THREE-BODY SYSTEMS

RESONANT POST-NEWTONIAN ECCENTRICITY EXCITATION IN HIERARCHICAL THREE-BODY SYSTEMS Long-term eccentricity evolution of highly inclined, eccentric hierarchical triple systems under GR and Newtonian secular perturbations. First-order post-Newtonian secular Hamiltonian expansion for hierarchical triples, kept to leading order in the semi-major-axis ratio and combined with Kozai–Lidov three-body dynamics. Adds a new secular post-Newtonian interaction term beyond the standard GR precession terms for the inner and outer orbits. title: Return of the Devil in the Details: Delving Deep into Convolutional Nets
searchable_text:
Return of the Devil in the Details: Delving Deep into Convolutional Nets Image recognition and object detection benchmark performance comparisons across CNN-based and previous shallow visual representations. Rigorous common-ground evaluation of deep CNN architectures versus shallow Bag-of-Visual-Words and Improved Fisher Vector representations, with implementation details and reproducible code/models. Moved from isolated CNN benchmark successes to a controlled, reproducible comparison exposing implementation details and transferring data augmentation benefits to shallow methods. title: Rich Feature Hierarchies for Accurate Object Detection and Semantic Segmentation
searchable_text:
Rich Feature Hierarchies for Accurate Object Detection and Semantic Segmentation Object detection and semantic segmentation on PASCAL VOC-style natural images. Apply high-capacity CNNs to bottom-up region proposals, with supervised pre-training on an auxiliary task followed by domain-specific fine-tuning, to produce R-CNN region features for detection and segmentation. Replaced complex ensembles of multiple low-level features and context with a simpler scalable pipeline using region proposals plus high-capacity CNN features and fine-tuning. ABSTRACT:
Object detection performance, as measured on the canonical PASCAL VOC dataset, has plateaued in the last few years. The best-performing methods are complex ensemble systems that typically combine multiple low-level image features with high-level context. In this paper, we propose a simple and scalable detection algorithm that improves mean average precision (mAP) by more than 30% relative to the previous best result on VOC 2012 -- achieving a mAP of 53.3%

title: Riemannian Center of Mass and so called karcher mean

title: Risk-Constrained Reinforcement Learning with Percentile Risk Criteria

Risk-Constrained Reinforcement Learning with Percentile Risk Criteria Risk-constrained Markov decision processes minimizing expected cumulative cost under chance constraints or CVaR constraints on cumulative cost. Lagrangian-gradient formula with policy-gradient and actor-critic updates that descend in policy parameters and ascend in the Lagrange multiplier for percentile risk constraints. Introduces Lagrangian-gradient-based policy-gradient and actor-critic reinforcement learning algorithms for percentile risk-constrained MDPs using chance-constraint or CVaR risk criteria. In many sequential decision-making problems one is interested in minimizing an expected cumulative cost while taking into account \emph{risk}, i.e., increased awareness of events of small probability and high consequences. title: Risk-Limiting Load Restoration for Resilience Enhancement With Intermittent Energy Resources
searchable_text:
Risk-Limiting Load Restoration for Resilience Enhancement With Intermittent Energy Resources Critical-load restoration after natural disasters in distribution networks with microgrids and stochastic intermittent energy resources, especially without power forecasting tools. Gaussian-mixture prior modeling of WT/PV outputs, recursively updated with latest measurements, feeds a risk-limiting restoration formulation transformable into mixed-integer linear programming. Replaces forecast-dependent restoration with measurement-based recursive probability updates for intermittent generation uncertainty. TITLE: Risk-Limiting Load Restoration for Resilience Enhancement With Intermittent Energy Resources

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Microgrids can be used to restore critical load after a natural disaster, enhancing resilience of a distribution network. To deal with the stochastic nature of intermittent energy resources, such as wind turbines (WTs) and photovoltaics (PVs), forecast information is usually required. However, some microgrids may not be equipped with power forecasting tools. To fill this gap, a risk-limiting strategy based on measurements is proposed. A Gaussian mixture

title: RNN Decoding of Linear Block Codes
searchable_text:
RNN Decoding of Linear Block Codes Low-complexity, close-to-optimal channel decoding of powerful algebraic linear block codes with short to moderate block length, including BCH codes. A recurrent neural network architecture is used as a decoder for linear block codes, sharing computation to reduce parameter count versus feed-forward neural decoding. Replaces the closest neural predecessor, a feed-forward decoder, with a recurrent neural network decoder for linear block codes. TITLE: RNN Decoding of Linear Block Codes

YEAR: 2017

ABSTRACT:
Designing a practical, low complexity, close to optimal, channel decoder for powerful algebraic codes with short to moderate block length is an open research problem. Recently it has been shown that a feed-forward neural network architecture can improve on standard belief propagation decoding, despite the large example space. In this paper we introduce a recurrent neural network architecture for decoding linear block codes. Our method shows comparable bit error rate results compared to the feed-forwa

title: ROBO-AO KEPLER PLANETARY CANDIDATE SURVEY. II. ADAPTIVE OPTICS IMAGING OF 969 KEPLER EXOPLANET CANDIDATE HOST STARS
searchable_text:
ROBO-AO KEPLER PLANETARY CANDIDATE SURVEY. II. ADAPTIVE OPTICS IMAGING OF 969 KEPLER EXOPLANET CANDIDATE HOST STARS Finding nearby stars in Kepler photometric apertures that create false-positive exoplanet detections, bias transit radii, and reveal stellar multiplicity effects around Kepler planet candidate hosts. High-angular-resolution visible-light laser adaptive optics imaging of Kepler candidate host stars to detect blended nearby stellar companions contributing to photometric apertures. Extended the initial 715-star survey with 969 additional Kepler planet candidate hosts, revealing a higher nearby-star probability than the predecessor sample. We initiated the Robo-AO Kepler Planetary Candidate Survey in 2012 to observe each Kepler exoplanet candidate host star with high angular resolution, visible light, laser adaptive optics (AOs) imaging. Our goal is to find nearby stars lying in Kepler's photometric apertures that are responsible for the relatively high probability of false-positive exoplanet detections and that cause underestimates of the size of transit

title: Robust Adversarial Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
Robust Adversarial Reinforcement Learning Reinforcement learning policies that must generalize across simulation-to-real gaps and changed test conditions such as friction, masses, or disturbances. Train an RL agent with a jointly learned destabilizing adversary applying disturbance forces, optimizing a zero-sum minimax objective for robust policies. Instead of standard RL under nominal conditions, RARL reframes modeling errors as disturbances and adds a learned adversarial force policy inspired by H∞ control. TITLE: Robust Adversarial Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep neural networks coupled with fast simulation and improved computation have led to recent successes in the field of reinforcement learning (RL). However, most current RL-based approaches fail to generalize since: (a) the gap between simulation and real world is so large that policy-learning approaches fail to transfer; (b) even if policy learning is done in real world, the data scarcity leads to failed generalization from training to test scenarios (e.g., due to different friction or objec

title: RobustFill: Neural Program Learning under Noisy I/O
searchable_text:
RobustFill: Neural Program Learning under Noisy I/O Automatic program learning from input/output examples for large-scale, real-world tasks with noisy data such as typos. Modified attention RNN encodes variable-sized sets of I/O examples to support neural program synthesis and induction under noisy inputs. Compared with the previous best neural synthesis approach, RobustFill adds variable-sized I/O set encoding and robustness to realistic noise, improving reported accuracy from 34% to 92%. TITLE: RobustFill: Neural Program Learning under Noisy I/O

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The problem of automatically generating a computer program from some specification has been studied since the early days of AI. Recently, two competing approaches for automatic program learning have received significant attention: (1) neural program synthesis, where a neural network is conditioned on input/output (I/O) examples and learns to generate a program, and (2) neural program induction, where a neural network generates new outputs directly using a latent program representatio

title: Safe controller optimization for quadrotors with Gaussian processes
searchable_text:
Safe controller optimization for quadrotors with Gaussian processes Automatic safe tuning of controller parameters for quadrotor and dynamic-system control laws, avoiding manual real-system tuning and safety-critical failures. Apply SafeOpt with a Gaussian process performance model to tune controller parameters, exploring only settings predicted to exceed a safe performance threshold with high probability. Instead of standard Bayesian optimization that may test unsafe parameters, it applies SafeOpt to automatic controller tuning for the first time with explicit high-probability safety filtering. TITLE: Safe controller optimization for quadrotors with Gaussian processes

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
One of the most fundamental problems when designing controllers for dynamic systems is the tuning of the controller parameters. Typically, a model of the system is used to obtain an initial controller, but ultimately the controller parameters must be tuned manually on the real system to achieve the best performance. To avoid this manual tuning step, methods from machine learning, such as Bayesian optimization, have been used. However, as these methods evaluate differe

title: Safe Exploration in Finite Markov Decision Processes with Gaussian Processes
searchable_text:
Safe Exploration in Finite Markov Decision Processes with Gaussian Processes Safe exploration of finite Markov decision processes for safety-critical settings where unsafe state-action choices can cause failure. Cautious GP-based safe exploration of finite MDPs, using noisy observations to expand confidence-certified safe state-action pairs while explicitly enforcing reachability and safe return. Unlike classical reinforcement learning that permits arbitrary short-term loss, this work explores only under an unknown safety constraint and accounts for reachability to avoid unsafe dead ends. TITLE: Safe Exploration in Finite Markov Decision Processes with Gaussian Processes

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In classical reinforcement learning, when exploring an environment, agents accept arbitrary short term loss for long term gain. This is infeasible for safety critical applications, such as robotics, where even a single unsafe action may cause system failure. In this paper, we address the problem of safely exploring finite Markov decision processes (MDP). We define safety in terms of an, a priori unknown, safety constraint that depends on states and actions. W

title: Safe Exploration in Markov Decision Processes
searchable_text:
Safe Exploration in Markov Decision Processes Safe exploration for Markov decision processes with uncertain dynamics, especially non-ergodic physical systems where unsafe exploration can break the system. Formulate safety via ergodicity, then use an efficient constrained optimization algorithm that restricts policies to a safe subset while favoring exploration bonuses. Unlike prior exploration algorithms relying on ergodicity and rare-state bonuses, this work explicitly constrains exploration to guaranteed safe policies. TITLE: Safe Exploration in Markov Decision Processes

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In environments with uncertain dynamics exploration is necessary to learn how to perform well. Existing reinforcement learning algorithms provide strong exploration guarantees, but they tend to rely on an ergodicity assumption. The essence of ergodicity is that any state is eventually reachable from any other state by following a suitable policy. This assumption allows for exploration algorithms that operate by simply favoring states that have rarely been visited before. For most physical

title: Safe Model-based Reinforcement Learning with Stability Guarantees
searchable_text:
Safe Model-based Reinforcement Learning with Stability Guarantees Safe reinforcement learning for real-world safety-critical systems where unsafe exploration can damage or destabilize the system. Model-based reinforcement learning using statistical dynamics models and Lyapunov stability verification to synthesize high-performance policies with provable stability certificates. Extends Lyapunov stability verification by incorporating statistical models of dynamics to enable safe model-based reinforcement learning with stability certificates. TITLE: Safe Model-based Reinforcement Learning with Stability Guarantees

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Reinforcement learning is a powerful paradigm for learning optimal policies from experimental data. However, to find optimal policies, most reinforcement learning algorithms explore all possible actions, which may be harmful for real-world systems. As a consequence, learning algorithms are rarely applied on safety-critical systems in the real world. In this paper, we present a learning algorithm that explicitly considers safety, defined in terms of stability guarantees.

title: Safe Policy Improvement by Minimizing Robust Baseline Regret
searchable_text:
Safe Policy Improvement by Minimizing Robust Baseline Regret Safe policy improvement in sequential decision-making under uncertainty with limited data and an imperfect dynamics model. Model-based robust optimization directly minimizes negative regret relative to a baseline policy using an inaccurate dynamics model with known accuracy guarantees. Instead of guaranteeing safety by conservative robust optimization, it minimizes regret so the policy improves where dynamics are accurate and falls back to the baseline elsewhere. TITLE: Safe Policy Improvement by Minimizing Robust Baseline Regret

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
An important problem in sequential decision-making under uncertainty is to use limited data to compute a safe policy, i.e., a policy that is guaranteed to perform at least as well as a given baseline strategy. In this paper, we develop and analyze a new model-based approach to compute a safe policy when we have access to an inaccurate dynamics model of the system with known accuracy guarantees. Our proposed robust method uses this (inaccurate) model to directly minimize the

title: Safe Policy Improvement with Baseline Bootstrapping
searchable_text:
Safe Policy Improvement with Baseline Bootstrapping Batch reinforcement learning with limited data where a new policy must perform at least as well as a given baseline policy. Bootstrap the learned target policy with the baseline policy in uncertain regions, using computationally efficient value-based and policy-based safe policy improvement algorithms with theoretical bounds. Introduces baseline bootstrapping for SPI via two efficient algorithms and three variants, contrasting with literature algorithms whose limits are empirically shown. TITLE: Safe Policy Improvement with Baseline Bootstrapping

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A common goal in Reinforcement Learning is to derive a good strategy given a limited batch of data. In this paper, we adopt the safe policy improvement (SPI) approach: we compute a target policy guaranteed to perform at least as well as a given baseline policy. Our SPI strategy, inspired by the knows-what-it-knows paradigms, consists in bootstrapping the target policy with the baseline policy when it does not know. title: Safe Reinforcement Learning via Shielding
searchable_text:
Safe Reinforcement Learning via Shielding Safe reinforcement learning for policies that must obey temporal-logic properties during learning or execution. Synthesize a reactive shield from a temporal-logic specification to monitor learner actions and correct only those that would violate the specification. Instead of unconstrained reward maximization, reinforcement learning is augmented with an external reactive shield enforcing temporal-logic safety constraints. TITLE: Safe Reinforcement Learning via Shielding

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 Reinforcement learning algorithms discover policies that maximize reward, but do not necessarily guarantee safety during learning or execution phases. We introduce a new approach to learn optimal policies while enforcing properties expressed in temporal logic. To this end, given the temporal logic specification that is to be obeyed by the learning system, we propose to synthesize a reactive system called a shield. The shield monitors the actions from the learner and corrects them only if t

title: SAGA: A Fast Incremental Gradient Method With Support for Non-Strongly Convex Composite Objectives
searchable_text:
SAGA: A Fast Incremental Gradient Method With Support for Non-Strongly Convex Composite Objectives Optimization of composite objectives, including non-strongly convex problems and problems with inherent strong convexity. An incremental gradient optimisation method with fast linear convergence and proximal support for composite objectives. Compared with SAG and SVRG, SAGA offers better theoretical convergence rates; unlike SDCA, it directly supports non-strongly convex problems. TITLE: SAGA: A Fast Incremental Gradient Method With Support for Non-Strongly Convex Composite Objectives

YEAR: 2014
