ABSTRACT:
In this work we introduce a new optimisation method called SAGA in the spirit of SAG, SDCA, MISO and SVRG, a set of recently proposed incremental gradient algorithms with fast linear convergence rates. SAGA improves on the theory behind SAG and SVRG, with better theoretical convergence rates, and has support for composite objectives where a proximal operator is used on the regulariser. Unlike SDCA, SAGA supports non-strongly convex probl

title: Sample Efficient Actor-Critic with Experience Replay
searchable_text:
Sample Efficient Actor-Critic with Experience Replay Challenging reinforcement learning environments, including the discrete 57-game Atari domain and several continuous control problems. Actor-critic deep reinforcement learning with experience replay, using truncated importance sampling with bias correction, stochastic dueling network architectures, and trust region policy optimization. Adds experience replay to actor-critic deep RL with truncated importance sampling bias correction, stochastic dueling architectures, and a new trust region policy optimization method. TITLE: Sample Efficient Actor-Critic with Experience Replay

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents an actor-critic deep reinforcement learning agent with experience replay that is stable, sample efficient, and performs remarkably well on challenging environments, including the discrete 57-game Atari domain and several continuous control problems. To achieve this, the paper introduces several innovations, including truncated importance sampling with bias correction, stochastic dueling network architectures, and a new trust region policy optimization method.

title: Scalable Bayesian Optimization Using Deep Neural Networks
searchable_text:
Scalable Bayesian Optimization Using Deep Neural Networks Large-scale Bayesian optimization for expensive objectives requiring many evaluations, especially hyperparameter optimization for convolutional vision models and neural image captioning. Use neural networks for adaptive basis function regression as the Bayesian optimization surrogate distribution over functions. Replaces the typical Gaussian process surrogate, which scales cubically with observations, with a neural-network parametric adaptive basis regression model scaling linearly with data. TITLE: Scalable Bayesian Optimization Using Deep Neural Networks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Bayesian optimization is an effective methodology for the global optimization of functions with expensive evaluations. It relies on querying a distribution over functions defined by a relatively cheap surrogate model. An accurate model for this distribution over functions is critical to the effectiveness of the approach, and is typically fit using Gaussian processes (GPs). However, since GPs scale cubically with the number of observations, it has been challenging to handle obje

title: Scalable Gradient-Based Tuning of Continuous Regularization Hyperparameters
searchable_text:
Scalable Gradient-Based Tuning of Continuous Regularization Hyperparameters Hyperparameter selection for neural network regularization without running many full training trials. Gradient-based local adjustment of continuous regularization hyperparameters during model training to make parameter gradients more beneficial for validation cost. Replaces multiple full training trials and heavier gradient-based hyperparameter optimization with cheaper in-training local hyperparameter updates. TITLE: Scalable Gradient-Based Tuning of Continuous Regularization Hyperparameters

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Hyperparameter selection generally relies on running multiple full training trials, with selection based on validation set performance. We propose a gradient-based approach for locally adjusting hyperparameters during training of the model. Hyperparameters are adjusted so as to make the model parameter gradients, and hence updates, more advantageous for the validation cost. We explore the approach for tuning regularization hyperparameters and find that in expe

title: Scalable noise estimation with random unitary operators
searchable_text:
Scalable noise estimation with random unitary operators Experimental characterization of coherent quantum transformation accuracy in prototype quantum processing devices, especially large Hilbert spaces where process tomography is infeasible. Stochastic motion reversal of random unitary operators estimates generalized fidelities and average gate fidelity from measurable fidelity decay. Replaces infeasible quantum process tomography in large Hilbert spaces with a scalable stochastic protocol based on random-unitary motion reversal. TITLE: Scalable noise estimation with random unitary operators

YEAR: 2005

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We describe a scalable stochastic method for the experimental measurement of generalized fidelities characterizing the accuracy of the implementation of a coherent quantum transformation. The method is based on the motion reversal of random unitary operators. In the simplest case our method enables direct estimation of the average gate fidelity. The more general fidelities are characterized by a universal exponential rate of fidelity loss. In all cases the measurable fidelity dec

title: Scaling Algorithms for Unbalanced Transport Problems
searchable_text:
Scaling Algorithms for Unbalanced Transport Problems Approximating variational problems involving relaxed transport between arbitrary positive measures, including unbalanced transport, gradient flows, and barycenters. Extend entropic regularization to unbalanced optimal transport, yielding Sinkhorn-like algorithms based on diagonal scaling of transportation couplings. Moves beyond classical optimal transport on normalized probability distributions by regularizing and solving transport between arbitrary positive measures. TITLE: Scaling Algorithms for Unbalanced Transport Problems

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This article introduces a new class of fast algorithms to approx-imate variational problems involving unbalanced optimal transport. While classical optimal transport considers only normalized probability distributions, it is important for many applications to be able to compute some sort of re-laxed transportation between arbitrary positive measures. A generic class of such “unbalanced” optimal transport problems has been recently proposed by several authors. In this paper, we show

title: Scaling Deep Learning-Based Decoding of Polar Codes via Partitioning
searchable_text:
Scaling Deep Learning-Based Decoding of Polar Codes via Partitioning Scalable neural-network-based channel decoding for polar codes, especially beyond very short block lengths where full neural decoding becomes infeasible. Partition the polar encoding graph into small sub-blocks, train NN-based decoders for each sub-block near MAP performance, then connect them using remaining conventional belief-propagation decoding stages. Instead of using a fully conventional iterative polar decoder or monolithic NND, selected decoder sub-blocks are replaced by individually trained NN components. TITLE: Scaling Deep Learning-Based Decoding of Polar Codes via Partitioning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The training complexity of deep learning-based channel decoders scales exponentially with the codebook size and therefore with the number of information bits. Thus, neural network decoding (NND) is currently only feasible for very short block lengths. In this work, we show that the conventional iterative decoding algorithm for polar codes can be enhanced when sub-blocks of the decoder are replaced by neural network (NN) based components. Thus, we partition the encodi

title: Search for a standard model Higgs boson produced in association with a top-quark pair and decaying to bottom quarks using a matrix element method
searchable_text:
Search for a standard model Higgs boson produced in association with a top-quark pair and decaying to bottom quarks using a matrix element method Search for SM ttH production with Higgs decaying to bottom quarks in CMS 8 TeV pp collision events with hadronic jets and one or two oppositely charged leptons. Assign signal and t t-bar + jets background probability densities to each event with a matrix element method; use their ratio in a maximum likelihood fit to extract signal yield.  TITLE: Search for a standard model Higgs boson produced in association with a top-quark pair and decaying to bottom quarks using a matrix element method

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A search for a standard model Higgs boson produced in association with a top-quark pair and decaying to bottom quarks is presented. Events with hadronic jets and one or two oppositely charged leptons are selected from a data sample corresponding to an integrated luminosity of 19.5 inverse femtobarns collected by the CMS experiment at the LHC in pp collisions at a centre-of-mass energy of 8 T

title: Secure Multiparty Computation for Privacy-Preserving Data Mining
searchable_text:
Secure Multiparty Computation for Privacy-Preserving Data Mining Privacy-preserving data mining problems where parties want to compute over data without revealing private inputs. A survey-based synthesis of secure multiparty computation paradigms, definitions, constructions, efficiency issues, and application pitfalls for privacy-preserving data mining. Compared with basic SMC treatments, this work explicitly maps SMC to privacy-preserving data mining, identifies common application errors, and delineates solved versus unsolved problems. TITLE: Secure Multiparty Computation for Privacy-Preserving Data Mining

YEAR: 2009

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we survey the basic paradigms and notions of secure mul- tiparty computation and discuss their relevance to the fleld of privacy-preserving data mining. In addition to reviewing deflnitions and constructions for secure mul- tiparty computation, we discuss the issue of e-ciency and demonstrate the di-cul- ties involved in constructing highly e-cient protocols. We also present common errors that are prevalent in the literature when secure multiparty computat

title: Self-concordant analysis for logistic regression
searchable_text:
Self-concordant analysis for logistic regression Non-asymptotic theoretical analysis of logistic regression for binary classification with L2- and L1-norm regularization. Use and extend self-concordant convex-optimization tools to transfer non-asymptotic square-loss theory to logistic loss, including L2- and L1-regularized logistic regression. Replaces square-loss closed-form analysis with self-concordant analysis for logistic loss, extending square-loss theoretical results to regularized logistic regression. TITLE: Self-concordant analysis for logistic regression

YEAR: 2009

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Most of the non-asymptotic theoretical work in regression is carried out for the square loss, where estimators can be obtained through closed-form expressions. In this paper, we use and extend tools from the convex optimization literature, namely self-concordant functions, to provide simple extensions of theoretical results for the square loss to the logistic loss. We apply the extension techniques to logistic regression with regularization by the $\ell_2$-norm and regularization by the

title: Semantic Understanding of Scenes Through the ADE20K Dataset
searchable_text:
Semantic Understanding of Scenes Through the ADE20K Dataset Semantic understanding, scene parsing, and instance segmentation of complex everyday visual scenes across broad scene and object categories. Construct a densely annotated ADE20K dataset with pixel-wise labels for scenes, objects, object parts, and parts of parts, then use it for scene parsing and instance segmentation benchmarks. Compared with prior scarce datasets, ADE20K adds 25k densely annotated everyday-scene images spanning scenes, objects, object parts, and sometimes parts of parts, plus benchmarks and baselines. TITLE: Semantic Understanding of Scenes Through the ADE20K Dataset

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Semantic understanding of visual scenes is one of the holy grails of computer vision. Despite efforts of the community in data collection, there are still few image datasets covering a wide range of scenes and object categories with pixel-wise annotations for scene understanding. In this work, we present a densely annotated dataset ADE20K, which spans diverse annotations of scenes, objects, parts of objects, and in some cases even parts of parts. Totally there are 25k images

title: Semantics derived automatically from language corpora contain human-like biases
searchable_text:
Semantics derived automatically from language corpora contain human-like biases Detecting implicit semantic biases in human language corpora, including associations involving race, gender, career, family, flowers, and insects. Apply a purely statistical machine-learning word-association model to ordinary Web text corpora to recover semantic associations comparable to IAT-measured human biases. Shifted implicit-bias measurement from human IAT responses to automatically learned word associations from standard Web-scale text corpora. TITLE: Semantics derived automatically from language corpora contain human-like biases

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Machines learn what people know implicitly AlphaGo has demonstrated that a machine can learn how to do things that people spend many years of concentrated study learning, and it can rapidly learn how to do them better than any human can. Caliskan et al. now show that machines can learn word associations from written texts and that these associations mirror those learned by humans, as measured by the Implicit Association Test (IAT) (see the Perspective by G

title: Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks
searchable_text:
Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks Semi-supervised learning and classification on graph-structured data, including citation networks and knowledge graph datasets. Graph convolutional neural network using a localized first-order approximation of spectral graph convolutions, operating directly on graphs and combining node features with local graph structure. Replaces more expensive spectral graph convolution with a localized first-order approximation, enabling scalable convolutional neural networks directly on graphs. TITLE: Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a scalable approach for semi-supervised learning on graph-structured data that is based on an efficient variant of convolutional neural networks which operate directly on graphs. We motivate the choice of our convolutional architecture via a localized first-order approximation of spectral graph convolutions. Our model scales linearly in the number of graph edges and learns hidden layer representations that encode both local graph structure and features of node

title: Semi-supervised Sequence Learning
searchable_text:
Semi-supervised Sequence Learning Sequence learning tasks using recurrent networks, including NLP language modeling, text classification, and image recognition. Pretrain recurrent sequence models on unlabeled data using next-step prediction or a sequence autoencoder, then initialize supervised models with the pretrained parameters. Adds unlabeled-data pretraining via language modeling or sequence autoencoding before supervised recurrent sequence learning. TITLE: Semi-supervised Sequence Learning

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present two approaches to use unlabeled data to improve Sequence Learning with recurrent networks. The first approach is to predict what comes next in a sequence, which is a language model in NLP. The second approach is to use a sequence autoencoder, which reads the input sequence into a vector and predicts the input sequence again. These two algorithms can be used as a "pretraining" algorithm for a later supervised sequence learning algorithm. In other words, the parameters obtained from the pretr

title: Sensor fusion for semantic segmentation of urban scenes
searchable_text:
Sensor fusion for semantic segmentation of urban scenes Semantic segmentation of urban scenes for robotics and intelligent autonomous systems using image and 3D point-cloud data. Late-fusion semantic segmentation combining images and 3D point clouds across multiple scales, followed by a pairwise CRF to enforce spatial consistency. Introduces an image/3D point-cloud late-fusion architecture with multi-scale information and CRF post-processing, rather than relying on a single modality or earlier segmentation approach. TITLE: Sensor fusion for semantic segmentation of urban scenes

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Semantic understanding of environments is an important problem in robotics in general and intelligent autonomous systems in particular. In this paper, we propose a semantic segmentation algorithm which effectively fuses information from images and 3D point clouds. The proposed method incorporates information from multiple scales in an intuitive and effective manner. A late-fusion architecture is proposed to maximally leverage the training data in each modality. Finally, a pairwis

title: Seq2SQL: Generating Structured Queries from Natural Language using Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
Seq2SQL: Generating Structured Queries from Natural Language using Reinforcement Learning Translating natural language questions into corresponding SQL queries over relational database tables. A deep neural network maps natural language to SQL using policy-based reinforcement learning from query execution rewards and SQL-structure-based pruning. Compared with a state-of-the-art semantic parser, Seq2SQL adds in-loop query execution rewards, policy-based reinforcement learning, SQL-structure pruning, and evaluation on the larger WikiSQL dataset. TITLE: Seq2SQL: Generating Structured Queries from Natural Language using Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Relational databases store a significant amount of the worlds data. However, accessing this data currently requires users to understand a query language such as SQL. We propose Seq2SQL, a deep neural network for translating natural language questions to corresponding SQL queries. Our model uses rewards from in the loop query execution over the database to learn a policy to generate the query, which contains unordered parts that are less suitable

title: SeqGAN: Sequence Generative Adversarial Nets with Policy Gradient
searchable_text:
SeqGAN: Sequence Generative Adversarial Nets with Policy Gradient Generating sequences of discrete tokens where adversarial training struggles with non-differentiable outputs and incomplete-sequence evaluation. Models the sequence generator as a stochastic reinforcement-learning policy, using discriminator rewards on complete sequences and Monte Carlo search to assign intermediate action rewards. Changes GAN sequence training from discriminator-to-generator gradient backpropagation to direct policy-gradient updates with Monte Carlo reward propagation. TITLE: SeqGAN: Sequence Generative Adversarial Nets with Policy Gradient

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 As a new way of training generative models, Generative Adversarial Net (GAN) that uses a discriminative model to guide the training of the generative model has enjoyed considerable success in generating real-valued data. However, it has limitations when the goal is for generating sequences of discrete tokens. A major reason lies in that the discrete outputs from the generative model make it difficult to pass the gradient update from the discriminative model to the g

title: Sequential regulatory activity prediction across chromosomes with convolutional neural networks
searchable_text:
Sequential regulatory activity prediction across chromosomes with convolutional neural networks Predicting phenotypic, epigenetic, transcriptional, and gene-expression outcomes from genotypes in large mammalian genomes. Convolutional neural networks predict cell type-specific epigenetic and transcriptional profiles from DNA sequence alone, identifying promoters and distal regulatory elements to synthesize gene-expression predictions.  TITLE: Sequential regulatory activity prediction across chromosomes with convolutional neural networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Models for predicting phenotypic outcomes from genotypes have important applications to understanding genomic function and improving human health. Here, we develop a machine-learning system to predict cell type-specific epigenetic and transcriptional profiles in large mammalian genomes from DNA sequence alone. Using convolutional neural networks, this system identifies promoters and distal regulatory elements and synthesizes their content t

title: Seven temperate terrestrial planets around the nearby ultracool dwarf star TRAPPIST-1
searchable_text:
Seven temperate terrestrial planets around the nearby ultracool dwarf star TRAPPIST-1 Detection and characterization of temperate, Earth-like exoplanets around nearby ultracool dwarf stars suitable for atmospheric studies. Photometric monitoring from ground and space of TRAPPIST-1 transits to infer planet sizes, masses, orbital periods, and system architecture. Expanded the known TRAPPIST-1 system from three detected Earth-sized transiting planets to at least seven temperate terrestrial planets, adding orbital resonance and migration inference. TITLE: Seven temperate terrestrial planets around the nearby ultracool dwarf star TRAPPIST-1

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
One aim of modern astronomy is to detect temperate, Earth-like exoplanets that are well suited for atmospheric characterization. Recently, three Earth-sized planets were detected that transit (that is, pass in front of) a star with a mass just eight per cent that of the Sun, located 12 parsecs away. The transiting configuration of these planets, combined with the Jupiter-like size of their host star—named TRAPPIST-1—makes possible in-depth studies of

title: Several natural BQP-Complete problems
searchable_text:
Several natural BQP-Complete problems BQP-complete computational tasks that characterize quantum-computing power and potential quantum speedups over classical computation. Define and analyze natural BQP-complete sampling problems grounded in linear algebra, especially Local Hamiltonian Eigenvalue Sampling and Phase Estimation Sampling. Moved beyond prior BQP-complete problems based on signed weight enumerators and Jones polynomial approximation to natural linear-algebra sampling problems. TITLE: Several natural BQP-Complete problems

YEAR: 2006

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A central problem in quantum computing is to identify computational tasks which can be solved substantially faster on a quantum computer than on any classical computer. By studying the hardest such tasks, known as BQP-complete problems, we deepen our understanding of the power and limitations of quantum computers. We present several BQP-complete problems, including Local Hamiltonian Eigenvalue Sampling and Phase Estimation Sampling. Different than the previous known BQP-complete problems (the Quad

title: ShapeNet: An Information-Rich 3D Model Repository
searchable_text:
ShapeNet: An Information-Rich 3D Model Repository Information-rich 3D object model data for computer graphics and vision research, visualization, data-driven geometric analysis, and quantitative benchmarking. A large-scale 3D CAD repository organizes object models under WordNet and attaches semantic annotations such as alignments, parts, symmetries, sizes, and keywords. It shifted 3D model collections toward a richly annotated, WordNet-organized, web-accessible benchmark repository at multi-million-model scale. TITLE: ShapeNet: An Information-Rich 3D Model Repository

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present ShapeNet: a richly-annotated, large-scale repository of shapes represented by 3D CAD models of objects. ShapeNet contains 3D models from a multitude of semantic categories and organizes them under the WordNet taxonomy. It is a collection of datasets providing many semantic annotations for each 3D model such as consistent rigid alignments, parts and bilateral symmetry planes, physical sizes, keywords, as well as other planned annotations. Annotations are made available throug

title: ShuffleNet: An Extremely Efficient Convolutional Neural Network for Mobile Devices
searchable_text:
ShuffleNet: An Extremely Efficient Convolutional Neural Network for Mobile Devices Image classification and object detection on mobile devices with very limited computing power, especially 10-150 MFLOPs budgets. Use pointwise group convolution plus channel shuffle to reduce CNN computation cost while maintaining accuracy. Compared with predecessors such as MobileNet, ShuffleNet adds pointwise group convolution and channel shuffle as efficiency-focused architectural operations. TITLE: ShuffleNet: An Extremely Efficient Convolutional Neural Network for Mobile Devices

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce an extremely computation-efficient CNN architecture named ShuffleNet, which is designed specially for mobile devices with very limited computing power (e.g., 10-150 MFLOPs). The new architecture utilizes two new operations, pointwise group convolution and channel shuffle, to greatly reduce computation cost while maintaining accuracy. Experiments on ImageNet classification and MS COCO object detection demonstrate the superior performance of

title: SIDRA: a blind algorithm for signal detection in photometric surveys
searchable_text:
SIDRA: a blind algorithm for signal detection in photometric surveys Quick, accurate detection and classification of signals in photometric survey light curves, including transits, variables, microlensing events, eclipsing binaries, and constant sources. A Random Forest detection/classification algorithm using four light-curve features to assign photometric survey signals to classes. Compared with a classic Box Least Square search, SIDRA adds Random-Forest-based light-curve classification and detects 7.5% more planets, especially at lower signal-to-noise. TITLE: SIDRA: a blind algorithm for signal detection in photometric surveys

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present the Signal Detection using Random-Forest Algorithm (SIDRA). SIDRA is a detection and classification algorithm based on the Machine Learning technique (Random Forest). The goal of this paper is to show the power of SIDRA for quick and accurate signal detection and classification. We first diagnose the power of the method with simulated light curves and try it on a subset of the Kepler space mission catalogue. We use five classes of simulated light curves (C

title: Sim-to-Real Transfer of Robotic Control with Dynamics Randomization
searchable_text:
Sim-to-Real Transfer of Robotic Control with Dynamics Randomization Sim-to-real robotic control for object pushing with a robotic arm, moving objects to desired locations from random initial configurations. Randomize simulator dynamics during training so policies learn adaptive control robust to diverse dynamics and real-world mismatch. Replaces reliance on a single calibrated simulator or real-system training with dynamics-randomized simulation to produce adaptive policies transferable without physical training. TITLE: Sim-to-Real Transfer of Robotic Control with Dynamics Randomization

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Simulations are attractive environments for training agents as they provide an abundant source of data and alleviate certain safety concerns during the training process. But the behaviours developed by agents in simulation are often specific to the characteristics of the simulator. Due to modeling error, strategies that are successful in simulation may not transfer to their real world counterparts. In this paper, we demonstrate a simple method to bridge this “reality

title: Simple all-microwave entangling gate for fixed-frequency superconducting qubits.
searchable_text:
Simple all-microwave entangling gate for fixed-frequency superconducting qubits. Entangling gates for fixed-frequency superconducting qubits operating at optimal bias points. All-microwave two-qubit gate using microwave irradiation on one qubit at the transition frequency of the other, tunable via irradiation amplitude. Replaced added subcircuitry or frequency tuning with a simple all-microwave gate for fixed-frequency qubits. TITLE: Simple all-microwave entangling gate for fixed-frequency superconducting qubits.

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We demonstrate an all-microwave two-qubit gate on superconducting qubits which are fixed in frequency at optimal bias points. The gate requires no additional subcircuitry and is tunable via the amplitude of microwave irradiation on one qubit at the transition frequency of the other. We use the gate to generate entangled states with a maximal extracted concurrence of 0.88, and quantum process tomography reveals a gate fidelity of 81%.

title: Simple and Effective Multi-Paragraph Reading Comprehension
searchable_text:
Simple and Effective Multi-Paragraph Reading Comprehension Document-level and multi-paragraph reading comprehension for question answering over entire documents, including TriviaQA and SQuAD. Adapt paragraph-level QA to documents by sampling multiple paragraphs per document and training with a global objective that rewards correct answers while ignoring non-answer paragraphs. Replaces naive independent paragraph application with a modified training scheme using multiple sampled paragraphs and globally correct document-level supervision. TITLE: Simple and Effective Multi-Paragraph Reading Comprehension

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a method of adapting neural paragraph-level question answering models to the case where entire documents are given as input. Most current question answering models cannot scale to document or multi-document input, and naively applying these models to each paragraph independently often results in them being distracted by irrelevant text. We show that it is possible to significantly improve performance by using a modified training scheme that teaches the model to ig

title: Simple And Efficient Architecture Search for Convolutional Neural Networks
searchable_text:
Simple And Efficient Architecture Search for Convolutional Neural Networks Finding well-performing convolutional neural network architectures, especially for image classification benchmarks such as CIFAR-10. Automatic CNN architecture search using simple hill climbing operators that apply network morphisms, followed by short cosine-annealing optimization runs. Replaces manual expert trial-and-error architecture design with an automatic hill-climbing search based on network morphisms and short optimization runs. TITLE: Simple And Efficient Architecture Search for Convolutional Neural Networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Neural networks have recently had a lot of success for many tasks. However, neural network architectures that perform well are still typically designed manually by experts in a cumbersome trial-and-error process. We propose a new method to automatically search for well-performing CNN architectures based on a simple hill climbing procedure whose operators apply network morphisms, followed by short optimization runs by cosine annealing. Surprisingly, this simple

title: Simple and Scalable Predictive Uncertainty Estimation using Deep Ensembles
searchable_text:
Simple and Scalable Predictive Uncertainty Estimation using Deep Ensembles Predictive uncertainty estimation for deep neural network classification and regression, including robustness under dataset shift and ImageNet-scale evaluation. Use deep ensembles as a simple, parallelizable alternative to Bayesian neural networks for predictive uncertainty estimation. Replaced approximate Bayesian neural networks with a standard deep-ensemble approach requiring fewer training-procedure changes, less computation, little tuning, and easy parallelization. TITLE: Simple and Scalable Predictive Uncertainty Estimation using Deep Ensembles

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep neural networks (NNs) are powerful black box predictors that have recently achieved impressive performance on a wide spectrum of tasks. Quantifying predictive uncertainty in NNs is a challenging and yet unsolved problem. Bayesian NNs, which learn a distribution over weights, are currently the state-of-the-art for estimating predictive uncertainty; however these require significant modifications to the training procedure and are computationally expensive co

title: Simulating Hamiltonian dynamics with a truncated Taylor series.
searchable_text:
Simulating Hamiltonian dynamics with a truncated Taylor series. Quantum-computer simulation of Hamiltonian time evolution for physical systems. Approximate the evolution operator’s truncated Taylor series, implemented via linear combinations of unitary operations and robust oblivious amplitude amplification. Relative to another recent algorithm, it simplifies the algorithm and analysis using LCU implementation and robust oblivious amplitude amplification. TITLE: Simulating Hamiltonian dynamics with a truncated Taylor series.

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We describe a simple, efficient method for simulating Hamiltonian dynamics on a quantum computer by approximating the truncated Taylor series of the evolution operator. Our method can simulate the time evolution of a wide variety of physical systems. As in another recent algorithm, the cost of our method depends only logarithmically on the inverse of the desired precision, which is optimal. However, we simplify the algorithm and its analysis by using a method for implemen

title: Sinkhorn Distances: Lightspeed Computation of Optimal Transport
searchable_text:
Sinkhorn Distances: Lightspeed Computation of Optimal Transport Optimal transportation distances for high-dimensional histograms where classical linear-program computation becomes prohibitive. Entropically regularize classical optimal transport and compute the resulting optimum with Sinkhorn-Knopp matrix scaling. Replaces classical linear-program optimal transport with a maximum-entropy, entropically smoothed transport problem solved by matrix scaling. TITLE: Sinkhorn Distances: Lightspeed Computation of Optimal Transport

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Optimal transportation distances are a fundamental family of parameterized distances for histograms. Despite their appealing theoretical properties, excellent performance in retrieval tasks and intuitive formulation, their computation involves the resolution of a linear program whose cost is prohibitive whenever the histograms' dimension exceeds a few hundreds. We propose in this work a new family of optimal transportation distances that look at transportation problems fr

title: Sliced Wasserstein Kernels for Probability Distributions
searchable_text:
Sliced Wasserstein Kernels for Probability Distributions Machine learning tasks requiring discrepancy measures for probability distributions, especially applications of optimal transport distances. Provably positive definite kernel methods constructed from the Sliced Wasserstein distance for comparing probability distributions. Moves optimal-transport-flavored distribution distances into widely used positive definite kernel methods using the Sliced Wasserstein distance. TITLE: Sliced Wasserstein Kernels for Probability Distributions

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Optimal transport distances, otherwise known as Wasserstein distances, have recently drawn ample attention in computer vision and machine learning as powerful discrepancy measures for probability distributions. The recent developments on alternative formulations of the optimal transport have allowed for faster solutions to the problem and have revamped their practical applications in machine learning. In this paper, we exploit the widely used kernel methods and provide a family

title: SMASH: One-Shot Model Architecture Search through HyperNetworks
searchable_text:
SMASH: One-Shot Model Architecture Search through HyperNetworks Accelerated selection of deep neural network architectures across flexible connectivity patterns and image/model classification benchmarks. Learn an auxiliary HyperNet that generates weights for candidate architectures, then rank architectures by validation performance using those generated weights. Replaces separately training each candidate architecture with one HyperNet-conditioned weight-generation run for relative architecture evaluation. TITLE: SMASH: One-Shot Model Architecture Search through HyperNetworks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Designing architectures for deep neural networks requires expert knowledge and substantial computation time. We propose a technique to accelerate architecture selection by learning an auxiliary HyperNet that generates the weights of a main model conditioned on that model's architecture. By comparing the relative validation performance of networks with HyperNet-generated weights, we can effectively search over a wide range of architectures at the cost of a single training

title: Smoothed Dual Embedding Control
searchable_text:
Smoothed Dual Embedding Control Policy optimization for reinforcement learning control problems, including off-policy learning from a single sample path. Reformulates Bellman optimality via Nesterov smoothing and Fenchel-dual saddle-point optimization, yielding SDEC with arbitrary learnable function approximators and no separate policy evaluation step. Changes policy optimization from Bellman/policy-evaluation-based methods to a smoothed Fenchel-dual saddle-point reformulation that bypasses policy evaluation. TITLE: Smoothed Dual Embedding Control

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We revisit the Bellman optimality equation with Nesterov's smoothing technique and provide a unique saddle-point optimization perspective of the policy optimization problem in reinforcement learning based on Fenchel duality. A new reinforcement learning algorithm, called Smoothed Dual Embedding Control or SDEC, is derived to solve the saddle-point reformulation with arbitrary learnable function approximator. The algorithm bypasses the policy evaluation step in the policy optimization from a principled s

title: Soft drop
searchable_text:
Soft drop Jet substructure grooming and analysis for high-energy jets, including groomed observables, boosted W tagging, and potential pileup mitigation. Soft drop declustering recursively removes soft wide-angle radiation from a jet using a soft threshold zcut and angular exponent β. Introduces a two-parameter soft-drop generalization of modified mass drop, with β = 0 corresponding roughly to the modified mass drop procedure. TITLE: Soft drop

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A bstractWe introduce a new jet substructure technique called “soft drop declustering”, which recursively removes soft wide-angle radiation from a jet. The soft drop algorithm depends on two parameters — a soft threshold zcut and an angular exponent β — with the β = 0 limit corresponding roughly to the (modified) mass drop procedure. To gain an analytic understanding of soft drop and highlight the β dependence, we perform resummed calculations for three observables on soft-dropped jets: the energy correlation functions, the

title: Solving General Arithmetic Word Problems
searchable_text:
Solving General Arithmetic Word Problems Automatically solving arithmetic word problems, including problems requiring multiple steps and operations. Represent arithmetic word problems as expression trees, decompose them into classification problems, then compose solutions using quantity schemas, world knowledge, and constrained inference. Unlike prior approaches, it handles multi-step, multi-operation arithmetic problems without additional annotations or predefined templates. TITLE: Solving General Arithmetic Word Problems

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents a novel approach to automatically solving arithmetic word problems. This is the first algorithmic approach that can handle arithmetic problems with multiple steps and operations, without depending on additional annotations or predefined templates. title: Solving Geometry Problems: Combining Text and Diagram Interpretation
searchable_text:
Solving Geometry Problems: Combining Text and Diagram Interpretation Solving unaltered SAT geometry questions that require both text understanding and diagram interpretation. Models geometry-question understanding as submodular optimization, selecting a formal problem description compatible with text and diagram, then passing it to a geometric solver. Introduces GEOS as the first automated system combining text understanding and diagram interpretation for unaltered SAT geometry questions. TITLE: Solving Geometry Problems: Combining Text and Diagram Interpretation

ABSTRACT:
This paper introduces GEOS, the first automated system to solve unaltered SAT geometry questions by combining text understanding and diagram interpretation. We model the problem of understanding geometry questions as submodular optimization, and identify a formal problem description likely to be compatible with both the question text and diagram. GEOS then feeds the description to a geometric solver that attempts to determine the correct answer. In our experiments, G

title: Solving ill-posed inverse problems using iterative deep neural networks
searchable_text:
Solving ill-posed inverse problems using iterative deep neural networks Ill-posed inverse problems, including non-linear tomographic inversion with simulated Sheep-Logan phantom and head CT data. Partially learned gradient-like iterations use a convolutional network to learn the update component while ingesting forward-model data-discrepancy and regulariser gradients at each iteration. Compared with filtered backprojection and total variation reconstruction, it replaces fixed hand-crafted updates with a learned gradient component informed by the forward operator, noise model, and regulariser. TITLE: Solving ill-posed inverse problems using iterative deep neural networks

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a partially learned approach for the solution of ill-posed inverse problems with not necessarily linear forward operators. The method builds on ideas from classical regularisation theory and recent advances in deep learning to perform learning while making use of prior information about the inverse problem encoded in the forward operator, noise model and a regularising functional. title: Solving Imperfect Information Games Using Decomposition
searchable_text:
Solving Imperfect Information Games Using Decomposition Solving large imperfect-information games, especially when run-time adaptation or memory/disk storage constraints prevent monolithic equilibrium computation. Decompose imperfect-information games into independently solvable subgames while preserving full-game optimality guarantees. Unlike prior decomposition methods, this approach retains optimality guarantees and introduces CFR-D for equilibrium solving beyond available storage. TITLE: Solving Imperfect Information Games Using Decomposition

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:

 
 Decomposition, i.e. independently analyzing possible subgames, has proven to be an essential principle for effective decision-making in perfect information games. However, in imperfect information games, decomposition has proven to be problematic. To date, all proposed techniques for decomposition in imperfect information games have abandoned theoretical guarantees. title: Solving parametric PDE problems with artificial neural networks
searchable_text:
Solving parametric PDE problems with artificial neural networks Parametric PDE problems, especially numerical PDEs with uncertain random coefficients in engineering and physics. Use a neural network to parameterise the PDE physical quantity of interest as a function of input coefficient fields, justified as time evolution toward PDE solutions. Replaces direct high-dimensional numerical parametrization with neural-network parametrization of quantities of interest over input coefficient fields. TITLE: Solving parametric PDE problems with artificial neural networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The curse of dimensionality is commonly encountered in numerical partial differential equations (PDE), especially when uncertainties have to be modelled into the equations as random coefficients. However, very often the variability of physical quantities derived from PDE can be captured by a few features on the space of the coefficient fields. title: SoundNet: Learning Sound Representations from Unlabeled Video
searchable_text:
SoundNet: Learning Sound Representations from Unlabeled Video Natural sound representation learning for acoustic scene/object classification from large-scale unlabeled in-the-wild video. Student-teacher transfer uses unlabeled synchronized video to distill discriminative visual recognition knowledge into an acoustic sound representation. Replaces label-dependent acoustic training with cross-modal supervision from pretrained visual recognition models over two-million unlabeled videos. TITLE: SoundNet: Learning Sound Representations from Unlabeled Video

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We learn rich natural sound representations by capitalizing on large amounts of unlabeled sound data collected in the wild. We leverage the natural synchronization between vision and sound to learn an acoustic representation using two-million unlabeled videos. Unlabeled video has the advantage that it can be economically acquired at massive scales, yet contains useful signals about natural sound. We propose a student-teacher training procedure which transfers discriminative

title: Spatial Transformer Networks
searchable_text:
Spatial Transformer Networks Computationally and parameter-efficient spatial invariance for neural networks processing input data and feature maps. A differentiable, learnable Spatial Transformer module inserted into convolutional architectures explicitly manipulates feature maps, conditioned on the feature map itself, without extra supervision or optimization changes. Added an insertable module that actively transforms feature maps inside existing convolutional networks instead of relying on fixed convolutional architectures alone. TITLE: Spatial Transformer Networks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Convolutional Neural Networks define an exceptionally powerful class of models, but are still limited by the lack of ability to be spatially invariant to the input data in a computationally and parameter efficient manner. In this work we introduce a new learnable module, the Spatial Transformer, which explicitly allows the spatial manipulation of data within the network. This differentiable module can be inserted into existing convolutional architectures, giving neural networks the ability to actively spat

title: Spatio-temporal Graph Convolutional Neural Network: A Deep Learning Framework for Traffic Forecasting
searchable_text:
Spatio-temporal Graph Convolutional Neural Network: A Deep Learning Framework for Traffic Forecasting Mid- and long-term traffic forecasting for urban traffic control and guidance, targeting spatial and temporal dependencies in complex traffic flow. Formulates traffic time-series prediction on graphs using complete convolutional Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks instead of regular convolutional and recurrent units. Replaces regular convolutional and recurrent units with graph-based, complete convolutional structures for faster training and fewer parameters. TITLE: Spatio-temporal Graph Convolutional Neural Network: A Deep Learning Framework for Traffic Forecasting

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Timely accurate traffic forecast is crucial for urban traffic control and guidance. Due to the high nonlinearity and complexity of traffic flow, traditional methods cannot satisfy the requirements of mid-and-long term prediction tasks and often neglect spatial and temporal dependencies. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning framework, Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (STGCN), to tackle the time series prediction

title: Spectral neighbor analysis method for automated generation of quantum-accurate interatomic potentials
searchable_text:
Spectral neighbor analysis method for automated generation of quantum-accurate interatomic potentials Automated generation of quantum-accurate interatomic potentials for solids and liquids, demonstrated on crystalline and liquid tantalum. Linear machine-learned atom energies from bispectrum components of local neighbor density, fit by weighted least-squares regression to QM energies, forces, and stresses. Compared with GAP using the same bispectrum descriptors, SNAP assumes a linear atom-energy relationship, enabling robust automated fitting to large QM datasets. TITLE: Spectral neighbor analysis method for automated generation of quantum-accurate interatomic potentials

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present a new interatomic potential for solids and liquids called Spectral Neighbor Analysis Potential (SNAP). The SNAP potential has a very general form and uses machine-learning techniques to reproduce the energies, forces, and stress tensors of a large set of small configurations of atoms, which are obtained using high-accuracy quantum electronic structure (QM) calculations. The local environment of each atom is characterized by

title: Spectral Networks and Locally Connected Networks on Graphs
searchable_text:
Spectral Networks and Locally Connected Networks on Graphs Signals defined on general graph-like domains lacking a translation group, beyond image and audio grids. Generalize CNNs to graph-domain signals using two constructions: hierarchical clustering of the domain and graph-Laplacian spectral methods. Moves CNN-like architectures from translation-invariant image/audio domains to non-translation graph domains via clustering-based and Laplacian-spectrum constructions. TITLE: Spectral Networks and Locally Connected Networks on Graphs

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Convolutional Neural Networks are extremely efficient architectures in image and audio recognition tasks, thanks to their ability to exploit the local translational invariance of signal classes over their domain. In this paper we consider possible generalizations of CNNs to signals defined on more general domains without the action of a translation group. title: Spectrally-normalized margin bounds for neural networks
searchable_text:
Spectrally-normalized margin bounds for neural networks Generalization analysis for neural networks, empirically examined on AlexNet trained with SGD on MNIST and CIFAR-10 using original and random labels. A margin-based multiclass generalization bound using margin-normalized spectral complexity: product of layer spectral norms as Lipschitz constant, times a correction factor. Recasts neural-network margin bounds around margin-normalized spectral complexity, linking generalization to the product of spectral norms plus a correction factor. TITLE: Spectrally-normalized margin bounds for neural networks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper presents a margin-based multiclass generalization bound for neural networks that scales with their margin-normalized "spectral complexity": their Lipschitz constant, meaning the product of the spectral norms of the weight matrices, times a certain correction factor. title: Stabilising Experience Replay for Deep Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
Stabilising Experience Replay for Deep Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Deep multi-agent reinforcement learning for decentralized tasks such as StarCraft unit micromanagement, where independent learners use replay memory under nonstationarity. Stabilise multi-agent experience replay by decaying obsolete samples with multi-agent importance sampling and conditioning value functions on replay-age fingerprints. Compared with independent Q-learning using standard deep replay, the paper adds obsolete-data decay via multi-agent importance sampling and replay-age disambiguation via fingerprints. TITLE: Stabilising Experience Replay for Deep Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many real-world problems, such as network packet routing and urban traffic control, are naturally modeled as multi-agent reinforcement learning (RL) problems. However, existing multi-agent RL methods typically scale poorly in the problem size. Therefore, a key challenge is to translate the success of deep learning on single-agent RL to the multi-agent setting. A major stumbling block is that independent Q-learning, the most popular multi-agent RL method, introdu

title: Stabilizer Codes and Quantum Error Correction
searchable_text:
Stabilizer Codes and Quantum Error Correction Quantum error correction for operational errors and decoherence in quantum computation and specified many-particle entangled states. Use a group-theoretical stabilizer-code formalism to construct and analyze quantum error-correcting codes. Shift from general quantum error correction to a group-theoretical stabilizer-code subclass for organizing codes, bounds, capacity, and fault tolerance. TITLE: Stabilizer Codes and Quantum Error Correction

YEAR: 1997

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Controlling operational errors and decoherence is one of the major challenges facing the field of quantum computation and other attempts to create specified many-particle entangled states. The field of quantum error correction has developed to meet this challenge. A group-theoretical structure and associated subclass of quantum codes, the stabilizer codes, has proved particularly fruitful in producing codes and in understanding the structure of both specific codes and classes of codes. title: StarCraft II: A New Challenge for Reinforcement Learning
searchable_text:
StarCraft II: A New Challenge for Reinforcement Learning Deep reinforcement learning in StarCraft II: multi-agent play under partial observability, huge action/state spaces, raw feature-plane observations, and long-horizon credit assignment. SC2LE: an open-source Python reinforcement-learning interface to StarCraft II, with specified observations, actions, rewards, mini-games, and expert replay data. Compared with prior RL benchmarks, SC2LE introduces a more difficult grand challenge with multi-agent interaction, imperfect information, large spaces, long horizons, mini-games, and human replays. TITLE: StarCraft II: A New Challenge for Reinforcement Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This paper introduces SC2LE (StarCraft II Learning Environment), a reinforcement learning environment based on the StarCraft II game. This domain poses a new grand challenge for reinforcement learning, representing a more difficult class of problems than considered in most prior work. title: Statistical Properties of Superflares on Solar-type Stars: Results Using All of the Kepler Primary Mission Data
searchable_text:
Statistical Properties of Superflares on Solar-type Stars: Results Using All of the Kepler Primary Mission Data Statistical frequency and energy characterization of superflares on solar-type and Sun-like stars. Analyze all Kepler primary mission and Gaia DR2 data with an updated high-pass-filter flare detector, while accounting for gyrochronology and flare-detection completeness. Compared with Notsu et al., the study used much larger samples and increased detected superflares from 527 to 2341 on solar-type stars and from three to 26 on Sun-like stars. ABSTRACT:

We updated the flare detection method from our previous studies by using a high-pass filter to remove rotational variations caused by starspots. title: Steerable CNNs
searchable_text:
Steerable CNNs Vision tasks requiring invariant or equivariant representations, especially CIFAR image classification. Steerable CNNs build equivariant convolutional networks from steerable representations composed of elementary feature types associated with symmetries, using type-dependent filter-bank parameterization. Compared with standard CNNs, the change is an explicit steerable representation type system that organizes features by symmetry and guides parameter-efficient filter-bank construction. TITLE: Steerable CNNs

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
It has long been recognized that the invariance and equivariance properties of a representation are critically important for success in many vision tasks. In this paper we present Steerable Convolutional Neural Networks, an efficient and flexible class of equivariant convolutional networks. We show that steerable CNNs achieve state of the art results on the CIFAR image classification benchmark. title: Stick-Breaking Policy Learning in Dec-POMDPs

Stick-Breaking Policy Learning in Dec-POMDPs Learning finite-state controllers for local policies in large decentralized partially observable Markov decision processes. Represents each agent’s local policy as variable-sized finite-state controllers constructed with a stick-breaking prior and learns controller parameters via variational Bayesian inference. Replaces fixed-size FSC EM learning with variable-sized stick-breaking FSC representation and variational Bayesian learning, not requiring an available Dec-POMDP model. Expectation maximization (EM) has recently been shown to be an efficient algorithm for learning finite-state controllers (FSCs) in large decentralized POMDPs (Dec-POMDPs). title: Sticking the Landing: Simple, Lower-Variance Gradient Estimators for Variational Inference
searchable_text:
Sticking the Landing: Simple, Lower-Variance Gradient Estimators for Variational Inference Gradient estimation for variational inference evidence lower bounds, including more complex variational distributions such as mixtures and importance-weighted posteriors. Remove the score-function part of the total derivative in the standard reparameterized variational ELBO gradient estimator to obtain a simpler unbiased estimator. Compared with the standard reparameterized gradient estimator, it removes the derivative component corresponding to the score function. We propose a simple and general variant of the standard reparameterized gradient estimator for the variational evidence lower bound. Specifically, we remove a part of the total derivative with respect to the variational parameters that corresponds to the score function. title: Stochastic Optimization for Large-scale Optimal Transport
searchable_text:
Stochastic Optimization for Large-scale Optimal Transport Large-scale optimal transport comparison of arbitrary discrete or continuous probability distributions accessible through sampling in machine-learning settings. Recast dual OT as expectation maximization and use entropic regularization to obtain smooth dual objectives solved by stochastic optimization, including incremental schemes, averaged SGD, and RKHS SGD. Instead of discretizing densities or relying on Sinkhorn, the work manipulates distributions by sampling and optimizes stochastic dual formulations with convergence guarantees. TITLE: Stochastic Optimization for Large-scale Optimal Transport

Optimal transport (OT) defines a powerful framework to compare probability distributions in a geometrically faithful way. However, the practical impact of OT is still limited because of its computational burden. We propose a new class of stochastic optimization algorithms to cope with large-scale problems routinely encountered in machine learning applications. title: Stochastic superoptimization
searchable_text:
Stochastic superoptimization Loop-free binary superoptimization for 64-bit x86 programs, starting from unoptimized LLVM-compiled binaries. Encode correctness and performance as cost-function terms, then use Markov Chain Monte Carlo to stochastically search possible loop-free binary programs. Superoptimization is reframed from exhaustive/complete search to stochastic MCMC exploration, enabling broader program scope and higher-quality outputs than existing superoptimizers. TITLE: Stochastic superoptimization

ABSTRACT:
We formulate the loop-free binary superoptimization task as a stochastic search problem. The competing constraints of transformation correctness and performance improvement are encoded as terms in a cost function, and a Markov Chain Monte Carlo sampler is used to rapidly explore the space of all possible programs to find one that is an optimization of a given target program. title: Stochastic synchronization of neuronal populations with intrinsic and extrinsic noise
searchable_text:
Stochastic synchronization of neuronal populations with intrinsic and extrinsic noise Stochastic phase synchronization in uncoupled neuronal population oscillators driven by common extrinsic noise and disrupted by intrinsic noise. Phase reduction and averaging applied to a Langevin approximation of a neural master equation with intrinsic and extrinsic noise. Extends noise-induced phase synchronization theory to neural master equations for stochastic neuronal population dynamics with both intrinsic and extrinsic noise. TITLE: Stochastic synchronization of neuronal populations with intrinsic and extrinsic noise

We extend the theory of noise-induced phase synchronization to the case of a neural master equation describing the stochastic dynamics of an ensemble of uncoupled neuronal population oscillators with intrinsic and extrinsic noise. title: Strengths and Weaknesses of Quantum Computing
searchable_text:
Strengths and Weaknesses of Quantum Computing Whether quantum polynomial time can efficiently solve all of NP, especially oracle-relative NP and NP∩coNP decision problems. Random-oracle and random-permutation oracle lower-bound proofs for quantum Turing machines, showing required query/runtime growth for solving NP and NP∩coNP. title: Structured Generative Models of Natural Source Code
searchable_text:
Structured Generative Models of Natural Source Code Building generative models of natural source code written and understood by humans. Generative models for natural source code combining sequential and hierarchical structure, distributed code-element representations, and close compiler integration, with scope-aware identifier generation. Introduces structured NSC generation that integrates sequential and hierarchical structure, learned distributed representations, compiler abstractions, and scope-sensitive identifier modeling. We study the problem of building generative models of natural source code (NSC); that is, source code written and understood by humans. Our primary contribution is to describe a family of generative models for NSC that have three key properties: First, they incorporate both sequential and hierarchical structure. title: Supervised Dimensionality Reduction via Distance Correlation Maximization

Supervised Dimensionality Reduction via Distance Correlation Maximization Supervised dimensionality reduction for learning low-dimensional feature representations from covariates and response variables without specifying regression distributions or model form. Learn a low-dimensional representation maximizing squared summed Distance Correlations between projected features and both response y and covariates x, optimized via Generalized Minimization Maximization. title: Supervised Learning Detection of Sixty Non-transiting Hot Jupiter Candidates
searchable_text:
Supervised Learning Detection of Sixty Non-transiting Hot Jupiter Candidates Detection of non-transiting short-period giant planet candidates in the Kepler field using stellar photometric phase variations. Use supervised learning trained on synthetic optical phase curves to identify time-dependent phase-curve signals indicating non-transiting short-period giant planets. title: Supervised learning in spiking neural networks with FORCE training

Supervised learning in spiking neural networks with FORCE training Spiking neuronal networks tasked with mimicking dynamical systems, classifying inputs, storing song-note sequences, reproducing songbird singing, and storing/replaying a movie scene. Populations of neurons display an extraordinary diversity in the behaviors they affect and display. title: Supervised Learning of Universal Sentence Representations from Natural Language Inference Data
searchable_text:
Supervised Learning of Universal Sentence Representations from Natural Language Inference Data Universal sentence representations for transfer to downstream NLP tasks. Train universal sentence encoders using supervised Stanford Natural Language Inference data for transferable sentence representations. Several attempts at learning unsupervised representations of sentences have not reached satisfactory enough performance to be widely adopted. title: Surface code quantum computing by lattice surgery
searchable_text:
Surface code quantum computing by lattice surgery Fault-tolerant universal quantum computation with surface-code logical qubits in experimentally realistic two-dimensional nearest-neighbour architectures. Split and merge planar surface-code lattices to couple encoded qubits and perform universal logic, including magic-state injection, while preserving a strictly 2D nearest-neighbour layout. Replaced transversal planar-code interactions and defect-code braided logic with split/merge operations on planar code surfaces, further reducing resources via a rotated planar lattice. In recent years, surface codes have become a leading method for quantum error correction in theoretical large-scale computational and communications architecture designs. Their comparatively high fault-tolerant thresholds and their natural two-dimensional nearest-neighbour (2DNN) structure make them an obvious choice for large scale designs in experimentally realistic systems. title: Survey of Consistent Software-Defined Network Updates
searchable_text:
Survey of Consistent Software-Defined Network Updates Fast and consistent updating of computer networks under policy changes, increasing traffic, or failures. Structured survey identifying consistency properties, algorithmic techniques, and speed/cost tradeoffs for fast, consistent SDN/network updates. Recasts established network-update problems in an SDN-motivated structured survey with historical perspective and links to classic algorithmic optimization. ABSTRACT:
Computer networks have become a critical infrastructure. title: Synchronization of delayed coupled neurons in presence of inhomogeneity
searchable_text:
Synchronization of delayed coupled neurons in presence of inhomogeneity Zero phase lag and phase-locked synchronization in heterogeneous systems of two delayed coupled neurons. Analytical study of inphase and near-inphase synchronization in two delayed coupled heterogeneous neurons, including asymmetric connections, supported by numerical biologically plausible models. Instead of requiring identical directly coupled oscillators, delayed coupling plus compensating inhomogeneity sources enables zero or near-zero phase synchronization in heterogeneous neurons. title: TALL: Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query

TALL: Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query Temporal localization of activities in untrimmed videos using natural language queries instead of a predefined action list. CTRL jointly models natural-language queries and video clips, producing cross-modal alignment scores and temporal boundary regression for candidate clips. Replaces predefined-action sliding-window classifiers with natural-language-query localization and introduces Charades-STA sentence temporal annotations. ABSTRACT:
This paper focuses on temporal localization of actions in untrimmed videos. Existing methods typically train classifiers for a pre-defined list of actions and apply them in a sliding window fashion. However, activities in the wild consist of a wide combination of actors, actions and objects; it is difficult to design a proper activity list that meets users’ needs. title: Team optimal control of coupled subsystems with mean-field sharing

Team optimal control of coupled subsystems with mean-field sharing Team optimal control for stochastic coupled subsystems where each controller observes its local state and the population mean-field. YEAR: 2014

ABSTRACT:
We investigate team optimal control of stochastic subsystems that are weakly coupled in dynamics (through the mean-field of the system) and are arbitrary coupled in the cost. title: Tell me Dave: Context-sensitive grounding of natural language to manipulation instructions
searchable_text:
Tell me Dave: Context-sensitive grounding of natural language to manipulation instructions Interpreting free-form natural language commands for sequences of mobile manipulation tasks in new robotic environments with varying object presence, location, and state. Energy-based context-sensitive grounding model, isomorphic to a conditional random field, using verb–environment instruction libraries plus environment context and task constraints to map natural language to robot manipulation instructions. title: TERRESTRIAL PLANET OCCURRENCE RATES FOR THE KEPLER GK DWARF SAMPLE

title: The appropriate use of radiography in clinical practice: a report of two cases of biomechanical versus malignant spine pain
searchable_text:
The appropriate use of radiography in clinical practice: a report of two cases of biomechanical versus malignant spine pain Back-pain patients initially appearing to have non-organic biomechanical spine pain but ultimately having malignant etiology in multidisciplinary outpatient chiropractic/medical practice. Retrospective case review evaluating conservative spine-pain management, delayed clinical re-evaluation, and escalation from plain-film radiography to magnetic resonance imaging when malignancy-relevant features emerged. The emphasis changed from early radiography for suspected biomechanical pain to judicious MRI after re-evaluation, persistent symptoms, or new severe pain suggested malignant causes. TITLE: The appropriate use of radiography in clinical practice: a report of two cases of biomechanical versus malignant spine pain

title: The Arcade Learning Environment: An Evaluation Platform for General Agents
searchable_text:
The Arcade Learning Environment: An Evaluation Platform for General Agents Evaluating general, domain-independent AI agents across diverse Atari 2600 game environments. A public platform and methodology interfacing with hundreds of Atari 2600 games to benchmark domain-independent reinforcement-learning and planning agents. Introduced a unified Atari 2600 evaluation environment with hundreds of games, public benchmark agents, and methodology for cross-game empirical comparison. TITLE: The Arcade Learning Environment: An Evaluation Platform for General Agents

In this article we introduce the Arcade Learning Environment (ALE): both a challenge problem and a platform and methodology for evaluating the development of general, domain-independent AI technology. title: The automated computation of tree-level and next-to-leading order differential cross sections, and their matching to parton shower simulations

A unified, highly parallelized program automates fixed-order, shower-matched, and merged tree-level/NLO cross-section computations with user input limited to physics quantities. It consolidates fixed-order, shower-matched, and merged computations into one flexible automated framework with high parallelisation and minimal human intervention. TITLE: The automated computation of tree-level and next-to-leading order differential cross sections, and their matching to parton shower simulations

title: THE BARYON OSCILLATION SPECTROSCOPIC SURVEY OF SDSS-III
searchable_text:
THE BARYON OSCILLATION SPECTROSCOPIC SURVEY OF SDSS-III Precision cosmological distance and expansion constraints from BAO in matter clustering at z < 0.7 and the Lyα forest at 2.15 < z < 3.5. Spectroscopic survey of 1.5M luminous galaxies and >150k quasar Lyα forest spectra to measure BAO across low and high redshifts. BOSS expands BAO spectroscopic mapping to a larger volume than all previous large-scale-structure spectroscopic surveys combined. TITLE: THE BARYON OSCILLATION SPECTROSCOPIC SURVEY OF SDSS-III

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']
