The Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey (BOSS) is designed to measure the scale of baryon acoustic oscillations (BAO) in the clustering of matter over a larger volume than the combined efforts of all previous spectroscopic surveys of large-scale structure. BOSS uses 1.5 million luminous galaxies as faint as i = 19.9 over 10,000 deg2 to measure BAO to redshifts z < 0.7. Observations of neutral hydrogen in the Lyα forest in more than 150,000 quasar spectra (g < 22) will constra

title: The Bose-Hubbard Model is QMA-complete
searchable_text:
The Bose-Hubbard Model is QMA-complete Approximating the ground energy of the Bose-Hubbard model on a graph at fixed particle number, plus related graph-defined 2-local Hamiltonians generalizing the XY model. Encode an n-qubit computation history in the hard-core boson subspace, leverage the hard-core-boson/spin mapping, and avoid perturbation-theory gadgets. Unlike perturbative Hamiltonian-complexity constructions, the analysis avoids perturbation theory and therefore avoids multiplying Hamiltonian terms by large coefficients. TITLE: The Bose-Hubbard Model is QMA-complete

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Bose-Hubbard model is a system of interacting bosons that live on the vertices of a graph. The particles can move between adjacent vertices and experience a repulsive on-site interaction. The Hamiltonian is determined by a choice of graph that specifies the geometry in which the particles move and interact. We prove that approximating the ground energy of the Bose-Hubbard model on a graph at fixed particle number is QMA-complete. In our QMA-hardness proof, we encode the history of an n-qubit

title: The Building Blocks of Inter-operability
searchable_text:
The Building Blocks of Inter-operability Patient demographic attribute availability for linking patient records and improving patient matching interoperability in U.S. healthcare systems. Compile 36 stakeholder-defined demographic elements and measure their availability longitudinally across multiple geographically distinct health systems. Shifted from identifying essential demographic attributes to empirically measuring their multisite, longitudinal availability and changes over time. TITLE: The Building Blocks of Inter-operability

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Summary Background: Patient matching is a key barrier to achieving interoperability. Patient demographic elements must be consistently collected over time and region to be valuable elements for patient matching. Objectives: We sought to determine what patient demographic attributes are collected at multiple institutions in the United States and see how their availability changes over time and across clinical sites. Methods: We compiled a list of 36 demographic elements that stakeholders previou

title: The California-Kepler Survey. I. High-resolution Spectroscopy of 1305 Stars Hosting Kepler Transiting Planets
searchable_text:
The California-Kepler Survey. I. High-resolution Spectroscopy of 1305 Stars Hosting Kepler Transiting Planets Uniform characterization of stars hosting Kepler transiting planets, supporting improved stellar properties, revised planet properties, and ensemble statistics. Acquire high-resolution optical HIRES/Keck spectra of Kepler planet-host stars and infer spectroscopic parameters using template matching plus direct spectral synthesis/radiative transfer. Compared with prior Kepler host characterization, CKS adds a large uniform high-resolution, high-SNR HIRES spectral survey, improving knowledge of transiting-planet host-star properties. TITLE: The California-Kepler Survey. I. High-resolution Spectroscopy of 1305 Stars Hosting Kepler Transiting Planets

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The California-Kepler Survey (CKS) is an observational program developed to improve our knowledge of the properties of stars found to host transiting planets by NASA’s Kepler Mission. The improvement stems from new high-resolution optical spectra obtained using HIRES at the W. M. Keck Observatory. The CKS stellar sample comprises 1305 stars classified as Kepler objects of interest, hosting a total of 2075 transiting planets. T

title: The Cityscapes Dataset for Semantic Urban Scene Understanding
searchable_text:
The Cityscapes Dataset for Semantic Urban Scene Understanding Semantic urban scene understanding for complex real-world street scenes, targeting pixel-level and instance-level semantic labeling. A benchmark suite and large-scale dataset of stereo urban street sequences with pixel-level, instance-level, high-quality, and coarse semantic annotations. Compared with previous datasets, Cityscapes increases dataset size, annotation richness, scene variability, and complexity, while adding fine and coarse annotations from 50 cities. TITLE: The Cityscapes Dataset for Semantic Urban Scene Understanding

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Visual understanding of complex urban street scenes is an enabling factor for a wide range of applications. Object detection has benefited enormously from large-scale datasets, especially in the context of deep learning. For semantic urban scene understanding, however, no current dataset adequately captures the complexity of real-world urban scenes. To address this, we introduce Cityscapes, a benchmark suite and large-scale dataset to train and test approaches for pixel-lev

title: The CMA Evolution Strategy: A Tutorial
searchable_text:
The CMA Evolution Strategy: A Tutorial Real-parameter continuous-domain optimization of non-linear, non-convex functions. A stochastic evolution strategy for continuous optimization that uses covariance matrix adaptation to guide randomized search over real-valued parameters. Covariance Matrix Adaptation is the distinguishing addition to the Evolution Strategy. TITLE: The CMA Evolution Strategy: A Tutorial

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
This tutorial introduces the CMA Evolution Strategy (ES), where CMA stands for Covariance Matrix Adaptation. The CMA-ES is a stochastic, or randomized, method for real-parameter (continuous domain) optimization of non-linear, non-convex functions. We try to motivate and derive the algorithm from intuitive concepts and from requirements of non-linear, non-convex search in continuous domain.

title: The Deep Ritz Method: A Deep Learning-Based Numerical Algorithm for Solving Variational Problems
searchable_text:
The Deep Ritz Method: A Deep Learning-Based Numerical Algorithm for Solving Variational Problems Numerical solution of variational problems, especially those arising from partial differential equations, including eigenvalue problems. A deep learning-based Deep Ritz Method optimized within a simple framework compatible with stochastic gradient descent. Recasts Ritz-style variational problem solving as a deep learning-based method that fits stochastic gradient descent. TITLE: The Deep Ritz Method: A Deep Learning-Based Numerical Algorithm for Solving Variational Problems

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a deep learning-based method, the Deep Ritz Method, for numerically solving variational problems, particularly the ones that arise from partial differential equations. The Deep Ritz Method is naturally nonlinear, naturally adaptive and has the potential to work in rather high dimensions. The framework is quite simple and fits well with the stochastic gradient descent method used in deep learning. We illustrate the method on seve

title: The density-matrix renormalization group in the age of matrix product states
searchable_text:
The density-matrix renormalization group in the age of matrix product states Simulation of statics and dynamics of one-dimensional strongly correlated quantum lattice systems. Reformulate and implement the DMRG algorithm family exclusively in matrix product state terms to expose inner structure, potential, and limitations. Shifted DMRG thinking from traditional formulations to an explicitly MPS-language exposition and implementation perspective. TITLE: The density-matrix renormalization group in the age of matrix product states

YEAR: 2010

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Abstract The density-matrix renormalization group method (DMRG) has established itself over the last decade as the leading method for the simulation of the statics and dynamics of one-dimensional strongly correlated quantum lattice systems. title: The Derivation, Properties, and Value of Kepler’s Combined Differential Photometric Precision
searchable_text:
The Derivation, Properties, and Value of Kepler’s Combined Differential Photometric Precision Evaluating detectability of weak Earth-size planetary transit signatures in Kepler photometric light curves and estimating Kepler survey target completeness. Calculate Kepler Combined Differential Photometric Precision and publish per-target rms CDPP for 3-, 6-, and 12-hour transit durations to assess transit detectability and completeness. Introduces per-target rms CDPP tables for 3-, 6-, and 12-hour transit durations, available for all Kepler observations. TITLE: The Derivation, Properties, and Value of Kepler’s Combined Differential Photometric Precision

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Kepler Mission is searching for Earth-size planets orbiting solar-like stars by simultaneously observing >160,000 stars to detect sequences of transit events in the photometric light curves. The Combined Differential Photometric Precision (CDPP) is the metric that defines the ease with which these weak terrestrial transit signatures can be detected. An understanding of CDPP is invaluable for evaluating the completeness of the Kepler surve

title: The ecology of human fear: survival optimization and the nervous system
searchable_text:
The ecology of human fear: survival optimization and the nervous system Explaining how humans and other animals defend against recurring and novel threats across escalating stages of danger. A Survival Optimization System integrating ecological threat-behavior repertoires, predictive safe-environment manufacturing, threat orienting/assessment, reflexive defense, neural architecture, cognitive appraisal, and model-based learning. It unifies ecological models of contextually relevant threat-induced survival behaviors with contemporary human affective science into one Survival Optimization System. TITLE: The ecology of human fear: survival optimization and the nervous system

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a Survival Optimization System (SOS) to account for the strategies that humans and other animals use to defend against recurring and novel threats. The SOS attempts to merge ecological models that define a repertoire of contextually relevant threat induced survival behaviors with contemporary approaches to human affective science. We first propose that the goal of the nervous system is to reduce surprise and optimize actions by (i) predicting the sensor

title: The effects of galaxy formation on the matter power spectrum: a challenge for precision cosmology
searchable_text:
The effects of galaxy formation on the matter power spectrum: a challenge for precision cosmology Precision weak-lensing cosmology requiring nonlinear matter power-spectrum predictions at 1% accuracy for comoving wavenumbers relevant to LSST, EUCLID, and WFIRST. Use OWLS cosmological simulations to compare baryonic processes, mass-component power, baryon back-reaction on cold dark matter, and redshift evolution of matter power-spectrum effects. Introduced a more realistic AGN-feedback simulation than previous studies, one shown to solve overcooling and reproduce optical/X-ray galaxy-group observations, shifting expectation from baryon cooling boosting power to feedback suppressing it. TITLE: The effects of galaxy formation on the matter power spectrum: a challenge for precision cosmology

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Upcoming weak lensing surveys, such as LSST, EUCLID and WFIRST, aim to measure the matter power spectrum with unprecedented accuracy. In order to fully exploit these observations, models are needed that, given a set of cosmological parameters, can predict the non-linear matter power spectrum at the level of 1 per cent or better for scales corresponding to comoving wavenumbers 0.1 k 10h Mpc −1 . We have employed the large suite of simulati

title: The Einstein Toolkit: a community computational infrastructure for relativistic astrophysics
searchable_text:
The Einstein Toolkit: a community computational infrastructure for relativistic astrophysics Numerical relativity and relativistic astrophysics simulations of black holes, compact objects, collapsing stars, and related astrophysical systems. A Cactus-based high-performance computing toolkit using Carpet adaptive mesh refinement, BSSN spacetime evolution, and finite-volume general relativistic hydrodynamics. New publicly released community code components were integrated into a freely accessible toolkit combining core simulation infrastructure with analysis tools. TITLE: The Einstein Toolkit: a community computational infrastructure for relativistic astrophysics

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We describe the Einstein Toolkit, a community-driven, freely accessible computational infrastructure intended for use in numerical relativity, relativistic astrophysics, and other applications. title: THE ENIGMATIC AND EPHEMERAL M DWARF SYSTEM KOI 6705: CHESHIRE CAT OR WILD GOOSE?
searchable_text:
THE ENIGMATIC AND EPHEMERAL M DWARF SYSTEM KOI 6705: CHESHIRE CAT OR WILD GOOSE? Determining the origin of the KOI 6705.01 transit-like signal in the Kepler light curve of the mid-M dwarf KIC 6423922. Analyze Kepler light curves plus optical/infrared spectra, exclude astrophysical and instrumental false positives, and attribute the signal to detector-column charge transfer inefficiency. The interpretation shifts from a possible Moon-sized planet around an M dwarf to a likely instrumental false positive caused by charge transfer inefficiency. TITLE: THE ENIGMATIC AND EPHEMERAL M DWARF SYSTEM KOI 6705: CHESHIRE CAT OR WILD GOOSE?

We confirm a 0.995 day periodic planetary transit-like signal, KOI 6705.01, in the Kepler light curve of the star KIC 6423922. Optical and infrared spectra show that this star is a mid M-type dwarf with an effective temperature = 3327 ± 60 ?> K, metallicity [Fe/H] = −0.08 ± 0.10, radius = 0.31 ± 0.03 ?> R⊙, and mass = 0.28 ± 0.05M⊙. title: The Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor
searchable_text:
The Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor Interpretation of genomic variants in coding and non-coding regions across diverse study designs. A configurable, extensible open-source toolset that analyzes, annotates, and prioritizes genomic variants using extensive genomic annotation through multiple interfaces.  TITLE: The Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor is a powerful toolset for the analysis, annotation, and prioritization of genomic variants in coding and non-coding regions. It provides access to an extensive collection of genomic annotation, with a variety of interfaces to suit different requirements, and simple options for configuring and extending analysis. title: THE EXOPLANET CENSUS: A GENERAL METHOD APPLIED TO KEPLER
searchable_text:
THE EXOPLANET CENSUS: A GENERAL METHOD APPLIED TO KEPLER Estimating the underlying planetary distribution function for announced Kepler planet candidates transiting solar-type stars. A maximum-likelihood PLDF fitting method for exoplanet surveys that handles multiple planets per star and arbitrary planet or target-star properties. Moves from simpler occurrence fits to a general survey likelihood accommodating multi-planet systems, arbitrary properties, and Kepler detection efficiencies. TITLE: THE EXOPLANET CENSUS: A GENERAL METHOD APPLIED TO KEPLER

YEAR: 2011

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We develop a general method to fit the underlying planetary distribution function (PLDF) to exoplanet survey data. This maximum likelihood method accommodates more than one planet per star and any number of planet or target star properties. We apply the method to announced Kepler planet candidates that transit solar-type stars. title: THE FALSE POSITIVE RATE OF KEPLER AND THE OCCURRENCE OF PLANETS
searchable_text:
THE FALSE POSITIVE RATE OF KEPLER AND THE OCCURRENCE OF PLANETS Estimating Kepler astrophysical false-positive rates and true planet occurrence by spectral type, planet size, and orbital period. Numerically simulate Kepler targets, background stars, and bound companions, apply real noise and vetting models, then compare simulated false positives to KOIs. It replaces assumed planet frequencies with direct subtraction of a simulated astrophysical false-positive population from actual Kepler candidates down to Earth size. TITLE: THE FALSE POSITIVE RATE OF KEPLER AND THE OCCURRENCE OF PLANETS

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Kepler mission is uniquely suited to study the frequencies of extrasolar planets. This goal requires knowledge of the incidence of false positives such as eclipsing binaries in the background of the targets, or physically bound to them, which can mimic the photometric signal of a transiting planet. title: The Global Schmidt law in star forming galaxies
searchable_text:
The Global Schmidt law in star forming galaxies Global star formation law in star-forming galaxies across gas densities and star formation rates. Combine Hα, H I, CO, far-infrared observations across normal spirals and infrared-selected starbursts to fit global star formation laws, especially Schmidt-law and orbital-timescale formulations. Expanded the global Schmidt-law test by combining normal spiral galaxies with infrared-selected starburst galaxies across the full observed range of gas densities and star formation rates. TITLE: The Global Schmidt law in star forming galaxies

YEAR: 1997

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Measurements of Hα, H I, and CO distributions in 61 normal spiral galaxies are combined with published far-infrared and CO observations of 36 infrared-selected starburst galaxies, in order to study the form of the global star formation law over the full range of gas densities and star formation rates (SFRs) observed in galaxies. title: The Heisenberg Representation of Quantum Computers
searchable_text:
The Heisenberg Representation of Quantum Computers Understanding and describing what quantum computers can do, especially error-correction states, communication-protocol states, and an important class of quantum operations. Describe quantum computers in the Heisenberg picture by tracking operator evolution, using stabilizer groups of tensor products of Pauli matrices for relevant operations and states. Shifted description from evolving quantum states to evolving operators, focusing on stabilizers rather than full state vectors. TITLE: The Heisenberg Representation of Quantum Computers

YEAR: 1998

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Since Shor`s discovery of an algorithm to factor numbers on a quantum computer in polynomial time, quantum computation has become a subject of immense interest. Unfortunately, one of the key features of quantum computers--the difficulty of describing them on classical computers--also makes it difficult to describe and understand precisely what can be done with them. title: The Hele–Shaw Asymptotics for Mechanical Models of Tumor Growth
searchable_text:
The Hele–Shaw Asymptotics for Mechanical Models of Tumor Growth Mathematical modeling of tumor growth dynamics, including cell density evolution and nutrient supply through vasculature. Formulate a Hele–Shaw-type free boundary tumor-growth model with growth terms by deriving it from cell-level mechanics via the stiff limit of the pressure law. Extends the classical Hele–Shaw problem to a growth-term variant derived from cell-level descriptions and stiff pressure limits, then couples nutrient vasculature effects. TITLE: The Hele–Shaw Asymptotics for Mechanical Models of Tumor Growth

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Models of tumor growth, now commonly used, present several levels of complexity, both in terms of the biomedical ingredients and the mathematical description. Our first goal here is to formulate a free boundary model of Hele–Shaw type, a variant including growth terms, starting from the description at the cell level and passing to the stiff limit in the pressure law of state. title: The human genome browser at UCSC.
searchable_text:
The human genome browser at UCSC. Rapid, reliable visualization and access to vertebrate genome annotations across any requested genomic region and scale. Web browser backed by a MYSQL database displays genome regions as stacked, coregistered annotation tracks, supports text/sequence search, secondary links, and user custom tracks. Changed from isolated sequence/annotation resources to an integrated browser combining contigs, gaps, alignments, gene predictions, homologies, variants, markers, repeats, external links, and custom user tracks. TITLE: The human genome browser at UCSC.

YEAR: 2002

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
As vertebrate genome sequences near completion and research refocuses to their analysis, the issue of effective genome annotation display becomes critical. A mature web tool for rapid and reliable display of any requested portion of the genome at any scale, together with several dozen aligned annotation tracks, is provided at http://genome.ucsc.edu. title: THE IMPACT OF STELLAR MULTIPLICITY ON PLANETARY SYSTEMS. I. THE RUINOUS INFLUENCE OF CLOSE BINARY COMPANIONS
searchable_text:
THE IMPACT OF STELLAR MULTIPLICITY ON PLANETARY SYSTEMS. I. THE RUINOUS INFLUENCE OF CLOSE BINARY COMPANIONS Quantifying how close stellar binary companions affect the occurrence, formation, and survival of planetary systems around Kepler planet-host candidates. High-resolution Keck II adaptive-optics imaging and nonredundant aperture-mask interferometry of Kepler Objects of Interest to identify and characterize binary companions around planet-host candidates. Moved from theoretical uncertainty and detection difficulty to a large high-resolution imaging survey measuring the close-binary companion deficit among Kepler planet-host candidates. TITLE: THE IMPACT OF STELLAR MULTIPLICITY ON PLANETARY SYSTEMS. I. THE RUINOUS INFLUENCE OF CLOSE BINARY COMPANIONS

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The dynamical influence of binary companions is expected to profoundly influence planetary systems. However, the difficulty of identifying planets in binary systems has left the magnitude of this effect uncertain; despite numerous theoretical hurdles to their formation and survival, at least some binary systems clearly host planets. title: The importance of modeling epileptic seizure dynamics as spatio-temporal patterns
searchable_text:
The importance of modeling epileptic seizure dynamics as spatio-temporal patterns Understanding and predicting epileptic seizure dynamics across epileptic disorders, especially two important seizure types and patient-specific treatment outcomes. Use mechanistic neural population models to describe epileptic seizure rhythms as spatio-temporal dynamics, potentially adapted to patient functional and structural data. Moves seizure modeling from generic rhythm descriptions toward mechanistic, macroscopic, spatio-temporal, patient-specific models of epileptic seizure dynamics. TITLE: The importance of modeling epileptic seizure dynamics as spatio-temporal patterns

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
The occurrence of seizures is the common feature across the spectrum of epileptic disorders. We describe how the use of mechanistic neural population models leads to novel insight into the dynamic mechanisms underlying two important types of epileptic seizures. We specifically stress the need for a spatio-temporal description of the rhythms to deal with the complexity of the pathophenotype. Adapted to functional and structural patient data, the macroscopic models

title: The Intrinsic Alignment of Galaxies and its Impact on Weak Gravitational Lensing in an Era of Precision Cosmology
searchable_text:
The Intrinsic Alignment of Galaxies and its Impact on Weak Gravitational Lensing in an Era of Precision Cosmology Intrinsic galaxy-shape alignments as contaminants and probes in cosmic shear and weak gravitational lensing for precision cosmology. Systematic synthesis of intrinsic-alignment theory, observations, large-scale weak-lensing impacts, and isolation or mitigation methods. Consolidates two decades of intrinsic-alignment work into a systematic review emphasizing large-scale cosmic-shear impact and mitigation strategies. TITLE: The Intrinsic Alignment of Galaxies and its Impact on Weak Gravitational Lensing in an Era of Precision Cosmology

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The wealth of incoming and future cosmological observations will allow us to map out the structure and evolution of the observable universe to an unprecedented level of precision. Among these observations is the weak gravitational lensing of galaxies, e.g., cosmic shear that measures the minute distortions of background galaxy images by intervening cosmic structure. title: The LAMBADA dataset: Word prediction requiring a broad discourse context
searchable_text:
The LAMBADA dataset: Word prediction requiring a broad discourse context Evaluation of computational text understanding through last-word prediction in narrative passages requiring broad discourse context rather than local sentence context. A collection of narrative passages curated so humans can guess the final word from the whole passage, but not from the last sentence alone, forcing broad discourse tracking. Introduces LAMBADA as a benchmark where target words are predictable only with whole-passage discourse, unlike local-context word prediction from the preceding sentence. TITLE: The LAMBADA dataset: Word prediction requiring a broad discourse context

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce LAMBADA, a dataset to evaluate the capabilities of computational models for text understanding by means of a word prediction task. LAMBADA is a collection of narrative passages sharing the characteristic that human subjects are able to guess their last word if they are exposed to the whole passage, but not if they only see the last sentence preceding the target word. title: The Limitations of Deep Learning in Adversarial Settings
searchable_text:
The Limitations of Deep Learning in Adversarial Settings Adversarial vulnerability of deep neural networks, especially computer-vision inputs intentionally crafted to cause targeted misclassification. Formalize adversary space and craft adversarial samples using algorithms that exploit the DNN input-output mapping. Introduced a formal adversary space and a novel targeted adversarial-sample crafting class based on understanding DNN input-output mappings. TITLE: The Limitations of Deep Learning in Adversarial Settings

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Deep learning takes advantage of large datasets and computationally efficient training algorithms to outperform other approaches at various machine learning tasks. However, imperfections in the training phase of deep neural networks make them vulnerable to adversarial samples: inputs crafted by adversaries with the intent of causing deep neural networks to misclassify. In this work, we formalize the space of adversaries against deep neural networks (DNNs) and introduce a novel c

title: The LOFAR pilot surveys for pulsars and fast radio transients
searchable_text:
The LOFAR pilot surveys for pulsars and fast radio transients Low-frequency radio searches for pulsars and dispersed fast radio transients across large northern-sky areas. LOFAR pilot surveys at ~140 MHz used incoherent high-band antenna fields and coherent Superterp tied-array beams to search northern-sky pointings for pulsars and fast transients. Compared with LPPS, LOTAS surveyed less sky but achieved roughly fivefold higher sensitivity via coherent Superterp tied-array beams covering 4 sq. deg per pointing. TITLE: The LOFAR pilot surveys for pulsars and fast radio transients

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We have conducted two pilot surveys for radio pulsars and fast transients with the Low-Frequency Array (LOFAR) around 140 MHz and here report on the first low-frequency fast-radio burst limit and the discovery of two new pulsars. The first survey, the LOFAR Pilot Pulsar Survey (LPPS), observed a large fraction of the northern sky, ~1.4 x 10^4 sq. deg, with 1-hr dwell times. Each observation covered ~75 sq. deg using 7 independent fields formed by incoherently summing the hi

title: The magnetic field and turbulence of the cosmic web measured using a brilliant fast radio burst
searchable_text:
The magnetic field and turbulence of the cosmic web measured using a brilliant fast radio burst Constraining intergalactic magnetic fields and turbulence in the cosmic web using fast radio bursts as cosmological probes. Use a bright, polarized FRB’s wavelength-dependent Faraday rotation plus dispersion delay and scintillation to infer line-of-sight magnetic field and intergalactic turbulence. An exceptionally bright FRB enabled direct localization and polarization/Faraday measurements sufficient to constrain the intergalactic magnetic field along one sightline. TITLE: The magnetic field and turbulence of the cosmic web measured using a brilliant fast radio burst

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Probing the intergalactic magnetic field Fast radio bursts (FRBs) are powerful flashes of astronomical radio waves that last just milliseconds and whose origins are a matter of debate. Ravi et al. discovered a FRB that is exceptionally bright (see the Perspective by Kaspi). This allowed them to measure how the polarization of the signal varies with wavelength (Faraday rotation). Combining this with the time delay of the flash at different w

title: The Marginal Value of Adaptive Gradient Methods in Machine Learning
searchable_text:
The Marginal Value of Adaptive Gradient Methods in Machine Learning Training and generalization behavior of adaptive gradient methods for machine-learning and deep neural network optimization. Construct a linearly separable overparameterized classification counterexample and empirically compare AdaGrad, Adam, and RMSProp against GD/SGD on deep-learning generalization. Shifted evaluation of adaptive methods from optimization convenience/training performance to their implicit solution bias and downstream generalization relative to GD/SGD. TITLE: The Marginal Value of Adaptive Gradient Methods in Machine Learning

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Adaptive optimization methods, which perform local optimization with a metric constructed from the history of iterates, are becoming increasingly popular for training deep neural networks. Examples include AdaGrad, RMSProp, and Adam. We show that for simple overparameterized problems, adaptive methods often find drastically different solutions than gradient descent (GD) or stochastic gradient descent (SGD). We construct an illustrative binary classification problem wh

title: THE MASS OF Kepler-93b AND THE COMPOSITION OF TERRESTRIAL PLANETS
searchable_text:
THE MASS OF Kepler-93b AND THE COMPOSITION OF TERRESTRIAL PLANETS Determining Kepler-93b’s mass, density, and rocky composition, and situating it within the mass–radius composition trend of terrestrial exoplanets. Combine HARPS-N and archival Keck/HIRES radial-velocity observations to estimate Kepler-93b’s mass, then compare its density with dense planets of known mass and radius. Added a precise Kepler-93b mass from new HARPS-N plus archival Keck/HIRES radial velocities, enabling a stronger density and terrestrial-composition comparison. TITLE: THE MASS OF Kepler-93b AND THE COMPOSITION OF TERRESTRIAL PLANETS

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Kepler-93b is a 1.478 ± 0.019 R⊕ planet with a 4.7 day period around a bright (V = 10.2), astroseismically characterized host star with a mass of 0.911 ± 0.033 M☉ and a radius of 0.919 ± 0.011 R☉. Based on 86 radial velocity observations obtained with the HARPS-N spectrograph on the Telescopio Nazionale Galileo and 32 archival Keck/HIRES observations, we present a precise mass estimate of 4.02 ± 0.68 M⊕. The corresponding high density of 6.88 ± 1.18 g cm−3 is consistent

title: The Numerics of GANs
searchable_text:
The Numerics of GANs Training Generative Adversarial Networks, especially common and notoriously hard-to-train GAN architectures. Smooth two-player game analysis of GAN gradient vector-field Jacobians to design a convergence-improving training algorithm addressing problematic eigenvalue structure. Instead of relying on current GAN training algorithms, it introduces a new algorithm motivated by numerical analysis of the GAN gradient vector field. TITLE: The Numerics of GANs

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this paper, we analyze the numerics of common algorithms for training Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). Using the formalism of smooth two-player games we analyze the associated gradient vector field of GAN training objectives. Our findings suggest that the convergence of current algorithms suffers due to two factors: i) presence of eigenvalues of the Jacobian of the gradient vector field with zero real-part, and ii) eigenvalues with big imaginary part. Using these findings, we design a new algorithm that

title: THE OCCURRENCE OF POTENTIALLY HABITABLE PLANETS ORBITING M DWARFS ESTIMATED FROM THE FULL KEPLER DATASET AND AN EMPIRICAL MEASUREMENT OF THE DETECTION SENSITIVITY
searchable_text:
THE OCCURRENCE OF POTENTIALLY HABITABLE PLANETS ORBITING M DWARFS ESTIMATED FROM THE FULL KEPLER DATASET AND AN EMPIRICAL MEASUREMENT OF THE DETECTION SENSITIVITY Estimating occurrence rates of small, potentially habitable planets orbiting M dwarfs in Kepler data. Search the full four-year Kepler M-dwarf dataset with a custom transit pipeline, then empirically calibrate star-by-star completeness through transit injection and recovery simulations. It improves prior occurrence estimates by using the full four-year Kepler dataset plus empirical detection sensitivity measurements from transit injection and recovery. TITLE: THE OCCURRENCE OF POTENTIALLY HABITABLE PLANETS ORBITING M DWARFS ESTIMATED FROM THE FULL KEPLER DATASET AND AN EMPIRICAL MEASUREMENT OF THE DETECTION SENSITIVITY

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We present an improved estimate of the occurrence rate of small planets orbiting small stars by searching the full four-year Kepler data set for transiting planets using our own planet detection pipeline and conducting transit injection and recovery simulations to empirically measure the search completeness of our pipeline. We identified 156 planet candidates, including one

title: THE OCCURRENCE RATE OF SMALL PLANETS AROUND SMALL STARS
searchable_text:
THE OCCURRENCE RATE OF SMALL PLANETS AROUND SMALL STARS Occurrence rates of small planets, including habitable-zone Earth-size planets, orbiting cool Kepler stars below 4000 K. Use Kepler optical/near-infrared photometry to improve cool-star properties, refit transits, revise planet radii, and compare candidates with detectable-star counts to estimate occurrence rates. Improved cool-star radii and refitted transit radius ratios replaced prior planet-size estimates, enabling revised planet radii and occurrence-rate calculations for cool Kepler targets. TITLE: THE OCCURRENCE RATE OF SMALL PLANETS AROUND SMALL STARS

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We use the optical and near-infrared photometry from the Kepler Input Catalog to provide improved estimates of the stellar characteristics of the smallest stars in the Kepler target list. We find 3897 dwarfs with temperatures below 4000 K, including 64 planet candidate host stars orbited by 95 transiting planet candidates. We refit the transit events in the Kepler light curves for these planet candidates and combine the revised planet/star radius ratios with our improved stellar

title: The onion universe: all sky lightcone simulations in spherical shells
searchable_text:
The onion universe: all sky lightcone simulations in spherical shells Compressed all-sky large-scale-structure products for survey analyses with limited radial/redshift resolution, especially BAO galaxy clustering and weak-lensing convergence studies. Run a 2048^3-particle GADGET-2 N-body lightcone simulation, then project matter into concentric finite-width spherical shells to build all-sky angular “onion” maps. Moves from raw lightcone simulation data to finite-width spherical-shell angular maps, adding a new 2048^3-particle all-sky simulation compressed by more than a factor of 1000. TITLE: The onion universe: all sky lightcone simulations in spherical shells

YEAR: 2007

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Galaxy surveys provide a large-scale view of the universe that typically has a limited line of sight or redshift resolution. The lack of radial accuracy in these surveys can be modelled by picturing the universe as a set of concentric radial shells of finite width around the observer, i.e. an onion-like structure. We present a new N-body simulation with 2048 3 particles developed at the Marenostrum supercomputer with the GADGET-2 code. Using the lightcone output we

title: The physics driving the cosmic star formation history
searchable_text:
The physics driving the cosmic star formation history Explaining the physics controlling the cosmic star formation history across redshift, including its rise, peak, and decline toward the present. Use more than 50 large cosmological hydrodynamical OWLS simulations, systematically varying model parameters to identify dominant physical processes and robust aspects of cosmic star formation history. Moves from single-model explanation to systematic parameter variation across the OWLS simulation suite to test which processes dominate and which results are robust. TITLE: The physics driving the cosmic star formation history

YEAR: 2009

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We investigate the physics driving the cosmic star formation (SF) history using the more than 50 large, cosmological, hydrodynamical simulations that together comprise the OverWhelmingly Large Simulations project. We systematically vary the parameters of the model to determine which physical processes are dominant and which aspects of the model are robust. Generically, we find that SF is limited by the build-up of dark matter haloes at high redshift, reaches a broad maximum at inte

title: THE POPULATION OF LONG-PERIOD TRANSITING EXOPLANETS
searchable_text:
THE POPULATION OF LONG-PERIOD TRANSITING EXOPLANETS Population and occurrence rate of long-period transiting exoplanets around the ∼40,000 brightest Sun-like Kepler targets. Fully automated search of archival Kepler light curves for long-period exoplanets showing only one or two transits, with empirical detection-efficiency characterization. Moved from short-period-biased Kepler transit catalogs to a no-human-intervention search for single- or double-transit long-period candidates with empirical detection efficiency. TITLE: THE POPULATION OF LONG-PERIOD TRANSITING EXOPLANETS

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The Kepler mission has discovered thousands of exoplanets and revolutionized our understanding of their population. This large, homogeneous catalog of discoveries has enabled rigorous studies of the occurrence rate of exoplanets and planetary systems as a function of their physical properties. However, transit surveys such as Kepler are most sensitive to planets with orbital periods much shorter than the orbital periods of Jupiter and Saturn, the most massive planets in our solar sys

title: The Power of Normalization: Faster Evasion of Saddle Points
searchable_text:
The Power of Normalization: Faster Evasion of Saddle Points Non-convex optimization requiring convergence to local minima and saddle-point evasion, demonstrated on online tensor decomposition. Normalized Gradient Descent using only gradient direction, with carefully chosen parameters and noise injection to evade saddle points. Adds parameter tuning and noise injection to NGD, improving rates over Ge et al. (2015)’s fastest known first-order algorithm. TITLE: The Power of Normalization: Faster Evasion of Saddle Points

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A commonly used heuristic in non-convex optimization is Normalized Gradient Descent (NGD) - a variant of gradient descent in which only the direction of the gradient is taken into account and its magnitude ignored. We analyze this heuristic and show that with carefully chosen parameters and noise injection, this method can provably evade saddle points. We establish the convergence of NGD to a local minimum, and demonstrate rates which improve upon the fastest known first orde

title: The Predictron: End-To-End Learning and Planning
searchable_text:
The Predictron: End-To-End Learning and Planning Learning models that support planning, tested on procedurally generated random mazes and a simulator for the game of pool. A fully abstract Markov reward process model rolled forward for multiple imagined planning steps, accumulating internal rewards and values, trained end-to-end to approximate true value. Replaces conventional deep neural network prediction with an end-to-end trainable abstract planning model that rolls forward multiple imagined steps before predicting value. TITLE: The Predictron: End-To-End Learning and Planning

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
One of the key challenges of artificial intelligence is to learn models that are effective in the context of planning. In this document we introduce the predictron architecture. The predictron consists of a fully abstract model, represented by a Markov reward process, that can be rolled forward multiple "imagined" planning steps. Each forward pass of the predictron accumulates internal rewards and values over multiple planning depths. The predictron is trained end-to-end so as to make t

title: The reorganization of corticomuscular coherence during a transition between sensorimotor states
searchable_text:
The reorganization of corticomuscular coherence during a transition between sensorimotor states Human motor control during transitions between sensorimotor states, specifically rapid changes in force output between two targets. Measure corticomuscular coherence across beta, alpha, and gamma bands during rapid force transitions, using phase spectra and spatial topology to infer afferent versus efferent corticospinal interactions. Shifted focus from beta-band coherence during sustained contractions to frequency-specific reorganization during transitions, revealing transient alpha and gamma corticospinal processing streams. TITLE: The reorganization of corticomuscular coherence during a transition between sensorimotor states

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Recent research suggests that neural oscillations in different frequency bands support distinct and sometimes parallel processing streams in neural circuits. Studies of the neural dynamics of human motor control have primarily focused on oscillations in the beta band (15-30 Hz). During sustained muscle contractions, corticomuscular coherence is mainly present in the beta band, while coherence in the alpha (8-12 Hz) and gamma (30-80 Hz) bands has not

title: The RooFit Toolkit for Data Modeling
searchable_text:
The RooFit Toolkit for Data Modeling Data modeling, fitting, plotting, and toy Monte Carlo generation for complex statistical models in the ROOT environment. Represent variables, probability density functions, functions, and integrals as C++ objects, composing complex fit models with classes that mimic mathematical operators inside ROOT. Compared with generic ROOT usage, RooFit adds an extensible object framework for mathematical modeling plus concise interfaces for likelihood, chi-squared fitting, plotting, and toy generation. TITLE: The RooFit Toolkit for Data Modeling

YEAR: 2003

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
RooFit is a library of C++ classes that facilitate data modeling in the ROOT environment. Mathematical concepts such as variables, (probability density) functions and integrals are represented as C++ objects. The package provides a flexible framework for building complex fit models through classes that mimic math operators, and is straightforward to extend. For all constructed models RooFit provides a concise yet powerful interface for fitting (binned and unbinned likelihood, chi^2), plotting and t

title: The RooStats Project
searchable_text:
The RooStats Project Not specified beyond the recovered corpus/domain label and the RooStats project title. A project identified as RooStats; the source excerpt does not describe its technical approach. Unknown; no predecessor or comparison is described in the excerpt. TITLE: The RooStats Project

YEAR: 2010

title: The Sinkhorn-Knopp Algorithm: Convergence and Applications
searchable_text:
The Sinkhorn-Knopp Algorithm: Convergence and Applications Balancing square nonnegative matrices and computing two-sided node measures in graph and traffic-flow settings. Diagonal scaling via the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm to balance a square nonnegative matrix into a doubly stochastic form. Extends Sinkhorn-Knopp from matrix balancing toward a modified algorithm for scalable two-sided graph node measurement. TITLE: The Sinkhorn-Knopp Algorithm: Convergence and Applications

YEAR: 2008

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
As long as a square nonnegative matrix $A$ contains sufficient nonzero 
elements, the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm can be used to balance the matrix, 
that is, to find a diagonal scaling of $A$ that is doubly stochastic. 
 
We relate balancing to problems in traffic flow and describe how balancing 
algorithms can be used to give a two sided measure of nodes in a graph. We 
show that with an appropriate modification, the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm is a 
natural candidate for computin

title: The skewed weak lensing likelihood: why biases arise, despite data and theory being sound
searchable_text:
The skewed weak lensing likelihood: why biases arise, despite data and theory being sound Bias in weak-lensing likelihood analyses caused by skewed distributions of noisy two-point correlation data. Derives a skewed weak-lensing likelihood using a simple hierarchical forward model for noisy two-point functions. Replaces the standard Gaussian likelihood with a skewed likelihood that passes four objective cosmology-independent tests and accounts for asymmetric errors. TITLE: The skewed weak lensing likelihood: why biases arise, despite data and theory being sound

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We derive the essentials of the skewed weak lensing likelihood via a simple hierarchical forward model. Our likelihood passes four objective and cosmology-independent tests which a standard Gaussian likelihood fails. We demonstrate that sound weak lensing data are naturally biased low, since they are drawn from a skewed distribution. This occurs already in the framework of Lambda cold dark matter. Mathematically, the biases arise because noisy tw

title: The Sources of Kolmogorov’s Grundbegriffe
searchable_text:
The Sources of Kolmogorov’s Grundbegriffe Origins of Kolmogorov’s modern probability formalism and its philosophy connecting probability to experience. Historical source analysis examining earlier scholars whose ideas Kolmogorov synthesized in the Grundbegriffe. Shifted attention from Kolmogorov’s finished axiomatization to the earlier intellectual sources synthesized in it. TITLE: The Sources of Kolmogorov’s Grundbegriffe

YEAR: 2006

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Andrei Kolmogorov's Grundbegriffe der Wahrscheinlichkeits-rechnung put probability's modern mathematical formalism in place. It also provided a philosophy of probability--an explanation of how the formalism can be connected to the world of experience. In this article, we examine the sources of these two aspects of the Grundbegriffe--the work of the earlier scholars whose ideas Kolmogorov synthesized.

title: The Uncertainty Bellman Equation and Exploration
searchable_text:
The Uncertainty Bellman Equation and Exploration Exploration/exploitation in reinforcement learning, especially deep Q-learning in large systems with complex generalization. Use an uncertainty Bellman equation that propagates uncertainty across time-steps and computes a fixed-point upper bound on posterior Q-value variance for exploration. Extends exploration bonuses from individual time-step uncertainty to propagated variance via a Bellman-style uncertainty equation, unlike count-based standard-deviation compounding or existing optimism methods. TITLE: The Uncertainty Bellman Equation and Exploration

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider the exploration/exploitation problem in reinforcement learning. For exploitation, it is well known that the Bellman equation connects the value at any time-step to the expected value at subsequent time-steps. In this paper we consider a similar \textit{uncertainty} Bellman equation (UBE), which connects the uncertainty at any time-step to the expected uncertainties at subsequent time-steps, thereby extending the potential exploratory benefit of a policy beyond individual tim

title: The WebNLG Challenge: Generating Text from RDF Data
searchable_text:
The WebNLG Challenge: Generating Text from RDF Data Generating natural language text from sets of RDF triples in the WebNLG challenge. A shared benchmark and evaluation setup for training, evaluating, and comparing microplanning systems that verbalise RDF triple sets into text. Introduces the WebNLG microplanning task, data preparation, evaluation methodology, participant-result analysis, and system descriptions as a common benchmark. TITLE: The WebNLG Challenge: Generating Text from RDF Data

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The WebNLG challenge consists in mapping sets of RDF triples to text. It provides a common benchmark on which to train, evaluate and compare “microplanners”, i.e. generation systems that verbalise a given content by making a range of complex interacting choices including referring expression generation, aggregation, lexicalisation, surface realisation and sentence segmentation. In this paper, we introduce the microplanning task, describe data preparation, introduce our evaluation met

title: Theano: A Python framework for fast computation of mathematical expressions
searchable_text:
Theano: A Python framework for fast computation of mathematical expressions Efficient computation of mathematical expressions involving multi-dimensional arrays, especially for machine learning models and frameworks. A Python library/compiler to define, optimize, and evaluate mathematical expressions over multi-dimensional arrays efficiently on CPU and GPU. Relative to earlier Theano releases, the paper highlights recently introduced functionalities, improvements, and steady performance gains since development began in 2008. TITLE: Theano: A Python framework for fast computation of mathematical expressions

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Theano is a Python library that allows to define, optimize, and evaluate mathematical expressions involving multi-dimensional arrays efficiently. Since its introduction, it has been one of the most used CPU and GPU mathematical compilers - especially in the machine learning community - and has shown steady performance improvements. Theano is being actively and continuously developed since 2008, multiple frameworks have been built on top of it and it has been u

title: ThiNet: A Filter Level Pruning Method for Deep Neural Network Compression
searchable_text:
ThiNet: A Filter Level Pruning Method for Deep Neural Network Compression Acceleration and compression of convolutional neural network models during both training and inference. Formulate CNN filter-level pruning as an optimization problem that discards whole less-important filters using statistics computed from the next layer while preserving the original network structure. Unlike existing pruning methods based on the current layer, ThiNet prunes filters using statistics information from the next layer. TITLE: ThiNet: A Filter Level Pruning Method for Deep Neural Network Compression

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose an efficient and unified framework, namely ThiNet, to simultaneously accelerate and compress CNN models in both training and inference stages. We focus on the filter level pruning, i.e., the whole filter would be discarded if it is less important. Our method does not change the original network structure, thus it can be perfectly supported by any off-the-shelf deep learning libraries. We formally establish filter pruning as an optimization problem, an

title: Three Statistically Validated K2 Transiting Warm Jupiter Exoplanets Confirmed as Low-mass Stars
searchable_text:
Three Statistically Validated K2 Transiting Warm Jupiter Exoplanets Confirmed as Low-mass Stars K2 transiting warm-Jupiter candidates whose radii resemble gas giants but may instead be low-mass stellar companions. Use radial-velocity monitoring of statistically validated K2 transiting systems to measure companion masses and reclassify planet candidates as stellar binaries. Moves beyond statistical validation by adding medium-precision radial-velocity mass measurements to separate gas giant planets, brown dwarfs, and small stars. TITLE: Three Statistically Validated K2 Transiting Warm Jupiter Exoplanets Confirmed as Low-mass Stars

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We have identified three K2 transiting star–planet systems, K2-51 (EPIC 202900527), K2-67 (EPIC 206155547), and K2-76 (EPIC 206432863), as stellar binaries with low-mass stellar secondaries. The three systems were statistically validated as transiting planets, and through measuring their orbits by radial velocity (RV) monitoring we have derived the companion masses to be (EPIC 202900527 B), (EPIC 206155547 B), and 0.0942 ± 0.0019 (EPIC 2064

title: TL;DR: Mining Reddit to Learn Automatic Summarization
searchable_text:
TL;DR: Mining Reddit to Learn Automatic Summarization Automatic text summarization training data, especially abstractive summarization corpora outside the news genre. Mine social media posts for author-provided TL;DR summaries, pairing long posts with appended TL;DR text to create summarization training data. Instead of relying primarily on news corpora, the work recovers summarization data from Reddit TL;DR conventions. TITLE: TL;DR: Mining Reddit to Learn Automatic Summarization

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Recent advances in automatic text summarization have used deep neural networks to generate high-quality abstractive summaries, but the performance of these models strongly depends on large amounts of suitable training data. We propose a new method for mining social media for author-provided summaries, taking advantage of the common practice of appending a “TL;DR” to long posts. A case study using a large Reddit crawl yields the Webis-TLDR-17 dataset, complementing existing corpora

title: TMVA - Toolkit for Multivariate Data Analysis
searchable_text:
TMVA - Toolkit for Multivariate Data Analysis High-energy physics analyses that need to extract small signals from large datasets using multivariate classification. ROOT-integrated toolkit hosting many multivariate classifiers and managing their simultaneous training, testing, performance evaluation, and application to data. TMVA unified many classifier families inside ROOT with a user-friendly interface for simultaneous training, testing, evaluation, and deployment. TITLE: TMVA - Toolkit for Multivariate Data Analysis

YEAR: 2007

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
n high-energy physics, with the search for ever smaller signals in ever larger data sets, it has become essential to extract a maximum of the available information from the data. Multivariate classification methods based on machine learning techniques have become a fundamental ingredient to most analyses. Also the multivariate classifiers themselves have significantly evolved in recent years. Statisticians have found new ways to tune and to combine classifiers to further gain in performanc

title: To prune, or not to prune: exploring the efficacy of pruning for model compression
searchable_text:
To prune, or not to prune: exploring the efficacy of pruning for model compression Model compression for energy-efficient inference in resource-constrained environments across CNN, stacked LSTM, and seq2seq LSTM neural networks. Gradually prune connection matrices during training to induce sparsity, yielding large sparse networks with fewer nonzero parameters while preserving architecture capacity. Instead of only reporting pruning gains, the paper tests pruning against dense width reduction and introduces a gradual pruning technique integrated into training. TITLE: To prune, or not to prune: exploring the efficacy of pruning for model compression

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Model pruning seeks to induce sparsity in a deep neural network's various connection matrices, thereby reducing the number of nonzero-valued parameters in the model. Recent reports (Han et al., 2015; Narang et al., 2017) prune deep networks at the cost of only a marginal loss in accuracy and achieve a sizable reduction in model size. This hints at the possibility that the baseline models in these experiments are perhaps severely over-parameterized at th

title: Topology and Geometry of Half-Rectified Network Optimization
searchable_text:
Topology and Geometry of Half-Rectified Network Optimization Loss-surface topology and geometry for deep neural network optimization, especially absence of bad local minima and level-set behavior in half-rectified networks. Proves asymptotic connectedness of half-rectified single-layer network loss landscapes via conditions on data distribution, architecture, smoothness, and over-parameterization; estimates level-set regularity to study gradient-descent conditioning. Moves beyond spin-glass and mean-field simplifications by analyzing nonlinear half-rectified networks without those assumptions and deriving explicit bounds linking data smoothness with over-parameterization. TITLE: Topology and Geometry of Half-Rectified Network Optimization

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The loss surface of deep neural networks has recently attracted interest in the optimization and machine learning communities as a prime example of high-dimensional non-convex problem. Some insights were recently gained using spin glass models and mean-field approximations, but at the expense of strongly simplifying the nonlinear nature of the model. 
In this work, we do not make any such assumption and study conditions on the data distribution and model architecture that pr

title: Toward Controlled Generation of Text
searchable_text:
Toward Controlled Generation of Text Generating plausible natural language sentences whose attributes can be dynamically controlled through disentangled latent representations with designated semantics. Combines variational auto-encoders with holistic attribute discriminators, differentiable approximations to discrete text samples, and explicit independent attribute-control constraints. Adds holistic attribute discriminators and explicit semantic-attribute control constraints to variational auto-encoder text generation for disentangled, controllable sentence attributes. TITLE: Toward Controlled Generation of Text

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Generic generation and manipulation of text is challenging and has limited success compared to recent deep generative modeling in visual domain. This paper aims at generating plausible natural language sentences, whose attributes are dynamically controlled by learning disentangled latent representations with designated semantics. We propose a new neural generative model which combines variational auto-encoders and holistic attribute discriminators for effective imposition of semantic structures. Wi

title: Towards AI-Complete Question Answering: A Set of Prerequisite Toy Tasks
searchable_text:
Towards AI-Complete Question Answering: A Set of Prerequisite Toy Tasks Reading-comprehension question answering for reasoning and natural-language dialogue agents, including chaining facts, induction, and deduction. A suite of prerequisite proxy question-answering tasks plus an extended Memory Networks model to evaluate reasoning skills in reading comprehension. Introduces classified prerequisite QA toy tasks and extends/improves the recently introduced Memory Networks model. TITLE: Towards AI-Complete Question Answering: A Set of Prerequisite Toy Tasks

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
One long-term goal of machine learning research is to produce methods that are applicable to reasoning and natural language, in particular building an intelligent dialogue agent. To measure progress towards that goal, we argue for the usefulness of a set of proxy tasks that evaluate reading comprehension via question answering. Our tasks measure understanding in several ways: whether a system is able to answer questions via chaining facts, simple induction, deduct

title: Towards an understanding of the correlations in jet substructure
searchable_text:
Towards an understanding of the correlations in jet substructure Jet substructure discrimination for quark/gluon jets and boosted heavy-object tagging, including W bosons and top quarks. Original particle-level studies of relationships, complementarity, and dependence among jet substructure observables across jet properties. Shifted emphasis from proposing many jet substructure observables to systematically studying their correlations, complementarity, and property dependence. TITLE: Towards an understanding of the correlations in jet substructure

YEAR: 2015

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Over the past decade, a large number of jet substructure observables have been proposed in the literature, and explored at the LHC experiments. Such observables attempt to utilize the internal structure of jets in order to distinguish those initiated by quarks, gluons, or by boosted heavy objects, such as top quarks and W bosons. This report, originating from and motivated by the BOOST2013 workshop, presents original particle-level studies that aim to improve our underst

title: Towards Conceptual Compression
searchable_text:
Towards Conceptual Compression Unsupervised image modeling and conceptual compression on ImageNet and Omniglot. A simple recurrent variational auto-encoder architecture that models images and can store only global conceptual information. Introduces recurrence into a variational auto-encoder for improved image modeling and conceptual compression. TITLE: Towards Conceptual Compression

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce a simple recurrent variational auto-encoder architecture that significantly improves image modeling. The system represents the state-of-the-art in latent variable models for both the ImageNet and Omniglot datasets. We show that it naturally separates global conceptual information from lower level details, thus addressing one of the fundamentally desired properties of unsupervised learning. Furthermore, the possibility of restricting ourselves to storing only global information about an image

title: Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks
searchable_text:
Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks Deep neural network vulnerability to adversarial examples: inputs nearly indistinguishable from natural data but classified incorrectly. Frame adversarial robustness as robust optimization to derive reliable training and attack methods, including security against first-order adversaries. Moves from ad hoc adversarial defenses toward a principled robust-optimization framework specifying a concrete security guarantee against broad adversary classes. TITLE: Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Recent work has demonstrated that deep neural networks are vulnerable to adversarial examples---inputs that are almost indistinguishable from natural data and yet classified incorrectly by the network. In fact, some of the latest findings suggest that the existence of adversarial attacks may be an inherent weakness of deep learning models. To address this problem, we study the adversarial robustness of neural networks through the lens of robust optimization. This approach p

title: Training Deep Nets with Sublinear Memory Cost
searchable_text:
Training Deep Nets with Sublinear Memory Cost Training very deep neural networks and long-sequence recurrent networks under GPU memory constraints. Use computation graph analysis, in-place operations, memory sharing, and recomputation to trade extra forward computation for sublinear training memory. Changed deep-net training from storing many intermediate feature maps and gradients to systematic sublinear-memory training via graph-level memory optimization and recomputation. TITLE: Training Deep Nets with Sublinear Memory Cost

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We propose a systematic approach to reduce the memory consumption of deep neural network training. Specifically, we design an algorithm that costs O( √ n) memory to train a n layer network, with only the computational cost of an extra forward pass per mini-batch. As many of the state-of-the-art models hit the upper bound of the GPU memory, our algorithm allows deeper and more complex models to be explored, and helps advance the innovations in deep learning research. We focus on reducing th

title: Trajectory Optimization Through Contacts and Automatic Gait Discovery for Quadrupeds
searchable_text:
Trajectory Optimization Through Contacts and Automatic Gait Discovery for Quadrupeds Automatic gait discovery and dynamic whole-body motion planning for quadruped robots across varied contact-rich locomotion tasks. Trajectory optimization for whole-body motion planning through contacts, optimizing full robot dynamics while letting contact switches, timings, points, and gait patterns emerge from the optimization. Unlike prior methods, contact switches, timings, points, and gait patterns are not prespecified; they are optimized as outcomes alongside the entire robot dynamics. TITLE: Trajectory Optimization Through Contacts and Automatic Gait Discovery for Quadrupeds

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
In this letter, we present a trajectory optimization framework for whole-body motion planning through contacts. We demonstrate how the proposed approach can be applied to automatically discover different gaits and dynamic motions on a quadruped robot. In contrast to most previous methods, we do not prespecify contact-switches, -timings, -points or gait patterns, but they are a direct outcome of the optimization. Furthermore, we optimize over the entir

title: Transfer Learning for Low-Resource Neural Machine Translation
searchable_text:
Transfer Learning for Low-Resource Neural Machine Translation Neural machine translation for low-resource language pairs. Train a high-resource parent NMT model, then transfer selected learned parameters to initialize and constrain training of a low-resource child model. Instead of training only the low-resource NMT model, initialization and training constraints are inherited from a high-resource parent language-pair model. TITLE: Transfer Learning for Low-Resource Neural Machine Translation

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
The encoder-decoder framework for neural machine translation (NMT) has been shown effective in large data scenarios, but is much less effective for low-resource languages. We present a transfer learning method that significantly improves Bleu scores across a range of low-resource languages. Our key idea is to first train a high-resource language pair (the parent model), then transfer some of the learned parameters to the low-resource pair (the child model) to initialize and

title: Transferability in Machine Learning: from Phenomena to Black-Box Attacks using Adversarial Samples
searchable_text:
Transferability in Machine Learning: from Phenomena to Black-Box Attacks using Adversarial Samples Black-box adversarial attacks on machine learning classification systems, including cross-architecture and cross-training-set transfer between substitute and victim models. Train a substitute model via victim-oracle labels on synthetic data, improve substitute training with reservoir sampling, then craft adversarial examples on the substitute and transfer them to the victim. Adds reservoir sampling to recent oracle-labeled substitute training and introduces transferability attacks for previously unexplored substitute-victim model pairs, especially SVMs and decision trees. TITLE: Transferability in Machine Learning: from Phenomena to Black-Box Attacks using Adversarial Samples

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Many machine learning models are vulnerable to adversarial examples: inputs that are specially crafted to cause a machine learning model to produce an incorrect output. Adversarial examples that affect one model often affect another model, even if the two models have different architectures or were trained on different training sets, so long as both models were trained to perform the same task. An attacker may therefore train their own s

title: Transformation of quantum states using uniformly controlled rotations
searchable_text:
Transformation of quantum states using uniformly controlled rotations Unitary transformation mapping any given n-qubit quantum register state into another, for quantum computer initialization and quantum algorithms. Use uniformly controlled rotations with analytically specified rotation angles to implement the desired n-qubit state transformation. Reduced the CNOT and one-qubit rotation counts versus prior results, while adding an analytic expression for required rotation angles. TITLE: Transformation of quantum states using uniformly controlled rotations

YEAR: 2004

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We consider a unitary transformation which maps any given state of an n-qubit quantum register into another one. This transformation has applications in the initialization of a quantum computer, and also in some quantum algorithms. Employing uniformly controlled rotations, we present a quantum circuit of 2n+2 - 4n - 4 CNOT gates and 2n+2 - 5 one-qubit elementary rotations that effects the state transformation. The complexity of the circuit is noticeably lower than t

title: Transient Classification in LIGO data using Difference Boosting Neural Network
searchable_text:
Transient Classification in LIGO data using Difference Boosting Neural Network Detection and classification of short-duration transients in gravitational-wave detector data, including astrophysical events and non-astrophysical noise sources. Hybrid supervised/unsupervised classifier trained on relative wavelet energy and entropy from one-dimensional wavelet decomposition of detector data. Introduces a hybrid supervised/unsupervised transient-classification method using wavelet-derived relative energy and entropy features; no closest-predecessor comparison is stated. TITLE: Transient Classification in LIGO data using Difference Boosting Neural Network

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Detection and classification of transients in data from gravitational wave detectors are crucial for efficient searches for true astrophysical events and identification of noise sources. We present a hybrid method for classification of short duration transients seen in gravitational wave data using both supervised and unsupervised machine learning techniques. To train the classifiers we use the relative wavelet energy and the corresponding entropy obtained

title: Transient Localized Wave Patterns and Their Application to Migraine
searchable_text:
Transient Localized Wave Patterns and Their Application to Migraine Migraine pathophysiology, specifically whether cortical spreading depression waves underlie headache in both migraine with aura and migraine without aura. Analyze transient cortical wave patterns in a canonical reaction-diffusion model with mean field inhibition, driven by ghost behavior near a saddle-node bifurcation. Extends cortical spreading depression from migraine with aura to migraine without aura, reframing silent aura as transient wave patterns below aura-reporting conditions. TITLE: Transient Localized Wave Patterns and Their Application to Migraine

YEAR: 2012

DOMAIN: ['corpus']

ABSTRACT:
Transient dynamics is pervasive in the human brain and poses challenging problems both in mathematical tractability and clinical observability. We investigate statistical properties of transient cortical wave patterns with characteristic forms (shape, size, duration) in a canonical reaction-diffusion model with mean field inhibition. The patterns are formed by ghost behavior near a saddle-node bifurcation in which a stable traveling wave (node) collides with its critical nuclea

title: Transit shapes and self-organizing maps as a tool for ranking planetary candidates: application to Kepler and K2
searchable_text:
Transit shapes and self-organizing maps as a tool for ranking planetary candidates: application to Kepler and K2 Ranking planetary candidates for follow-up in Kepler and K2 planet-hunting surveys, separating true planet signals from astrophysical false positives. Use Self Organising Maps to learn transit-shape structure of Kepler/K2 signals and classify or rank candidates as planets versus astrophysical false positives. Replaces human ‘eyeballing’ candidate selection with a fast SOM-based transit-shape classifier usable before candidate dispositions are available. TITLE: Transit shapes and self-organizing maps as a tool for ranking planetary candidates: application to Kepler and K2

YEAR: 2016

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
A crucial step in planet hunting surveys is to select the best candidates for follow up observations, given limited telescope resources. This is often performed by human ‘eyeballing’, a time consuming and statistically awkward process. Here we present a new, fast machine learning technique to separate true planet signals from astrophysical 
false positives. We use Self Organising Maps (SOMs) to study the transit shapes of K

title: TRANSITS AND OCCULTATIONS OF AN EARTH-SIZED PLANET IN AN 8.5 hr ORBIT
searchable_text:
TRANSITS AND OCCULTATIONS OF AN EARTH-SIZED PLANET IN AN 8.5 hr ORBIT Detection and characterization of Earth-sized planets in ultra-short-period orbits around relatively bright Kepler stars. Search Kepler short-period photometry, then validate the candidate as a transiting planet by excluding eclipsing-binary signatures via ellipsoidal-light and radial-velocity non-detections. The unusually short 8.5-hour orbit and bright host enabled robust detections of phase illumination and occultations for an Earth-sized Kepler planet. TITLE: TRANSITS AND OCCULTATIONS OF AN EARTH-SIZED PLANET IN AN 8.5 hr ORBIT

YEAR: 2013

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We report the discovery of an Earth-sized planet (1.16 ± 0.19 R⊕) in an 8.5 hr orbit around a late G-type star (KIC 8435766, Kepler-xx). The object was identified in a search for short-period planets in the Kepler database and confirmed to be a transiting planet (as opposed to an eclipsing stellar system) through the absence of ellipsoidal light variations or substantial radial-velocity variations. The unusually short orbital period and the relative brightness of th

title: Translation-based Recommendation
searchable_text:
Translation-based Recommendation Personalized sequential behavior prediction, or next-item recommendation, involving third-order interactions among a user, previously visited items, and the next item. Embed items into a transition space, modeling each user as a translation vector operating over item sequences to capture user-conditioned transitions. Replaces separate pairwise user-item and item-item components with a unified translation-based model of third-order user–previous item–next item relationships. TITLE: Translation-based Recommendation

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
Modeling the complex interactions between users and items as well as amongst items themselves is at the core of designing successful recommender systems. One classical setting is predicting users' personalized sequential behavior (or 'next-item' recommendation), where the challenges mainly lie in modeling 'third-order' interactions between a user, her previously visited item(s), and the next item to consume. Existing methods typically decompose these higher-order interactions into a combination of pair

title: Transport distances and geodesic convexity for systems of degenerate diffusion equations
searchable_text:
Transport distances and geodesic convexity for systems of degenerate diffusion equations Systems of nonlinear parabolic evolution equations and degenerate drift-diffusion systems for vector-valued densities on R. Wasserstein-like dynamical transport distances for vector-valued densities, replacing scalar mobility with a positive, concave mobility matrix within a gradient-flow variational framework. The scalar-theory mobility function is replaced by a positivity- and concavity-constrained mobility matrix for vector-valued densities. TITLE: Transport distances and geodesic convexity for systems of degenerate diffusion equations

YEAR: 2014

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
We introduce Wasserstein-like dynamical transport distances between vector-valued densities on $${\mathbb {R}}$$R. The mobility function from the scalar theory is replaced by a mobility matrix, that is subject to positivity and concavity conditions. Our primary motivation is to cast certain systems of nonlinear parabolic evolution equations in the variational framework of gradient flows.In the first part of the paper, we investigate the structural

title: TriviaQA: A Large Scale Distantly Supervised Challenge Dataset for Reading Comprehension
searchable_text:
TriviaQA: A Large Scale Distantly Supervised Challenge Dataset for Reading Comprehension Challenging reading comprehension over trivia question-answer pairs requiring answers from supporting evidence documents. A large-scale distantly supervised reading-comprehension dataset pairing trivia questions and answers with independently gathered evidence documents. Compared with other large-scale datasets, TriviaQA adds more complex questions, greater question-evidence variability, and more cross-sentence reasoning demands. TITLE: TriviaQA: A Large Scale Distantly Supervised Challenge Dataset for Reading Comprehension

YEAR: 2017

DOMAIN: ['corpus_recovered']

ABSTRACT:
